venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: JM Bairstow
commentary: loud, loud appeal for lbw, the appeal is not fading at all and the finger finally goes up for lbw. Bairstow gives it a though and reviews. Bairstow went for a sweep for the ball that pitched just outside eoff, it came in with the angle and ball-tracking shows it's hitting middle. Kings lose their review and Bairstow falls for another low score

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: LS Livingstone
commentary: Caressed to cover, Kane takes an excellent catch with a leap and a tumble forward! It was short and wide, looked slow too. Liam, who had been starved of strike, tried to cut it aerially without too much power. He toe-ended it aerially and Kane spotted it early and used his acrobatic skills effectively with forward movements, leap and the tumble.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: JM Sharma
commentary: top edge goes high and Malik takes a return catch by extending his follow through! Rushes Jitesh with his short and fast ball, he pulls but mistimes it and the ball lobs up straight before Umran Malik runs forward and takes it comfortably

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: M Shahrukh Khan
commentary: Short ball outside off is skied to cover! It was wide and short, looked a cross-seamer. Shahrukh tried the aerial cut but got a top-edge that went up for a while but Kane settled in and took it safely with his fingers pointing up

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: OF Smith
commentary: Back of a good length ball is skied to the bowler! It looked a slower one, Smith backed away and tried to whack the pull out of the city but it took the top-edge and went to the leg-side.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: K Rabada

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: RD Chahar
commentary: Off-stump flies behind after being clattered by the full ball! Chahar went across before the ball was bowler and came back far enough to make room and tried to slap it to the off-side but was castled. Umran enjoyed that.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: VG Arora
commentary: Was that a replay? No, it's another rocket that knocks the top of off! This was slightly shorter, the room-making was on again. Vaibhav tried to carve it but missed and the ball smashed the top of the stump to take Umran on the verge of a hat-trick and a wicket-maiden.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: Arshdeep Singh
commentary: Tries the full ball on the stumps, again. It's hit to cover. The ball went straight to the fielder who was in. The batter took-off but the throw came in to the keeper who whipped off the bails, the batter was stuck in the middle.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: Abhishek Sharma
commentary: Holes out at long-on! It was full and floated up on middle and off at 85 kmph, Abhishek came down the ground and tried to go over long-on with an extended aerial loft. He got to the pitch of it but the connection was good enough only to hand a comfortable catch in the deep around his bread basket.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: AK Markram

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: N Pooran
RV Uthappa
commentary: fuller and seaming in this time. Uthappa once again looks to smash it through midwicket. Once again he fails to connect. <b>An appeal for lbw but not given. Titans want to review this. No inside edge and the ball-tracking shows it would have gone on to hit the top of middle stump

name: MM Ali
commentary: chopped on! Not for the first time this season. Was a length ball pushed across. Moeen looks to drive through the off side, playing away from the body, but gets an inside edge onto the stumps

name: S Dube
commentary: a full toss around off, Jadeja smashes it down the ground but Miller moves quickly to his left from long-off and fires in the throw. Ferguson collects it and breaks the stumps. Dube was short

name: Shubman Gill
commentary: golden duck! A dream start for CSK. Choudhary bowled a length ball and offered width too. Gill walked down the ground and cut it well, but it's been hit straight to Uthappa at point, who takes it comfortably from near his head.

name: V Shankar
commentary: another cut, another wicket. Shankar gone for a duck too as Theekshana bowls a length ball quite wide outside off. The ball doesn't turn in either direction, skids straight through, and Shankar tries to play square. Takes the edge, and MSD does the rest.

name: A Manohar
commentary: Titans in tatters! Full ball. Manohar came down the ground and that has caused him his arms to lock. Gets too close to the pitch of the ball by skipping out, and therefore the attempted drive cannot generate the elevation to take it over the infield. Pocketed at cover!

name: R Tewatia
commentary: slower ball. Length ball just outside off. Tewatia comes down and tries to smack it over mid off. But the shot's been toe-ended, the ball's gone high up, and the captain settles under it at mid off

name: Rashid Khan
commentary: put the dancing shoes on, DJ! Bravo bowls a length ball, takes pace off, fourth-stump line. Rashid comes down the track and tries to hammer it, but he slices the shot. Ball goes high up, Moeen runs across to cover from mid off, and takes a running catch. Out for a 21-ball 40

name: JC Buttler
commentary: superb bouncer. Right at his badge, over the left shoulder, and Buttler gets a top edge on the pull. It carries all the way to the fine leg fence where Chakravarhty doesn't have to move. Buttler walks off with his bat and helmet held high

name: SV Samson
commentary: big leg-side boundary here. Russell goes slower ball, on a length, middle and leg, and Samson ends up skying it to deep midwicket. Samson does his job, going at 200 strike rate

name: R Parag
commentary: Parag goes straight over his head, slightly towards long-off. The connection is not the cleanest. Cummins runs to his right from long-off, gets into a sort of a bicycle jump, catches it overhead, and while he is stumbling, he lobs it towards Mavi, who has run to his left from long-on. Mavi completes the catch one-handed with a jump of his own. The scoreboard, though, will say c Mavi b Narine

name: KK Nair
commentary: full and straight, no power in this slog. This is seam up and in the slot. Nair just doesn't make a good connection. Caught at long-on. This is proving to be a good over for KKR

name: AJ Finch
commentary: The extra balls work out for the Royals as Finch upper cuts a short, wide ball but it finds the bottom of the bat and skies to deep backward point running diagonally to his left. Nair had enough time to settle under it and take it comfortably

name: SP Narine
commentary: Run-out comes from attempting a suicidal run! It was a full ball that was pushed to cover, they took off after Finch called. Narine responded but the run was not on. Hetmyer ran in from cover-point to his right, picked it up and threw it direct at the striker's end to hand a Diamond duck to Narine.

name: SS Iyer
commentary: Given LBW, Iyer reviews! It was a full ball that beat the whip towards deep mid-wicket. Iyer is walking as the Hawk-Eye's prediction comes in, it shows the ball was pitching in line and was hitting leg. Chahal knew it was out right away and roared as soon as it was given out.

name: N Rana
commentary: Full on leg-stump, it is slog-swept but in the air straight to Buttler at long-off! Rana tried to make room and go all in with the bat swing but could not get enough on it. It took the top edge and stayed up for a while in the air, allowing Buttler to settle under and take the catch comfortably

name: AD Russell
commentary: The carrom ball knocks over Russell's defence! It was a good length ball that pitched on middle and turned away ever so slightly, past the full-faced bat to knock off-stump. Ashwin is off on a celebratory run! It was bowled from wide of the crease, which Ian Bishop, observes accentuated the angle

name: VR Iyer
commentary: Googly beats the full-blooded swing down the ground and has Venkatesh stumped! It was a full ball that turned away from off and the batter had danced down the track but missed the ball. The keeper completed the dismissal and Chahal is chuffed!

name: PJ Cummins
commentary: Caught behind! Takes the outside edge of the cover-drive does a full ball spinning away from outside off. Cummins went across and pushed at it. It leads to Chahal celebrating with a leap and a slide to take him to the jolly pose at the boundary with the elbow on the ground. The hat-trick has RR firmly on top!

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: Anuj Rawat
commentary: taken at mid-off. Or is it? Rawat once again looks to hit down the ground but ends up mistiming this fuller delivery. It goes towards mid-off, where Rahul dives forward and across to catch. They are checking upstairs and the third umpire is happy with it, the fingers were under the ball

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: F du Plessis
commentary: Stoinis holds on this time at deep backward square! Results in heartbreak for du Plessis as he ends on his IPL best of 96 once again! Slower bouncer from Holder, Faf tries to smash a front foot pull over the leg side rope but he's out of energy after more than an hour at the crease and it swirls tamely to Stoinis 10 yards inside the rope, who takes a far less eventful chance than the previous one shelled in the over.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: V Kohli
commentary: two in two for Chameera. Kohli walks back for a first-ball duck. A wry smile on his face. Sadness on the faces of some of the fans in the stands. It was short of length outside off, Kohli played at it away from the body. But he couldn't keep it down and Hooda pouches it at backward point

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: Shahbaz Ahmed
commentary: back of a length on fifth stump, slapped into the covers straight to KL Rahul, Shahbaz sets off from the non-striker's end but Faf wasn't interested. Relay from Rahul goes to Holder on one bounce at the bowler's end as Shahbaz tries to scamper back. It goes to the third umpire to determine if his bat was over the line before the bails are dislodged. Looks like his bat is on the line, but not past the line and a fifty partnership is now toast.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: HV Patel

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: Q de Kock
commentary: taken at first slip. A length ball angling across de Kock, who pushes at it tentatively and gets an outside edge. Karthik puts in half a dive, leaving it for Maxwell eventually who grabs it with both hands to his right, just above waist height

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: KL Rahul
commentary: a slower one down the leg side. Looks to glance but misses. The umpire says it's not a wide. RCB have opted for a review. And the UltraEdge shows a murmur as the ball passes the bat. Rahul is surprised, he even gestures to the dugout if there was something on the UltraEdge. Kohli, meanwhile, lets out a roar

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: MK Pandey
commentary: a soft dismissal. Short of length at the body, Pandey goes for the pull but the ball hits him high on the bat. A simple catch for Harshal at forward square leg

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: KH Pandya
commentary: he looked uncomfortable the previous few balls and now holes out to deep midwicket. Slightly short on off stump and sticks in the wicket a touch as Krunal struggles keeping his hands back on a pull. He can't get any power behind it and picks out the man on the rope who takes this five yards in at chest height.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: DJ Hooda
commentary: superb effort at deep third man by Suyash! Short and wide, Hooda goes to uppercut this over the third man rope but gets under it too much. It swirls toward the man charging in from the rope who judges it well sliding on his knees to soften the catch 15 yards in from the boundary.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: A Badoni
commentary: Hazlewood sends down a knuckle slower ball full and very wide outside off, Badoni shuffles away across his stumps and tries to heave this ball from well outside the guidelines into the vacant leg side boundary but pays the price and feathers an edge through to DK.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: MP Stoinis
commentary: cover your children's ears! Stoinis shuffles way way outside off in the context of the previous ball that was not called a wide and is standing almost on the wide guideline trying to play a full ball into the leg side and inside edge's this onto the stumps. He lets out a massive "F*** you!" that can be heard through the stump mic clear as day all the way back to Australia before walking off.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: PVD Chameera

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: Ravi Bishnoi
MA Agarwal
commentary: Chops it on! It was a back of a good length that was going away with the angle just outside off, The body went to the left as the bat reached out as he tried to dab it to third man. It took the inside edge and went back to hit the stumps on the bounce

name: JM Bairstow
commentary: Pulled straight to deep-fine leg! It was a length ball on the hips, Bairstow went back in the crease and judged the length well. However, the placement of the pull was the conundrum, as the the Fizz, took it comfortably under the bread-basket

name: LS Livingstone
commentary: Runs down the ground and misses the straight loft, gets stumped! It was a good length ball over off, it straightened after pitching. The major issue was that Liam did not get to the pitch of the ball. The hands came through with the full bat-swing, the bottom hand came off, and so did the bails.

name: JM Sharma
commentary: full ball, and he looks for the sweep. Given out leg before, but he goes for the review immediately. He went across the stumps, and he was looking to get it fine. I think the question will simply be if he got a tickle on it. There's a slight spike, but that comes when the ball just grazes the ground. Goes on and hits the stumps, but with umpire's call on impact. He will have to go, but the review is retained

name: M Shahrukh Khan
commentary: edged through to Pant! That's probably the last surviving hope for Punjab to reach a fighting total. Length ball, angles across him, and he flays at it, but with little feet movement. Gets a decent edge through to the wicketkeeper, as Khaleel wheels off in celebration

name: K Rabada
commentary: fullish, and through his defence! Rabada has a hopeful poke at it, from the crease. Didn't really pick the length or the line properly. The ball goes through as he looks to drive, and it rattles somewhere between middle and leg. Kuldeep has deserved some reward for his spell today, and he gets it today

name: NT Ellis
commentary: Kuldeep strikes again! Just tosses this up, around off, and with the angle of his release, it slides on, and also turns just a touch. Ellis has a tentative attempt at a drive, but his bat also comes down at an angle, and leaves enough room between bat and pad for the ball to sneak in and rattle the stumps

name: RD Chahar
commentary: sweep, and straight to Rovman Powell at deep backward square leg! Chahar has just made too much of a connection with this ball. It wasn't tossed up a lot, but the line was teasing and tempting, angling in and on the stumps, at a fuller length. Probably there for the sweep, but he failed to keep it down

name: Arshdeep Singh
commentary: full, just outside off, and he has a swing and a miss. Wants a single, but won't be getting anything for this. Pant is quick to come over and whip the bails off. Unselfish, that is one way of looking at it, I guess

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: RG Sharma
commentary: that is catching practice for mid-on. Just a nothing little push to a length-ball inswinger, unable to keep it down even though he is not looking to hit a boundary off it. Easy catch for mid-on

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: Ishan Kishan
commentary: Kishan is on his knees. The off stump has gone for a walk. Swinging yorker. Okay not quite a yorker. A half-volley more like, but it ha swung late, and Kishan has played the wrong line. He is off his feet as he gets into a tangle, and the ball takes out the off stump. Both openers gone in the first over. Projected score 158 now

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: D Brevis
commentary: and Choudhary makes sure Jadeja's drop doesn't cost them much. He sees Brevis walking at him, Choudhary bowls the slower offcutter, in his half, Brevis looks for a sort of upper-cut, but the extra bounce does him in. Edged to Dhoni

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: SA Yadav
commentary: Santner has a wicket finally after the drop in his first over. And he has the big one. Surya has been given no room by the spinners, and they have had a strong leg-side field. So the boundary options for Surya are to go over the leg-side field. He did that against Theekshana, but gets a flat top edge on the same sweep against Santner. Taken at deep square leg

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: NT Tilak Varma

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: JD Unadkat

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: RV Uthappa
commentary: back of a length angled across fifth stump, Uthappa shuffles across trying to play a short arm jab paddle pull cheekily behind square but doesn't get it wide enough of Brevis at deep backward square leg and picks him out at chest height 10 yards in from the rope for a simple catch.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: MJ Santner
commentary: bounced out! Similar ball that Sams bowled to end the first over that Santner spanked over wide mid-on, but this time bowled tighter on a fourth or fifth stump line to cramp Santner slightly for room. Goes to pull this and top edges this to mid-on where Unadkat waits several seconds for the ball to come down before grasping onto it with elbows tucked into his torso and palms facing in.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: S Dube
commentary: perfect delivery, sensational catch! Sams bangs this back of a length in the fifth stump channel at full pace angling it in toward off, flat-footed poke by Dube clips the edge and flies high toward Kishan's left at chest height. He does a salmon leap sideways to snaffle it.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: RA Jadeja
commentary: slower ball lands full and this time within the wide guidelines at 113 kph forcing Jadeja to play at this but he's through the shot too early, shovels this high toward long-off, basic catch taken by Tilak running in and falling forward on his knees to claim it at waist height.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: MS Dhoni

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: D Pretorius
commentary: Pretorius shuffles way across his stumps and misses flicking across the line to a yorker. Looks stone dead live but umpire Oxenford gives it not out and they scamper a leg bye. Rohit calls for DRS immediately. Live, it looked like the ball pitched in line with leg stump. That was the only doubt. If it was, he's a gonner. Ball-tracking indeed shows the ball pitched in line. Oxenford has to overturn his decision to out! Pretorius was so far across his stumps, you could see leg stump showing live, so it was surprising that it was given not out on field.

name: JC Buttler
commentary: holes out to long on and an exhilarating innings comes to an end. A pat on the back from Samson as he walks back. Fizz went for the yorker. Buttler tried to hit it, and therefore ended up meeting it at a very low full toss height. Gets some connection, but doesn't have the distance. Warner at long on takes a simple one

name: SN Khan
commentary: Sarfaraz gifts his wicket to square leg! Fullish length ball on sixth stump, tries to drag this into the leg side with a sweep but picks out Prasidh in the ring, who shuffles to his right taking a catch at neck height just in front of the umpire.

name: RR Pant
commentary: Padkikkal shows Chahal how it's done! Length ball angled across off, Pant goes for a big heave over mid-on but a top-edge swirls over point. Padikkal calls off Samson who was running toward it and rightly so because Padikkal had a better angle and less ground to cover. He settles himself before reaching forward and takes a sharp steepler while going to the ground to cushion the ball as it comes back to earth.

name: AR Patel
commentary: flighted full on fourth stump, spins back in and defeats Axar's attempted slog sweep, middle and off stump are knocked back.

name: SN Thakur
commentary: length ball on fourth stump, slaps this to wide long-on, Shardul is hesitant turning back for the second but Lalit wanted it and turned hard for the non-striker's end. Riyan Parag charges hard and fires a bullet to Samson, who whips the bails off with Shardul a foot short. His path back to the striker's end didn't help matters. Ran a pronounced zig-zag, probably covered 24 yards instead of 22 trying to make the second.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: Shubman Gill
commentary: wicket first ball for Southee, nicked down the leg side! Not a great ball, but lovely seam position for that length ball on middle and leg it goes down further with the angle, Gill wants to tickle it fine but he's done it too fine and Billings grabs it with both hands

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: HH Pandya
commentary: Hardik holes out! How much did his groin issues have a role to play in that? Slower ball on a length, angling in. Pandya stands tall and pulls but doesn't connect well. Rinku Singh hares in and to his right and completes a tumbling catch. Massive wicket at this point for the Knight Riders.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: DA Miller
commentary: Now then! Mavi strikes and his offcutter works. Bowls it on a shorter length, Miller stands back to heave it over the on side. The bounce means that it takes the top edge and settles into the safe hands of Umesh, who takes some steps to his left from point.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: R Tewatia
commentary: Can't keep Rinku Singh away from the ball on the field today. Another slower ball on a short length, slanting away from Tewatia. This time he can't pull it and so tries to take on the bigger boundary. Finds sweeper to perfection instead.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: Rashid Khan
commentary: Two wickets in the over for Southee. Using the bounce in the wicket to good effect. Cross-seam ball on a shorter length, climbs as it reaches Rashid. Only spoons a catch to midwicket as he goes for the pull. Two-ball duck for Rashid and Titans are now withering away.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: A Manohar
commentary: Straightaway picks up a wicket! Knows that he is bowling with the longer boundary on his right and lands a slower one on a length. Abhinav gives it his all as he looks to go over deep midwicket but finds Rinku there. No distance only height.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: Yash Dayal
commentary: Is that a catch? Russell is celebrating for sure. What an over this has been from Dre Russ! Wow. Speared in a yorker that reached the batter at ankle eye. Dayal got it off the toe end and straight to Russell, who took a low diving catch. Four wickets in the over and the big West Indian has delivered for KKR!

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: SP Narine
commentary: taken at short fine leg! He expected the short ball, this wasn't a bouncer though, only short of length. He stays back to whip it off his hips, towards short fine leg, and Ferguson does well to go low to his right to get both hands under it

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: SS Iyer
commentary: edged behind, Dayal gets the KKR captain! Fourth wicket in seventh over, and all of them to fast bowlers. Dayal bowls a stifling line to Shreyas with the angle, makes him play outside off with his line around off, and he can only edge it behind to Saha. Dayal leaps with joy to celebrate the big wicket

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: N Rana
commentary: 149.1 kmh, a lone appeal for caught behind but turned down. Ferguson appealed all alone there, he has convinced Hardik and they take the review. Rana backed away a little, probably because of the pace, it was short of length around off, Rana poked inside the line while on the move towards leg, and UltraEdge shows there's a spike, the crowd comes alive as the decision is overturned!

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: VR Iyer
commentary: Brilliant catch from Abhinav Manohar at the deep midwicket fence. But has he touched the boundary cushion? Venkatesh went for the slog sweep against a wrong one that was full in length around middle and leg. Abhinav had his eyes on the ball but his momentum took him back and his back leg is the one in question. It is very close to tell. The cushion hasn't moved, so that is an indication. And that is all the TV ump needs, too. I am satisfied, says the TV umpire and Venky has to walk back. KKR continue to slide, as Rashid Khan picks up his 100th IPL wicket.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: AD Russell
commentary: Is this the decisive catch? Is it the wicket that seals it for the Titans? The decibel levels at the DY Patil stadium have soared. Short ball, tucked Russell up. Yet he continued with the pull and got more height than distance. Ferguson ran to his left and misjudged it by running in before going back to take that with a tumble. Massive. Everybody crowds him in celebration. Hardik's happy at the ropes.

name: F du Plessis
commentary: flattens the off-stump! The previous one nipped in, this one held its line. Faf is squared up by the length, he had to play at that alright, and he did so down the wrong line. What a ball, what a wicket.

name: Anuj Rawat
commentary: another edge, another catch in the slips. Markram dives to his right in front of first slip to catch that. Jansen gets this to move away, drawa Rawat forwardf with the length, he pushes at that without any conviction and gets the edge that is pouched safely.

name: V Kohli
commentary: snuffed him out in the slips! Golden duck for Kohli. A second in a row. Full and angling across, Kohli pushes with hard hands at the one that holds its line, and because he has jabbed at that, it flies at a comfortable height to second slip. Full marks to Williamson for bringing back the second slip for Kohli. He couldn't have asked for an easier offering.

name: Shahbaz Ahmed
commentary: appeal for caught behind. SRH think there was some glove. The umpire was walking across, seemingly to send it upstairs. He does go up to check if this was a clean catch, but there was no doubt as to whether Shahbaz gloved it or not. The catch is clean too and RCB slip into the abyss.

name: HV Patel
commentary: the off-stump goes on a cartwheel! Harshal plays down the wrong line as this full delivery hits the seam and jags away. Grave mistake

name: PWH de Silva
commentary: the stumps are flattened! Wanindu attempts a mow across the line but missed it completely.

name: Mohammed Siraj
commentary: straight up, so high that Williamson sizes up the distance and trajectory by taking a split second, then runs back two steps, stops and then again moves back to keep his balance as the ball descends ands completes an excellent catch moving to his left from cover. Siraj tried to give it the kitchen sink but had no timing.

name: Abhishek Sharma
commentary: Sharma falls trying to bring up his half-century and end the match all at once with a six! Length ball on fifth stump, shovels this high toward long-on. Easy catch by Rawat shuffling to his right about 10 yards in from his rope, catch taken at chest height.

name: Q de Kock
commentary: Varma's drop costs MI only six runs. de Kock is caught at short cover next ball. Low full-toss on off, de Kock drives weakly and chips it in the air. (Mis)hits it more or less straight to Rohit who snags a low catch in the infield

name: MK Pandey
commentary: Pandey charges at Pollard and top-edges it to short fine leg. He falls for a run-a-ball 22. Pandey advances at the bowler, Pollard bangs it shorter and outside off to mess with Pandey's pull. Spliced to Meredith

name: MP Stoinis
commentary: Stoinis holes out to Varma at deep midwicket. Rohit applauds it. Slower short ball on the stumps from Sams. Stoinis pulls this chest-high delivery straight to Varma in the outfield

name: KH Pandya
commentary: Pollard now nips out Krunal. Caught at deep midwicket. Cutter from Pollard, takes it away from Pandya. He fetches it from outside off and drags a pull to Shokeen. The Wankhede crowd roars. LSG continue to wobble around Rahul

name: DJ Hooda
commentary: Meredith gets away with that. He knows it and has a wry smile on his face. Short and into the pitch, finishes on the hips. Possibly a slower variation. Hooda flaps it weakly to short fine leg where Brevis pouches the catch

name: A Badoni
commentary: Picks put Pollard at long-on. On-pace full ball on the stumps, Meredith lands this in the swinging arc as well. But Badoni can't get enough elevation on this occasion

name: Ishan Kishan
commentary: An appeal for caught at slip, off a rebound of de Kock's shoe. The umpires have referred it upstairs. Was short and wide outside off, Kishan had to really reach out for it. He ends up bottom-edging it onto de Kock's left shoe and bounces off towards Holder at first slip. A simple catch for him but it almost slips out of his hands as he takes it in front of his face, but he recovers well to hold on to it. The question is if it bounced just before hitting de Kock's shoe. No, it didn't. A bizarre end to a bizarre innings

name: RG Sharma
commentary: Huge wicket! Fuller length around off, Rohit goes for the slog over deep midwicket but gets a top edge. Gowtham settles under it at short third man

name: D Brevis
commentary: short of length outside off, Brevis hops and goes for the upper cut but ends up edging it towards third man. Chameera takes a couple of steps in and takes it just inches above the ground. He was slow to start and for a moment, it looked like it might lands in front of him

name: SA Yadav
commentary: and he gets Suryakumar. Length ball around off, angled across. Surya looks to whip it on the leg side but gets a leading edge towards extra cover. Rahul dives forward to pouch it with both hands

name: NT Tilak Varma
commentary: hit well but hit straight to Bishnoi. A full toss outside off, Varma looks to go leg side once again but Bishnoi takes a smart catch at deep midwicket, moving to his left and getting on his knees to pouch it with reverse cups

name: JD Unadkat
commentary: Unadkat has been run out. A length ball on the stumps, Sams swings across but manages only to short fine leg. Unadkat is three quarters down the pitch but Sams hasn't even moved. He isn't interested in single. Unadkat tries to get back to his end but even with a wayward throw he fails and Kurnal takes the bails off

name: MA Agarwal
commentary: got him, carrom ball close to off stump. I think Agarwal picked it, but he didn't get the room he desired to be able to play that front-foot cut. Ends up hitting it in the air, straight to backward point

name: LS Livingstone
commentary: down the track, doesn't quite get to the pitch of this slower offcutter, and swings his body 360 degrees while looking to smash this over midwicket, out of the ground, and into the next postcode, but he doesn't middle it. Instead he gets a top edge and it flies high in the air and settles in the hands of the fielder at short third

name: JM Bairstow
commentary: slower length ball outside off, no pace to work with, flat-batted one bounce to long-on, and they run the second run just in case there's a fumble. The throw is at the keeper's end, there's no fumble from Dhoni, and Bairstow is run out by yards

name: RV Uthappa
shortText: c R Dhawan b Sandeep Sharma
commentary: length ball swings the other way in to the body at 130 kph, Uthappa tries to heave this over midwicket but is off balance and it climbs on him a touch to produce a top edge. It swirls over midwicket on the ring, easy catch for Rishi Dhawan waiting for it to come down into his arms at chest height with palms facing into the body.

name: MJ Santner
commentary: woof! This sums up better than anything how weak Santner was on his legs. He shuffles across and is bowled behind them! Fullish length ball at 138 kph, fairly innocuous except for the part where Santner goes too far across and leaves his stumps exposed. Misses yet another leg side flick and this time pays the price as it knocks out leg stump. Arshdeep cements the humiliation with a curious celebratory dance.

name: S Dube
commentary: drag on! Another fairly innocuous looking delivery angled across off stump at 130 kph, Dube doesn't really get his feet moving though throughout his stay and pokes at this with a big gap between bat and pad, gets an inside edge that cannons into leg stump.

name: MS Dhoni
commentary: it was a wide if he left it! Might have even gone for five wides down the leg side! Instead it's a leading edge skewed to deep midwicket! Half-volley directed well down leg, Dhoni tries to help it on its way over deep backward square ala the first ball of the over but instead it comes almost off the toe and flies to Bairstow who takes a safe catch 15 yards in from the rope at chest height.

name: JC Buttler
commentary: pulled into the hands of the diving mid-on! Vast, vast wicket! Hazlewood rightly given a second over in the Powerplay, a tight line, a fraction short, but not short enough to get over the top of the stroke. Siraj, permanently in the action so far, leaps forward to grab hold inches from the turf. This is getting very rowdy now!

name: R Ashwin
commentary: top-edged, Siraj calls and claims the catch! Short, but not that short, outside the batter's eyeline, as Ashwin commits to the front foot and can't get over the stroke. Straight up in the air, and plops into the bowler's hands roughly where silly point would be

name: SV Samson
commentary: ping! Out leaps the off stump! Another reverse sweep, but Hasaranga slides it through a touch faster and fuller, and that's a massive and timely breakthrough. Fifth time in T20s that he's got his man too

name: SO Hetmyer
commentary: hacked high, high, high into the leg side! Hetmeyer kneels into a slog-sweep, but he can't connect properly, fetching it from outside off, a spiralling top-edge, Maxi settles for the catch, and another massive wicket as he punches his blade in annoyance!

name: TA Boult
commentary: simply stunning take! Kohli at short midwicket flings himself into a full-blooded pull, low to his left and there's a moment of stunned silence before anyone really twigs he's clung on! He had a sighter from Boult's first ball, but that was quite the way to atone!

name: M Prasidh Krishna
commentary: tighter to off stump, a cuff to the on-side, an instant call, Prabhu at mid-on gathers, runs up to the stumps and beats the batter in a race to the crease!

name: V Kohli
commentary: Has the bumper got the better of Kohli? Is it off the helmet or the bat? Kohli's walking! Only nine runs and a wry smile to show. Was banged in short, right at the badge of the helmet. Seemed as if he missed the pull and got it off his helmet. But after Parag ran in from backward point and took a good diving catch, Kohli shook his head and began the long walk back. Got it off the toe end off the bat and no helmet at all. Superb delivery, must give it to Prasidh for working Kohli over.

name: F du Plessis
commentary: And Kuldeep strikes off his second ball. Soft dismissal. Length ball outside off, du Plessis looks to slap it over cover. Finds Buttler stationed there. He can't believe it but Kuldeep is ecstatic.

name: RM Patidar
commentary: 150th IPL wicket for R Ashwin and gets it in some style. Again bowls it from wide of the crease, the carrom ball that skids after landing on a good length. Patidar is back to cut it but misses and loses his off stump. Ashwin is up and running towards covers in celebration.

name: Shahbaz Ahmed
commentary: Keep Riyan Parag out of the game at your own peril! Shahbaz looks to tonk the shortish ball over long-on. But he ends up skying it up. Parag runs in from long-off even as the cover fielder runs backwards. The youngster puts in the dive to take that inches off the ground to give Ashwin his third.

name: PWH de Silva
commentary: The bouncer gets too hot for Hasaranga. The heat in Pune is already high and Kuldeep is increasing it with his incessant short-bowling. Hasaranga looks to heave this but ends up skying it, hurried by the extra pace. Sen settles under it at where non-striker would have been and takes it comfortably.

name: HV Patel
commentary: Fourth catch for Parag as Royal Challengers fold for 115. Length ball on the stumps, Harshal holes out to long-on, who didn't have to move an inch.

name: Mohammed Siraj
commentary: Aims Navi Mumbai goes Nagpur! Full ball, Siraj looks to smash it over mid-off. Gets it off the thick outer half and the ball carries to Kuldeep at third man.

name: Abhishek Sharma
commentary: drag on! Slower ball at 125 kph lands in the slot as a half-volley on off stump, Abhishek goes for a massive heave but the pace throws his timing off and he miscues this off an inside edge into middle and leg stump.

name: AK Markram
commentary: slower ball lands on a good length on sixth stump at 106 kph, results in a miscued top-edged swat on a cross bat shot off the toe aiming to go down the ground. David Miller charges in 25 yards off the rope almost to the edge of the 30-yard circle and lunges forward to hold on to a good catch.

name: N Pooran
shortText: c Shubman Gill b Mohammed Shami
commentary: half-volley at 138 kph, Pooran powers it flat to long-on but doesn't get the elevation to clear the man on the rope. Safe catch taken at head height a yard in fro the rope by Shubman Gill. Three wickets now, Sham-wow indeed!

name: Washington Sundar
commentary: super athletic effort from Joseph off his own bowling! Length ball into the hips, Sundar beaten for pace trying to swivel pull and belatedly sets off for a leg bye as the ball dribbles in front of square around short leg. Sundar was off balance with his body weight carrying him behind the stumps on the off side to slow his attempt for a run. Joseph charges forward, fields, spins, fires and nabs Sundar a foot short at the non-striker's end with a direct hit.

name: Shubman Gill
commentary: top of off, 144ks. Gill is looking to make room and use his pace to go over the cover, but Malik keeps his length, keeps his line, and crashes through to the off stump

name: HH Pandya
commentary: great field placement. The third man is fine, almost a back stop. Malik bowls the bouncer, Pandya is clearly late on it, and the top edge goes to that fine third man. Malik is blowing kisses in the sky. He is on top of the world

name: DA Miller
commentary: bullseye again! A 50-pointer rips out the middle of middle stump at 148 kph! Fired on a good length, Miller goes for a big heave over the leg side and instead of hearing bat on ball, it's the harsh and unmistakeable sound of ball onto timber!

name: A Manohar
commentary: one more bullseye! Five-for Umran! Fullish length on middle at 146 kph, Manohar fiddles at this more in hope than belief trying desperately to see off Umran's final ball before waging battle against someone else. Beats the bat and a faint deflection off the back leg onto middle and off stump halfway up. Umran runs away with arms aloft and kisses the sky!

name: AJ Finch
commentary: Sakariya castles Finch next ball. Nice and full, hint of inswing against the angle, clattering into off stump. Finch swings for the hills once again, he does not touch the ball this time

name: VR Iyer
commentary: pushed through on the pads with fine leg inside the circle. Venkatesh drops down to one knee and sweeps weakly as the ball lobs into the hands of Sakariya. Venkatesh's poor run this IPL continues

name: SS Iyer
commentary: Pant thinks he has his man. Umpire Pathak refers it to the TV umpire. Kuldeep snatches the bottom edge, but did Pant take it cleanly behind the stumps? Pant sticks his right hand out and takes a low, shin-high catch. Indeed, that is a fair catch and Shreyas has to go. Wrong'un from Kuldeep, he drags it shorter and wider of off. Shreyas searches for a cut and bottom-edges it behind

name: SP Narine
commentary: big appeal for lbw from Kuldeep, and umpire Pathak raises his finger. Was this wrong'un heading down the leg side? Narine thinks so and asks for a review. Nice and full, breaks back in. Narine plays back for it. The ball misses the inside edge and pings the pad in line. Ball-tracking suggests that it would have crashed into leg stump. Two in two for Kuldeep as Narine bags a golden duck

name: N Rana
commentary: Top catch from Sakariya running in from sweeper cover and diving forward to collect a low catch. Rana waits as they refer it to the TV umpire. It's a fair catch from Sakariya. Rana has to exit, he exchanges a few words with Pant while doing so. Misses the wide yorker, sliced in the air over the off side. Terrific effort from Sakariya

name: AD Russell
commentary: Kuldeep continues to smash his former franchise. He ventures wide of the crease, dangled up and turns in from outside off. Russell advances at him, swings and misses. Pant does not collect the ball cleanly and fumbles but manages to break the stumps, with the ball disturbing the timber after bursting out of the gloves. Lucky rebound for Pant and DC

name: MR Marsh
shortText: c VR Iyer b Harshit Rana
commentary: sharp catch at deep backward square! And what a scalp to get for your maiden IPL wicket on debut! A bit of luck definitely involved though. Leg stump half-volley at 141 kph, Marsh goes for another heave over mid-on but it clips the inside edge and flies flat and fast to deep backward square. Venkatesh Iyer judges it very well, comes off the rope five yards and takes a comfortable catch crouching at thigh height. That was not easy, probably 7 out of 10 in difficulty with the pace it had coming on the ball, but he made it look like it was never in doubt.

name: RR Pant
commentary: in the channel on sixth stump at 140 kph, Pant plays loosely well away from his body with no footwork and feathers a thin edge through to the keeper.

name: AR Patel
commentary: Laser of a relay from deep midwicket catches Axar short coming back for the second! Knee high full toss on the legs, flicked casually through midwicket, Shreyas charges hard off the rope and unleashes a howitzer to the non-striker's end where Venkatesh Iyer covering the stumps collects on one bounce and breaks the stumps with Axar's dive a half-yard short.

name: Q de Kock
commentary: QDK walks after an appeal that is turned down by the on-field umpire! It was a length ball outside off and de Kock tried to cut it off the backfoot behind point again but got a feather on it. The appeal was enthusiastic but was turned down. QDK walked before thoughts of the review coming in.

name: KL Rahul
commentary: Edged and taken! A length ball on middle has KL searching for it on the backfoot, he tries to defend it with a slightly open-faced bat. It looked like the wobble ball, it ended up taking the outside edge and went straight to the keeper who took it comfortably.

name: DJ Hooda
commentary: Direct hit at the non-striker's end! This might be out! Yes, Hooda is a goner! It was a short ball on the body that was pulled to deep square leg, Jonny ran in to the ball and, somehow, got a direct hit from the deep. The batter was a few inches short for the freak runout to be completed. Hooda was slow while turning which looked lazy but the bullet throw was accurate and took away any chance of Hooda coming back.

name: KH Pandya
commentary: Holes out to long-on! Cross seamer at a back of a good length outside off was going away with the angle, Pandya tried to drag it aerially over long-on but the bat turned in his hand and the ball ballooned up in the air. Dhawan took a comfortable catch with the hands pointing upwards

name: MP Stoinis
commentary: Slapped off the bottom of the bat to the bowler! Stoinis was making room and tries to slap the back of a good length ball that was spinning away towards the stumps, toe-edged it and the ball went slowly. That allowed time for Chahar to leap to his left and clasp it.

name: A Badoni
commentary: Skier taken brillianty by Liam! Another cross seamer on a good length on off, Badoni tried the whippy flick but got the top-edge. The ball went straight up, three fielders close by were converging, but Liam called it and took it with a forward lean as he came down.

name: PVD Chameera
commentary: Taken by deep-cover who ran in and held on to it with a slide! Chameera tried to go through with a straight swing but got it off the bottom of the outside half of the bat, it went up and Chahar ran in from deep cover and took it well

name: MA Agarwal
commentary: in the slot on middle stump, Mayank backs away looking to flay this 143 kph delivery over mid-off, he middles this drive but doesn't quite get the desired elevation. KL Rahul shuffles left and puts in a supremely athletic catch with a bunny hop reaching above his head going left to snaffle it.

name: JM Bairstow
commentary: full and wide at 141 kph, slashes this to Krunal at deep third man where a very sharp catch is taken lunging forward! This didn't get more than 20 feet off the ground, it was screaming flat and fast. Usually a team's weakest catching fielders are at fine leg and third man but Krunal judges this like an elite fielder and has soft enough hands on his lunge forward from five yards off the rope to ensure he holds on with ease at knee height. It might not look like a great catch but that's only because he made it look so easy and casual but it was a superb technical display from Krunal.

name: LS Livingstone
commentary: Livingstone trades power for finesse and pays the price! Shuffles across his stumps looking for a cute flick over the keeper and fine leg. He gets all the way across almost onto the wide guideline trying to flick this slower ball off a good length on eighth stump, the timing is off and a healthy but gentle connection off the open face of the bat goes through to de Kock at thigh height.

name: JM Sharma
commentary: arm ball skids onto middle stump as Sharma prods forward. Was it pad first or bat first? Big lbw shout denied as there's a double noise. LSG finally go for the review. Definitely pad first on the replays. Sharma is in trouble. Ball-tracking sure enough shows three reds. Decision overturned.

name: K Rabada
commentary: short and wide, slaps this high toward the cover sweeper. It floats gently toward Badoni who settles 10 yards in from the rope and takes a casual catch at chest height swiveling his hips sideways to take it with palms facing into the torso.

name: RD Chahar
commentary: slower ball at 127 kph on middle, clears the front leg to heave but top edges this to deep midwicket. Badoni remains on that side of the ground for the right-handers and runs in 15 yards before tumbling to his left for another sharp catch, holding on while falling to the turf.

name: V Kohli
commentary: Yorker kisses the base of off! Kohli made ample room and tried the cover drive but the ball was through before the shot. Shami raises his finger as Kohli walks back in frustration

name: F du Plessis
commentary: gets the outside edge, and what a return for Sangwan! Pitches around off, and angles across him yet again, and this time the RCB captain chases that. No foot movement, and gets a decent chunk of the outside edge through to Saha. Sangwan punches the air with joy

name: RM Patidar
commentary: Top edges a paddle to square leg. He was outside the offside tramline and tried to paddle it fine or find elevation, but it took the edge and ballooned to the Gill, who had to run just a few metres to his left to take an easy catch. Ends a productive, well-paced innings from Rajat. Sangwan gets his second.

name: MK Lomror
commentary: Miller takes a catch sans cable interruption this time! It's a full, wide ball that had Lomror slashing. He got it off the bottom, it went up and stayed up. Miller settled underneath and took it with his fingers pointing up

name: Shubman Gill
commentary: fired in, and may have trapped him in front. Given out leg before! He reviews after a chat with the non-striker. This will only save him either on line or on bounce. This went on straight with the arm, and he was looking to flick it away. No bat involved. And it will also return three reds. Hits the top of leg stump. Gujarat lose an important wicket, and a review. Shahbaz with a quick redemption arc!

name: B Sai Sudharsan
shortText: c sub (†Anuj Rawat) b de Silva
commentary: Rebound catch taken behind by the sub-keeper, Anuj! Sai tried to drive a back of a good length ball through cover, but it took the bottom edge and hit the keeper's pad. It lobbed up behind and Anuj had enough time to turn around, reach out and grab it. It's followed by cheers and celebrations.

name: HH Pandya
commentary: Holes out to long-on! Hardik saw the full ball in the slot on off, he whipped it like he often does. But the ball went flat and into Lomror's breadbasket. Shahbaz is stoked as he's turning this game around. Think he got it off the bottom, the lack of elevation was also key factor that led to the dismissal

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: JC Buttler
commentary: and the wide angle has worked. Redemption for Shokeen. He has shown the smarts here. Again hides this well outside off, but this is not going to be a wide. Bhuttler goes after it and manages just the cue end. He is caught at long-off, just before the death overs begin

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: SV Samson
commentary: a wicket second ball for Kartikeya. Oh it is a typical wristspinner's wicket. A rank long hop, but Samson has ended up upper-cutting it straight to deep point

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: SO Hetmyer

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: R Parag
commentary: short-arm jab to a short ball, and it goes straight to deep midwicket. Again looks like it has stopped a touch on him, and he has had to delay the shot. Just that mild pause in the downswing that has cost him the power

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: R Ashwin
commentary: no he bowls the slower bouncer to follow Ashwin as he moves away from the wicket. Looks to pull this fine, but ends up gloving it to the keeper. He walks off, but I am not sure his glove is on the handle when the ball hits him

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: TA Boult

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: RG Sharma
commentary: full on middle, goes to sweep and top edges this to square leg. Extra bounce from Ashwin, it was there to sweep on the length on fourth stump, but extra revs from Ashwin cause this to pop up to Mitchell adjacent to the leg side umpire for a simple catch at chest height.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: Ishan Kishan
commentary: short on the stumps, goes to pull this and top edges this over the keeper Samson, who shuffles a few steps to pouch it with ease.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: SA Yadav
commentary: flights this into the body, SKY tries to dance away to create room but is cramped trying to shovel this down the ground and a simple catch is taken by Riyan Parag shuffling 10 yards left before taking it at rib height with his hips swiveled sideways facing his friend at long-off to keep his body in control and soften the ball into his hands.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: NT Tilak Varma
commentary: full in the slot on off, goes to launch this down the ground and miscues this near the toe of the bat, it flutters gently to Riyan Parag at long-on who jogs forward and takes a very casual catch at thigh height 15 yards in from the rope before flicking it away with disdain.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: TH David
Q de Kock
commentary: Skied... and Lalit takes it at point! It was on a good length and was angling away which meant QDK had to reach for it. He did go for it with a flurry but the ball came off the bottom of the bat and went straight up. Lalit settled under it for a while and took it with the sunnies on and hands pointing up

name: KL Rahul
commentary: gone, Thakur gets a huge, huge wicket and the timing of it is perfect. So is the timing of Lalit Yadav's jump at the point boundary. Wide length ball, maybe a cutter. Rahul looked to slash it away over the fielder on the rope, but he can't quite clear Lalit, who jumps up, catches it, and turns around as he falls to the ground, holding onto the ball and making sure he doesn't touch the boundary cushions

name: DJ Hooda
commentary: gone, big breakthrough. In the blockhole on off stump, and Hooda, looking to get his front foot out of the way and punch down the ground, ends up hitting it back to the bowler at stomach height. Good reflex catch too, even though it came straight to him

name: MR Marsh
commentary: Edged, taken, umpire waited and gave it out eventually! Arm ball landed on a good length outside off and went straight. Marsh tried to make room and drive it hard through cover but got the outside edge. Quinton completed the sharp catch, Gowtham looked sheepishly at the keeper when it was given not-out but was given out later to his delight, and that of his teammates. Marsh leaves in frustration. Ultra-Edge shows no sound when the ball is next to the bat! The comms on air feel he should have reviewed it, but there was a woody sound and Marsh walked back immediately on being adjudged out.

name: RR Pant
commentary: bowled him, Mohsin's snuck one through! Full, on the stumps, and Pant looks to flick across the line and ends up missing. Not sure the ball did all that much. Pant simply played all around it, with his feet stuck in cement

name: SN Thakur
commentary: Second wicket of the over, caught on that shorter leg-side boundary once again. Slants a cutter away from off stump, and Thakur shuffles across to try and drag a slog-sweep into the leg side. All he manages is to hit it straight to deep midwicket

name: MS Dhoni
commentary: 135kph, fuller but down the leg side. Dhoni goes for the flick but ends up hitting straight into the hands of short fine leg

name: Abhishek Sharma
commentary: Well, the luck had to end, the timing was off on the pull. It was a cutter that had Abhishek going through with the shot a bit too early, he aimed for deep-midwicket but the ball went to wide-long on who had to move to his left and was able to take an easy catch. Ends an enterprising innings which gave SRH a good start

name: AK Markram
commentary: Half-tracker is pulled to wide-long on! It was short and slow, Aiden backed away and pulled it with all his might but it hit the sticker as he fell away, the eyes were on the ball as it travelled through. But it did not have the distance, the ball ballooned for Jadeja to take it comfortably

name: Shashank Singh
commentary: Given caught-behind. Shashank reviews. It was a short ball, seam-up, that went across outside off. Shashank tried to pull it with his front leg diagonally to the left of the back leg, there is a spike when the ball is close to the top-edge of the base of the bat. Decision stands.

name: Washington Sundar
commentary: Mukesh has four! Sundar went across and tried to paddle-scoop it but the ball went straight, may have kissed the bottom edge, and hit top of off. That's how far across he went.

name: JC Buttler
commentary: Brilliant catch from Mavi running to his right from long-on! And that's that tonight for Buttler. He had decided to tee off, clearly. Got a fair piece off the full ball on middle. Would have been a six, if not for Mavi, who timed his jump perfectly to catch that above his head.

name: SV Samson
commentary: Deep midwicket continues to be a popular catching zone today! Fullish length ball on off, Samson skies this a mile high but doesn't get the distance. Rinku Singh gets back into position very early to set his body before taking a step forward from the rope to take a safe catch at waist height.

name: KK Nair
commentary: Karun charges this and telegraphs it way too early. Anukul drags the length back very short, Karun continues on with a cross bat swipe to a short ball on fifth stump. Rinku Singh came in off the rope initially at deep midwicket before backpedaling and taking a head high catch five yards in from the rope.

name: R Parag
commentary: slower ball at 122 kph on off stump lands on a good length, heaves this toward deep midwicket but doesn't time it well. Anukul makes a casual jog 10 yards to his left along the rope and takes this at chest height in stride facing long-on three yards in from the rope.

name: AJ Finch
commentary: A delightful sight for a fast bowler. The stumps lighting up the alley! Finch falls early yet again and he wouldn't want to watch the replay. Length ball outside off, he looks to force the issue through the off side and only ends up playing it on. Immediately throws his head back in disgust as Kuldeep leaps to celebrate.

name: SS Iyer
commentary: short ball angled down leg, big nick on a pull shot and it looks like he's gloved this to Samson. The umpire incredulously signals it as a wide. Samson signals for DRS immediately and I think he'll win this. Sure enough, spike shows up on ultra edge passing the glove. Seemed fairly obvious live but Samson has DRS as insurance.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: Shubman Gill
commentary: sensational fielding. Hits the one visible stump down. Gill is not happy. He reckons Sandeep Sharma has got in the way. Gill has pushed this to extra cover and set off. It is timed well, and Rishi Dhawan also gets a good bounce. What also happens is that Gill realises he is struggling so he wants to run in the shortest possible line, which also involves Sandeep's follow-through. So he has to run around him. Caught well short. Don't even need the third umpire. Sandeep not in the wrong at all

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: B Sai Sudharsan

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: HH Pandya
commentary: what a big moment in the match. The wicket of Hardik Pandya after he has practically blocked out two overs in the powerplay. Looks to drive this length ball on the up, and the face is open because he is looking to go through the covers. The ball moves ever so slightly to take the edge through to the keeper

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: DA Miller
commentary: Livingstone ia doing a great job for Agarwal even as Chahar gets his hand looked at. Full ball, offbreak, Miller thinks this is in the wheelhouse, but I suspect it is too close to him. He doesn't get the height he needs. Also it turns to take the thick edge. Caught at long-off

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: R Tewatia
commentary: that wide length ball finally pays off. Rabada gets it spot on. Wide and slow, but not wide enough to be called. Tewatia tries to go into the short leg boundary, but gets a top edge, which is caught at short third man

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: Rashid Khan
commentary: he is on a hat-trick. Cross seam, on a length, just outside off, Rashid opens the face looking for a single, but it holds its line to take the edge through to the keeper

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: JM Bairstow
commentary: Sangwan clutches on after circling around to this top edge at deep fine leg! Length ball on fourth stump, another flat-footed heave results in a skier swirling behind square on the leg side. Sangwan jogs 25 yards to his left along the rope and steadies himself before lunging forward to cushion the ball in.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: LS Livingstone
V Kohli
commentary: Jaffa! A perfect off-spinner's dismissal, to use a cliche. And this is proper Test match wicket in a T20I game. Kohli has to walk back for 30 off 33 and RCB are in a hole. Tossed up outside off, inviting Kohli for the drive, just to turn past the inside edge and clatter the stumps.

name: F du Plessis
commentary: Moeen is in the team and on the board! A wicket on his second ball on return. Hafl-tracker on off and middle, du Plessis goes back and pulls. But finds deep midwicket to perfection. The RCB captain's poor run continues.

name: MK Lomror
commentary: low full toss at knee height, tries to muscle this over the midwicket rope but miscues it nearer the toe of the bat and it floats gently to the man at cow corner Gaikwad, who bobbles the chest high chance briefly before holding on five yards inside the rope.

name: RM Patidar
commentary: stupendous effort charging from deep backward square by Mukesh Choudhary! Length ball angled across off stump, heaves this across the line but gets under it too much and skies it over square leg. Choudhary charges 30+ yards from the rope from deep backward square coming forward in front of square, puts in a full length dive and holds on while getting plenty of carpet burn with his elbows sliding across the nailed in advertising logos square of the wicket. 10/10 on the judge's scorecards from Switzerland, Peru, Zambia, Fiji, South Korea and Canada!

name: PWH de Silva
commentary: Theekshana is on a hat-trick! Full on fifth stump, tries to muscle this drive over long-off but skews it again off the toe of the bat and it flutters to Gaikwad, who again juggles this chance 20 yards in from the rope but holds onto the second effort lunging forward at knee height.

name: Shahbaz Ahmed
commentary: Bullseye! Triple-wicket over for Theekshana! Carom ball lands on a good length on middle and skids on past a tame flick across the line to light up the stumps 3/4 of the way up middle and off.

name: HV Patel
commentary: full and wide yorker, driven wide of extra cover, who misfields to open the door for a possible second. DK wants the strike back no matter what. In fact, DK never left his crease and it was Harshal who ran two runs on his own that will sadly, and comically, count for none. He grounded his bat at the striker's end and came back as the relay came to Pretorius, who fumbled the take. It appears he has knocked the bails off the stumps without the ball in hand. But did the ball ricochet off his hands into the stumps with a bail still on to be dislodged?! The third umpire believes so and has given this out. One of the stranger and funnier sequences of cricket you'll see anywhere.

name: DP Conway
shortText: c Shahbaz Ahmed b de Silva
commentary: this time Conway does pick out deep backward square leg with the sweep! He got away with this shot in the previous over off Maxwell, but this time drags a full delivery from middle stump too fine behind square and doesn't get the distance nor the elevation, regulation catch at chest height five yards in from the rope for the fielder who barely had to move more than three yards to his right for the chance.

name: RV Uthappa
commentary: Oh, my word! Uthappa has picked backward point to perfection! A touch short outside off, it was there to be hit. And all he does is find the substitute Prabhudessai to perfection. Maxwell cannot believe his luck. CSK lose two in quick succession.

name: MM Ali
commentary: Harshal strikes back with the slower ball! Floats this up back of a length on the wide guideline, probably a wide of Moeen leaves it alone, instead he toe slaps it tamely to Siraj on the ring at extra cover.

name: RA Jadeja
commentary: length ball on fifth stump at 127 kph, skies a heave that swirls a mile high over mid-off in the ring. Kohli gets into position very early for this steepler that is by no means an easy take as gravity pulls it back to earth. Kohli goes up with his hands in front of his face, palms facing out, and takes a sharp catch while falling to the ground to cushion the take.

name: MS Dhoni
commentary: the fat lady is starting with her mi-mi-mi-mi-miiiii warm-up exercises. Length ball on fourth stump, Dhoni smokes a pull but doesn't get the elevation and picks out Patidar at head height five yards in from the deep midwicket rope.

name: D Pretorius
commentary: length ball at full pace, 129 kph after the slower ball to start the over, Pretorius is hustled for pace and skies a pull back over the bowler. Kohli takes a simple catch at long-on three yards in from the rope at rib height.

name: MR Marsh
commentary: 133 kmph leg-cutter sticks in the pitch and gets extra bounce, Marsh is too early on the shot and gets a leading edge which lobs to the bowler. The catch is taken with the fingers pointing upwards and the fielders celebrate. It was around a length and did not seem to move laterally but the revs meant Marsh's bat face turned as it closed on coming in contact with the ball and ballooned up.

name: RR Pant
commentary: Drags it on! A fully, a low fully outside off has Pant reaching out just a bit, he went for a hard cover drive but the ball took the inside edge, went back diagonally and pitched on its way to hit leg stump. Pant stood there, crestfallen, with his hands over the bat handle and looking down but had do go back. Gopal, who was taken down, comes back and celebrates heartily.

name: Abhishek Sharma
commentary: half-volley on the pads whipped straight to short fine leg! This was a boundary ball but Khaleel might wanna go buy lottery tickets tonight with the luck it takes to be caught where Abhishek falls. Whips this ball off his pads and middles it straight to Kuldeep who can't believe he's been picked out and falls over to his left to snatch this at thigh height. Three yards either side of Kuldeep and it's a boundary through fine leg.

name: AK Markram
commentary: one ball after nearly getting his head taken off by Pooran, Khaleel fights through the pain to snare Markram! Slower ball angled full on eighth stump at 127 kph, Markram is through the drive a touch early and skies this to Kuldeep Yadav at long-off. Simple catch taken in front of his face 10 yards in from the rope.

name: N Pooran
commentary: worst ball of the over and it's going to be a catch for Powell at long-on! Waist high full toss on fifth stump, should've disappeared into the stands again but Pooran miscues this a mile high to Powell who takes a simple catch at chest height. Pooran met the ball around waist height but he stepped several yards outside the crease and after a third umpire review it is deemed legal.

name: Shashank Singh
commentary: what a way to go. Almost a mirror reversed from the start of the innings of the catch at short fine leg gifted by Abhishek Sharma. Wide yorker is played with a billiards cue shot straight to short third man for a chest high catch to Nortje on the ring! It was a wide if he left it alone. Shashank tries to keep the scoring aggression going and pays the price for barely getting bat on ball.

name: Ishan Kishan
commentary: Soft dismissal as Kishan flicks one straight to midwicket! A 124 kph full ball on off has Ishan go through with the shot too early. This time, it costs him his wicket as that popped up straight to the fielder. The change-ups and disguising has come off for Joseph.

name: RG Sharma
commentary: goes for the reverse sweep, and strikes him on the pads instead. Not given, but Rashid has reviewed immediately. This could save him both on impact as well on height. There was a long stretch forward, and the ball has actually evaded bat and hit him on the area meant to be treated with gentle loving care. Oh, it reveals three reds! Just hits the top of off and middle, and Rashid has the breakthrough

name: SA Yadav
commentary: Straight up, taken at midwicket! Surya tried to lofts a length ball on off but the bat turned in his hand at the end leaving the ball ballooning in the air. He was through with the shot a bit too early. Rashid takes it comfortably, Kishan was as disappointed, if not more, than Surya who looked distraught himself.

name: NT Tilak Varma
commentary: Direct hit from mid-off, run-out check. The batter is about half a metre short and will have to leave. It was a full toss, just below the waist, and surprised Tilak who tried to hit through the line with a horizontal bat. Turns out it was over the waist, given no-ball late. Think the third ump had a word. The ball went in front of mid-off, who ran in and collected it, hit the stumps direct

name: Shubman Gill
commentary: Skier taken at long-on by Pollard! It was a legbreak on a full length on off, Gill came down the track and tried to whack it straight but he did not get to the pitch of the ball and the bat turned in the hand. The ball went straight up off the edge and Pollard had plenty of time to settle underneath and take it well over his head with his fingers pointing up. Ends Gill's excellent innings

name: HH Pandya
commentary: Runout chance after the direct hit from the keeper, sent upstairs. It was a good length ball outside off, Miller tried to chop it and got the underedge, it went behind on the pitch into the keeper. The proactive Kishan ran in and threw the stumps at the striker's end with an underarm throw. Replays show Pandya's bat was on the line! He has to leave, the game is in GT's hands but can this wicket change the status quo match-situation wise? It was Hardik's call and he slid his bat through, no dive. He looked to have known he was short instinctively.

name: B Sai Sudharsan
commentary: Hit wicket! as Sai swings himself off balance after being beaten on the pull His left hand came off the bat as the short ball at head-height was going past him, Pollard had beaten the shot with the lack of pace. Sai was probably expecting a length ball but not that short. It had him swing hard and the hand came off, the bat hit the stumps in the follow-through. Rohit celebrates. Pollard does so with his elbow whacking on his palm.

name: R Tewatia
commentary: Run-out review! He's gone! Tewatia pulled a length ball to deep-midwicket and scampered for a very tight second, the fielder ran to his right. Covered decent ground and threw it on a length to the keeper, who reached to his right and whipped off the bails. Tewatia's bat was on the line. MI celebrate, this game can still go both ways!

name: JM Bairstow
commentary: full on leg, misses the flick, roaring appeal from Samson for lbw and the umpire gives it out. Might be going down leg, Bairstow reviews it. It was a flat legbreak from Chahal that Bairstow wanted to flick off the pads. He is struck way down the crease, no edge, pitches on leg, ball-tracking says "umpire's call" for clipping leg stump and it's the third wicket for Chahal!

name: MA Agarwal
commentary: finds long-on, another wicket for Chahal, another catch for Buttler! Pitches it up a little bit but not much pace on leg stump, Agarwal wants to go all the way but he hands a simple catch to Buttler. Chahal takes a bow

name: LS Livingstone
commentary: Prasidh Krishna wins the contest with a yorker! Right at the base of the stumps, Livingstone has gone across to off again, swings for the leg side and misses it completely

name: YBK Jaiswal
commentary: Arshdeep breaks the stand and gets the big wicket! A stock, length delivery from Arshdeep just outside off, Jaiswal goes down the ground with a big drive but only gets height towards long-off, not much distance and the tall Livingstone gets under it

name: JC Buttler
commentary: Taken at short-third man in... a weird entanglement! It's a wide yorker and Jos shuffled across to meet it on the full, he tried to scoop it but the body was rotating, the ball hit the bottom and went to short-third who took it comfortably. Talk about living and dying off the same sword...

name: SV Samson
shortText: c S Dhawan b R Dhawan
commentary: Skied and taken by Shikhar! Length ball just outside off had Samson come down the track and slap it but the bat turned and the ball went straight up in the air. Shikhar waited under the ball and took it comfortably after backtracking a bit from point to make it a dismissal of the Dhawans

name: Q de Kock
commentary: gone, he's spooned it straight to long-off. Narine bowls a quick one into the pitch, just short of a length and spinning away from off stump. Stops on QdK as he tries to make room to hit over extra-cover. Goes through with the shot, but doesn't get hold of it, and ends up offering a simple catch to the man on the boundary

name: KL Rahul
commentary: length ball straightening towards off stump, and is KL Rahul run-out without facing??? Yes, he is! Sprints out of his crease quickly as de Kock mistimes his drive, off the inner half of the bat into the off side, and Iyer sprints forward and to his left from short extra-cover, spins around in a jiffy, and throws down the stumps at the bowler's end. Rahul, turning around and trying to stretch to regain his crease, is a few inches short. Massive, massive wicket, KKR get LSG's main man without having to bowl a ball to him. Replays suggest QdK called for the run initially and then decided against it

name: DJ Hooda
commentary: Russell strikes, and it's his extra bounce from that hard length that does it. Back of a length, hits the pitch hard, and Hooda, looking to pull from just outside off, ends up with his bat turning in his hands and the ball going higher than intended and not as far. Iyer runs in from the midwicket boundary and completes a straightforward catch

name: KH Pandya
commentary: gone, Russell gets his second, and this time it's the short ball banged hard into the pitch. It's angling away from Krunal, but it gets up above shoulder height, and he can't control the uppercut from that height. Ends up hitting it straight to the man at deep point. He goes for 25 off 27, a genuine struggle of an innings

name: MP Stoinis
commentary: Got him, but a few feet either side of that fielder and it may have been four sixes in a row. Looks for the cross-seam legcutter on an off-stumpish line, and it's a length ball with Stoinis deep in his crease. He whips it powerfully and flat, but he ends up picking out the fielder at deep midwicket

name: PVD Chameera
commentary: great finish from Southee here. Goes back of a length again, but angles the ball away from the right-hander and still finishes inside the tramline. Knew Badoni would be looking to create hitting room. Looks to reach out and slap it away, but fails to connect, and this time they can't get the bye, as Indrajith rolls the ball into the stumps at his end and catches Chameera well short

name: AJ Finch
commentary: One more wicket to the short ball! Finch made room and was followed, he was on the move as he tried to upper-cut it like one does with a twig while trying to pluck fruits from a tree but got the top-edge. It went straight up, QDK called for it and took it with a stroll to his left

name: SS Iyer
commentary: Short ball gets Iyer! It was a well-directed ball in front of Iyer's face. He tried to roll his wrist on the pull but the ball ballooned straight to square-leg. Badoni celebrates as LSG are now firmly on top.

name: N Rana
commentary: Knocks him over with a yorker! Rana was shaping for the short one, but the full ball came in just outside off, the front leg went to the side to make room and he opened the bat-face but it took the inside edge and went onto the stumps. It was a chop-on that almost looked like it came in, in real time

name: AD Russell
commentary: Taken at third man! Cross-seamed short ball had Russell swiping again, it went off the top-edge a few metres in front of third man. Holder, there, ran in, lunged and slid forward and caught it with his hands in front on the move

name: SP Narine
commentary: Krunal backtracks at cover and takes it! It was a length ball outside off, Narine tried to launch it over cover but got it off the bottom, it was going over Krunal's head but he backtracked and took the catch well

name: AS Roy
commentary: Given out caught behind and Anukul reviews. It was a length ball down leg and Anukul shuffled across and tried to nudge it fine, there was a subdued appeal from the bowler. The keeper underarmed the ball to the square-leg umpire. Ultra-Edge confirms that there was some contact made with the glove on its way and the decision stands

name: Harshit Rana
commentary: All fielders in and Harshit drives a good length ball down the ground. The ball trickled away and was pulled back, the throw came in and the batters came back for three. There could have been a run-out at either end, the ball came to the bowler and took off the bails before the batter made his ground. Comedy of errors ends the game.

name: V Kohli
commentary: what?! Another first-ball duck for Virat Kohli! It was gentle, innocuous delivery, fuller on the pads. Kohli chipped it on the leg side but failed to keep it down. A simple catch for Williamson at short midwicket

name: RM Patidar
commentary: Whacked to deep-midwicket and it is taken! Rajat stayed legside of the back of a good length ball following him, he opened his hips and took the front-leg out the way and pulled it in the air. It hit the inside-half of the bat. Suchith came and took it down in front. SRH finally hold on to a catch!

name: Abhishek Sharma
commentary: bowled 'im! This one doesn't turn. A length ball pitching on a length around off, Abhishek goes for the pull but ends up playing above the ball. Misses completely and the off stump is out of its groove

name: AK Markram
commentary: holes out to deep midwicket. Tossed up in the line of the stumps, Markram goes for the slog sweep but fails to clear Kohli who makes it look very casual

name: N Pooran
shortText: c Shahbaz Ahmed b de Silva
commentary: Spliced and taken! It was tossed up on the stumps, Pooran tried to slog it across the line, the googly took the edge and spooned up. Shahbaz ran in a bit from short-third and leapt forward to take it.

name: Shashank Singh
commentary: Holes out at long-on! Shashank came down the track and tried to loft the full ball over long-on but did not connect with it well enough. This, like the last two balls, were googlies.

name: B Kumar
commentary: And it's all over! Faf takes a good catch tumbling over after Bhuvi tried to loft a full ball after making room. It went up off the bottom edge and was taken.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: DP Conway
commentary: Conway has lost rhythm and now that he tries for a ramp, all he gets is a faint edge to Pant. No boundary since he was 81, I think. Khaleel has bowled the offcutter to draw the edge from him, and Pant dives to take the catch

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: S Dube
commentary: full and wide, away from his reach, but Dube still tries to go over long-off. Manages only the cue end. Caught at long-off

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: MS Dhoni

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: MM Ali
commentary: slower ball, draws the big mis-hit as Moeen looks to go over long-off. Gets too under it, and Warner takes the catch at long-off. The catch is important also because it keeps Dhoni off strike under new rules
