venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: RV Uthappa
commentary: big side is the leg side and Uthappa looks to play the pick-up shot as this legcutter trucks him up on the hip. Can't blame Uthappa, but the mis-hit settles with long leg

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: MR Marsh
commentary: Slog went up and stayed up, it is taken at long-on! It was full and wide outside off, the bat turned in the hand and Marsh knew he was in trouble. The ball was in the air a while, Ruturaj waited and settled under it. Took it cleanly

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: RR Pant
commentary: Leg-bail falls as Pant chops on! Pant looked distraught after chopping a length ball outside off, while backing away, and it went off the underedge into the pitch and then the stumps.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: AR Patel
commentary: Through the gate! It was a good length ball coming in slightly and Axar went with the full-faced drive, the ball went through and took off-bail on its way. Axar walks back gesticulating there was little he could done about that jaffa. It literally hit just the bail!

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: SN Thakur
commentary: Strangled down leg! It was a length ball pitching down leg, Thakur tried to nudge it fine. It took the edge and went low to MS' left and he took it. Bravo and Thakur have a friendly chat with smiles.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: Kuldeep Yadav
commentary: Sliced, goes straight up and it is taken at backward point! It was a length ball outside off, Kuldeep swung through the line and the ball took the top-edge, went up and Uthappa settled underneath and took a dolly

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: A Nortje

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: KK Ahmed
commentary: Slower yorker dips like a nacho and hits the stumps. Bravo runs around in celebration, it was low, Khaleel's bat went down from up, he stood still while he did it, the ball went underneath the bat and made its way to the stumps

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: VR Iyer
commentary: You live by the sword and you die by it - that's the saying Venkatesh followed tonight. Once again used his long reach to try and thwack it over the on side. But yet again, the length meant that it was out of Iyer's reach and all he could manage was a top edge. It was also the wristspin that was thrown a tad wider. Gobbled safely by Sams at cover. Kartikeya gets his MP team-mate.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: AM Rahane
commentary: And here's the false shot that brings Kartikeya the success. Another attempted reverse-sweep, Rahane misses it completely to lose his off stump. Kartikeya flighted this one and kept it closer to the stumps unlike the last ball. Gets rewarded for the second time this game. Smart, brave bowling.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: N Rana
commentary: That's out! Surely? The umpire doesn't think so and Mumbai opt for the DRS. Short ball from over the stumps, Rana is caught in an awkward position - he looks to duck as well as fend - and I heard a noise as the ball passed the bat. Ishan Kishan did so, too, while Bumrah also celebrated. But the umpire stayed unmoved. Rana knows he's gloved it and is seen walking back even as the Snicko confirms it. The short ball does him in yet again.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: SS Iyer
commentary: Beauts of a delivery to get rid of Shreyas Iyer, who is very good against spin. A perfect Test match dismissal. Tossed up, lands on a length around off and middle, turns, bounces, draws the batter forward. Shreyas lunges and only manages to glove it to Kishan behind. A wicket right after the break for Mumbai.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: AD Russell
commentary: Bumrah delivers with the big wicket and big man Polly seldom drops catches! Length ball this time around off, beats Russell for pace. Yet he goes with the heave and only ends up hitting it as far as Pollard at long-on.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: PJ Cummins
commentary: Tilak Varma's turn to take a good running catch now! Bumrah is bringing back the smiles in the MI dugout as well as supporters' section. Banged in short, Cummins' bottom hand comes off as he pulls. Ends up skying it high. Varma calls, has his eyes on the ball as he runs towards square leg from midwicket and takes it.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: SP Narine
commentary: Bumrah is here and how! Fast, faster and fastest have been Bumrah's speeds this over. This is the Bumrah teams fall to and he has got an amazing fifer. And mind you, he's only 16 balls into his spell. Runs in from around the stumps and bowls it back of a length. Narine is caught by surprise and looks to turn it round the corner. Gets it high on the bat and towards the bowler. Bumrah hares towards it and takes it in his followthrough, and throws it up in celebration. You have to be at DY Patil to feel it all.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: CV Varun

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: RG Sharma
commentary: back of a length on the hips, Rohit goes back to defend and it looks like he misses a back foot prod with the ball going off his back leg to the keeper. Rohit doesn't move, the appeal for caught behind is denied. KKR goes for the review. Looked fairly straightforward. All sorts of large spikes on ultra-edge before and after the ball passes the bat as well as while the ball is passing the bat. So many wild and large deviations there on the ultra-edge that it's hard to differentiate them. It looked like there was a gap between bat and ball too. But the third umpire says he's convinced one of those outrageous spikes had to be bat on ball. Decision overturned. Rohit is steaming as he walks off.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: Ishan Kishan
commentary: starts with a bouncer on middle and a top edge swirls to deep backward square leg where Rinku Singh continues to show off his catching prowess by holding onto this steepler 10 yards in from the rope, taking the catch falling forward to cushion the ball in.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: NT Tilak Varma
commentary: Russell keeps banging away on this same fifth stump back of a length ploy and it finally pays off! Tilak is cramped for room going onto his toes to cut, edge carries comfortably to Rana at chest height at slip.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: Ramandeep Singh
commentary: length ball on fourth stump, tries to heave this over midwicket and it winds up being skied tamely to short third man. Simple catch on the ring at chest height for Rana.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: TH David
commentary: David looked futile against spin and finally falls! 12 off 3 balls to start his innings against Dre Russ, 1 off his final 6 balls against a combination of Narine and Varun. He gets a length ball on middle that he tries to heave over the leg side rope but skies a top edge that swirls around before coming down into the hands of deep midwicket at chest height 15 yards inside the rope.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: JJ Bumrah
commentary: Back to back runouts end it! Length ball into the hips, goes to tap and run into midwicket. But Rinku is on the scene to charge, field, and fire from 12 yards out, his momentum taking him toward square leg before unleashing a skiddy one-bounce direct hit with one stump to aim at and catches Bumrah a yard short.

name: MS Wade
commentary: Wade will not want to see the replay! Avesh goes slightly wide outside off, Wade walks across to try and play a paddle over the vacant fine leg region, but ends up bunting a shot right into the hands of the keeper!

name: HH Pandya
commentary: short, wide, slashed hard, edged and taken! Hardik will be disappointed falling to a ball like that. Loses his wicket first ball after the time out. What helped Avesh was that he bowled full pace, and the extra bounce induced the edge to de Kock

name: DA Miller
commentary: short and wide slower ball at 116 kph, Miller slashes this straight to deep third man! Miller had one hand fly off the bat, was an uncontrolled shot to say the least and it sails gently to the man coming in five yards off the rope, catch taken at rib height.

name: Q de Kock
shortText: c Sai Kishore b Yash Dayal
commentary: six and out! Sai at cover point takes a tricky catch low. Dayal went full, de Kock looked to clobber a drive, but it's played square to the infielder

name: KL Rahul
commentary: into the night sky, and Wriddhiman Saha's not gonna miss that! The captain is gone, and Shami has picked off Rahul with the short ball. Off-stump line, rising delivery, Rahul pulls, but the top edge brings his demise. Shami does the season double over KL

name: DJ Hooda
shortText: c Mohammed Shami b Rashid Khan
commentary: top-edged sweep goes to Shami charging behind the keeper from short fine leg! It was a legbreak flighted full on middle, top edge swirled behind Saha and a very alert effort from Shami allowed him to take this in stride at chest height after running 20 yards to his right along the ring.

name: KS Sharma
commentary: flies to short third man! Almost as if the fielder was right there for that kind of shot. Left-arm seamers ruling here today as Dayal's length ball outside off tempts Karan to cut, and the thick edge flies to Miller inside the circle. Dayal gets his UP team-mate!

name: KH Pandya
commentary: a Rashid classic! Krunal stumbles out of his crease groping at another googly landed on a good length on middle that spins sharply past the edge. Saha whips the bails off and there's no bother checking with the third umpire on this one.

name: A Badoni
commentary: beautiful flight from Kishore and he gets Badoni dancing to nowhere! Slows up the pace and drags the length back a touch on fifth stump with Badoni telegraphing his charge, gets nowhere close to the pitch, flails aimlessly and Saha takes the bails off with Badoni four yards out of his crease.

name: MP Stoinis
commentary: as comical a runout as you'll ever see.... Fullish length ball on the stumps that Hooda sweeps to deep square leg. Stoinis thinks there's an easy two on the cards and turns to come back but Hooda doesn't respond and Stoinis is halfway down the track when a straightforward relay is fired in by Miller to Saha at the striker's end.

name: Avesh Khan
commentary: googly spins past another wild heave. Big appeal for caught behind from Saha but nothing from Rashid. Given not out. But Saha has convinced Hardik to go for the review. Ultraedge shows a feather off a slight inside edge. Decision overturned. A fitting way to cap a brilliant display from behind the stumps by Saha throughout this chase.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: YBK Jaiswal
commentary: steps outside leg stump, looking to give himself the best chance to flay Marsh away over the shorter off-side boundary. But Marsh spots his early trigger, and digs in a short offcutter, down the leg side. Jaiswal has no room, and miscues his pull straight to Lalit Yadav at deep midwicket. Clever from Marsh, and DC have their second

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: JC Buttler
commentary: pressure bursts pipe! Buttler chips straight to mid-on who hardly has to move an inch. Fullish, pretty much a half-volley on leg stump. He looks to swing it over the infield but his timing is awry and he picks out Shardul. Big early wicket for Delhi!

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: R Ashwin
commentary: gives himself room, stepping outside leg stump, but no timing. Looks to go up and over the off side but skies it hiiiiiigh straight to Warner, running round from extra cover towards mid-off

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: SV Samson
commentary: on a length, Samson skies it into the leg side... it's gone miles up, but Shardul settles underneath it and clings on! Angling in towards the pads, mistimed into the leg side. Thakur was running in off the rope and took an excellent catch, tumbling over as he did

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: R Parag
commentary: slower ball, Parag looks to muscle it over midwicket but mistimes it... and Powell settles underneath it to take a straightforward catch at long-on. Two for Sakariya, five for DC.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: TA Boult

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: MR Marsh
commentary: Top-edges a sweep straight to short-fine leg! Marsh is on his haunches before leaving but he has done his job. It was a full ball on leg and was there for the slog-sweep, the option Marsh went for but it spun away a bit and took the top-edge over the sticker. Kuldeep Sen takes a comfortable catch. Marsh leaves to a standing ovation from the dugout.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: RR Pant
DP Conway
commentary: Given LBW and the decision stays as there is no review! Ruturaj has a chat with the umpires but Conway has to leave. It looked to be going down leg. The full ball from Sams beat the flick and hit the pad, to Conway's surprise it was given out. It hit him just below the knee-roll, but looked to go down leg.

name: MM Ali
commentary: Soft dismissal as Moeen was in two minds on how to play the short ball! The short ball came in with the angle around throat-height and Moeen's hands went for the pull and then retracted but in the meantime, the ball took the edge and lobbed to short-midwicket. Dream start for MI!

name: RV Uthappa
commentary: Given LBW, DRS is still not available, Uthappa has to go! But this looked adjacent. It was a good length ball that that pitched around middle and beat the flick across the line and hit the pad in front of off. Uthappa looked to see if he could review but that was sadly not on

name: S Dube
commentary: Edged and taken! Dube stood still and tried to ramp it but all he could do is edge the short ball over middle and off. A good catch by Kishan as he leaps up to reach for it and take it. MI fielders celebrate in joy.

name: Simarjeet Singh
commentary: Given LBW, CSK review. It looked to have pitched on leg and beat the full-faced defence and hit the front pad just below the knee-roll. Nothing on Ultra-Edge. It's actually pitching on middle, the impact is in line and it's hitting the top of middle. Three reds and he's a goner.

name: M Theekshana
commentary: Taken at cover! Around a good length outside off, Maheesh reaches out and tries to drive it with a horizontal bat. It took the edge, a hand came off the bat, and and the ball floated to the fielder

name: Mukesh Choudhary
commentary: Is that a run out? This was odd, MS ducked under a short ball and scampered for the run. It looked premeditated, Choudhary ran in but was well short. Kishan's direct hit was on point and fast. He did it with the gloves on. MS shook his head, MI fielders celebrated

name: Ishan Kishan
commentary: Kishan didn't learn his lesson from the previous ball. Fullish length ball again swinging away from seventh stump, Kishan with another wild poke well away from his body and a thin edge goes through to Dhoni at waist height.

name: RG Sharma
commentary: Simarjeet replicates the ball at 3.2 and this time gets Rohit nibbling an edge! Good length in the fifth stump channel, Rohit pokes defensively away from his body and a healthy deflection carries through to Dhoni tumbling to his knees moving right.

name: T Stubbs
commentary: almost a carbon copy sequence to how Sams fell! Stubbs misses a juicy full toss on the previous ball that should've gone to the boundary and next ball is beaten by a back of a length delivery at 135 kph that traps him playing across the line on the back foot. He's nailed on the knee roll deep in the crease in front of middle. Huge appeal upheld immediately. Unlike Sams, Stubbs storms straight off without reviewing.

name: JM Bairstow
shortText: c Mohammed Siraj b Shahbaz Ahmed
commentary: big wicket for Shahbaz. Smart bowling. Keeps it wide outside off even as Bairstow leaves the crease. Bairstow looks to go down the ground, but gets a thick edge for a catch at backward point

name: LS Livingstone
commentary: oh he has ramped it straight up. Livingstone moves across looking to ramp him with the angle and over short third but Harshal bowls this closer to him. Livingstone also slightly early into the shot. Ends up going over the keepr but it goes high rather than long

name: MA Agarwal
commentary: Agarwal in two minds. Ends up getting dismissed. Again slower ball, into the pitch, about shoulder high or just under, Agarwal aborts his cut and decides to play a sort of ramp, but the ball hardly arrives, which means it goes straight to point as opposed to third man. Caught

name: JM Sharma
commentary: doesn't pick the wtong'un. Jitesh does the right thing in theory, taking on the threatening bowler, but he has come down the track and has been beaten in the air and then off the pitch too. Bowled middle stump

name: Harpreet Brar
commentary: six and out. Stays on pace, but bowls short of a length and wide, Brar looks to go into the off side, but manages only an edge through to the keeper. Everybody knows he is out but Brar waits for the umpire to raise the finger, which he eventually does

name: RD Chahar
commentary: low full toss, hit to long-on, good to see them trying the second that is not on. The throw from long-on goes to the striker's, and catches Chahar short

name: V Kohli
commentary: back of a length ball into Kohli's hips, late on a pull and the ball balloons off the body to short fine leg. Didn't look like it hit bat at all but the ricochet was so significant to carry to fine leg. He's been given not out on field. Punjab calls for DRS immediately. Ultraedge is showing a spike as the ball passes his gloves though. The decision will be overturned and Kohli's stay at the crease flames out like a supernova.

name: F du Plessis
commentary: length ball on fifth stump, Faf charges with a big swish and a massive appeal for caught behind is denied. Keeper is adamant about going for DRS and the captain agrees. Ultraedge is going to be two for two helping to overturn consecutive reviews in favor of Punjab Kings.

name: RM Patidar
commentary: good length ball again hugging the wide guideline, Patidar tries to drag this over wide long-on instead of playing with the turn into the off side and winds up clipping this closer to the toe of the bat floating tamely to Shikhar Dhawan, who takes a chest height catch 10 yards inside the rope.

name: MK Lomror
shortText: c S Dhawan b R Dhawan
commentary: Dhawan to Dhawan, tag-team for a wicket at midwicket! Length ball on leg stump, charge from Lomror who clobbers a pull straight out of the middle of the bat but picks out Shikhar on the ring at waist height. The Kabbadi thigh slap comes out in celebration.

name: Shahbaz Ahmed
commentary: slower ball lands on a good length on fifth stump at 115 kph, goes to cut and it floats gently to the man on the ring at backward point. He waits for it to arrive and takes a simple catch at rib height.

name: HV Patel
commentary: full length ball on middle, goes to drive and skies this over the ring. Mayank tracks this swirler over extra cover, runs backward and takes a superb over the shoulder effort.

name: PWH de Silva
commentary: full on fourth stump, goes for the big slog sweep and picks out Harpreet on the deep midwicket rope. He plucks this reaching high above his head after leaping in the air. He's falling over the rope and flicks this up before stepping over, then collects it upon reentering. Third umpire is checking if this is all kosher. It looks to be. A decade ago everyone would have lost their minds seeing this kind of effort, but this is standard fare now.

name: VR Iyer
commentary: Venkatesh chops on and knocks out his own off stump. Shorter outside off, from wide of the crease, Venkatesh throws his bat at ball and drags it back onto his own stumps. Gone for 7 off 6 balls. Welcome back, Marco

name: AM Rahane
shortText: c Shashank Singh b Umran Malik
commentary: Malik takes out Rana and Rahane in the same over. Shashank runs across to his right from sweeper cover and pouches it once again. He got into a side-on position, but managed to hold onto the ball at the boundary after a tumble. Banged in short and outside off, Rahane upper-cuts in the air, but can't clear the boundary

name: N Rana
shortText: c Shashank Singh b Umran Malik
commentary: Malik gets rid of Rana. This was on the cards, wasn't it? Flapped away to deep square leg. On a back of a length and just outside off, this isn't quite a full-blooded pull from Rana. Shashank runs in rather belatedly from the boundary but dives forward to pouch the catch. Rana helps it to Shashank

name: SS Iyer
commentary: 147.8ks, full and angling into the pads from wide of the crease. Shreyas goes deep in the crease, searching for the short ball. This is nowhere near that length. He still flicks it in the air. Holes out to Tripathi at deep midwicket. Dives forward to complete the catch

name: Abhishek Sharma
commentary: Varun gets the big wicket! Reduces the speed, Abhishek goes for the slog sweep and miscues it completely, it goes high towards square leg where Billings gets under the ball after calling for it

name: AK Markram
commentary: chops on! It's an attempted yorker outside off, just short of the yorker length, and Markram opens up to swing wildly to only get an inside edge onto his off stump

name: N Pooran
commentary: straight back to Narine this time for a return catch! Narine is turning it around for himself as the asking rate rises. Short and angling in, Pooran pounces on the short ball and slaps it back, Narine misjudges the trajectory and jumps a bit as the ball crashes towards his groin but he does well to get both hands to it

name: Washington Sundar
commentary: he strikes again, it's Russell's night. Bangs in the slower ball, Washington is through with his pull well before the ball reaches him, one hand comes off the bat when he makes the connection and the ball goes high to the long leg boundary where Venkatesh waits patiently to catch it just before the rope

name: Shashank Singh
commentary: the two Iyers are coming under the ball, Shreyas takes it easily! Scrambled seam outside off, Shashank swings big, doesn't middle it at all, and it's skied towards mid-off where Shreyas gets under the ball nicely

name: M Jansen
commentary: one more for Russell, a faint outside edge off Jansen's bat! Slower ball cutting away from length, Jansen swings into the leg side and makes a connection he didn't want to, low catch to the right for Billings

name: DP Conway
commentary: this is just too good. Test-match stuff. Round the wicket, angling in, making him play the angle, and then leaving him just a touch to take the edge. Conway tried to walk down at him but it didn't help him

name: MM Ali
shortText: c Rashid Khan b Sai Kishore
commentary: smart bowling. Slow in the air, outside off, making him drag it to his favourite slog into the leg side even though it is the bigger boundary. Caught at deep midwicket

name: S Dube
commentary: sharp bouncer. Joseph has been really quick this IPL. And Dube looks to pull out of this late, and gets a top edge into the shoulder and then through to Saha. The crowd, though, is delirious with joy. And they are all CSK fans, mind you

name: MS Dhoni
shortText: c Yash Dayal b Mohammed Shami
commentary: and Dhoni is out for 7 off 10. Shami digs this in, Dhoni looks to pull over midwicket, but perhaps the ball has come slower than he expects and he can't clear midwicket

name: Shubman Gill
commentary: No. 99 at the back, Malinga's bowling action, slinger Pathirana strikes first ball in the IPL! Gill clearly is done in by the pace and is late in bringing his bat down for the flick. It was speared full on middle. Gill asked for the review. Pad first, confirms the Snicko, Hawk-Eye returns three reds. First-ball strike for Pathirana, remember the name! What a mode of dismissal to kickstart your IPL career.

name: MS Wade
commentary: Wade loses two things on a single ball - his wicket as well as his bat! Yet again looks to attack Moeen but fails to pick the flight early. Almost yorks himself as he holes out to long-on. Loses his bat, that flew to almost hit Dhoni.

name: HH Pandya
commentary: Another wicket off the first ball of his over, Pathirana is on a roll! It was the offcutter that did Pandya in. It was full, Pandya was early into the flick and ended up getting a leading edge. Dube at mid-off kept his eyes on the ball and took it after taking a couple of steps back. Is there life in the game?

name: YBK Jaiswal
commentary: Skied and it's taken! It was on a length just outside off, Jaiswal tried to drag it through aerially and it took the top edge. Mid-on moved to his left, settled under it and took the catch

name: JC Buttler
commentary: knocks the off stump! Buttler has been on 2 off 5, he wants to up the scoring and goes across a long way to scoop it fine leg, but misses it completely. Avesh is pumped, he kind of gives Buttler a send-off while thumping his heart with his right fist. It was a perfect length ball that went on to hit top of off stump, which has gone to Marine Drive for a walk. Avesh had Buttler bowled the last time too

name: SV Samson
commentary: nearly checks his lofted drive, finds deep cover, and Holder brings out his three-finger celebration. It was slow and full outside off, Samson slices that square instead of muscling it over the covers, which he had been doing earlier, and it goes high before landing in the hands of Hooda in the deep

name: R Parag
commentary: Pulled in the air and it is taken by Stoinis at deep-midwicket! It was on the shorter side and on the stumps, Parag swiveled but the connection was not good enough. It went flat and Stoinis went to his right and took it low to his right

name: Q de Kock
commentary: taken at point, Neesham takes a sharp one! They're going to check for the catch though. de Kock slashed the short ball square, Neesham at point went low in front to get his fingers under it and the third umpire is convinced that he got his fingers under it cleanly

name: KL Rahul
commentary: Prasidh counters and Rahul is taken at point! He bangs it in short and nips it in from outside off, Rahul wants to slice it over point but Jaiswal is stationed absolutely square at the edge of the circle, and he times his jump nicely to get both hands to it

name: A Badoni
commentary: first ball duck for Badoni, Boult traps him lbw! Badoni reviews it after consulting his captain, but he might have wasted a review there because he wouldn't have asked Rahul if there was an edge. Pitched on middle, swinging in, hitting middle of middle. Boult knew it was out and appealed loudly with his team-mates as soon as it hit the pad after going past the bat as Badoni failed to connect with his flick

name: DJ Hooda
commentary: Samson uproots the stumps after fumbling the ball and somehow dismisses Hooda! The third umpire is checking, it came after Hooda came down the ground and tried to whack the full ball, following him, straight. It took the inside edge, then the pad, and dribbled to Samson. The keeper fumbled multiple times and managed to push the ball, sans control, on the stumps. Hooda, for whatever reason, did not try to come back. The third umpire decides on a few replays that the bails came off because of the ball, gives it out.

name: KH Pandya
commentary: Excellent assisted catch between Jos at long-on and Riyan at long-off! It's referred upstairs but it looked fine. It came after Krunal came down the ground and went through with the lofted straight swipe, it went flat but a few metres to the right of Jos, he managed to reach out, take it on the move, and lob it to his partner while going diagonally over the fence. Parag stretched up and back, he maintained balance and took it well. Ashwin celebrates as the crowd breaks into cheers.

name: MP Stoinis
commentary: Parag takes it easily at long-on this time! Length ball outside off, Stoinis slapped across the line, it goes up and Parag comes in and takes it. He slowly took the ball down and cheekily lifted it just before it hit the ground

name: PVD Chameera
commentary: Bowls him with a yorker on middle! Chameera had his front leg out the way, as is often the case at the death, and tried to whack it over midwicket. The ball went past the swing and disturbed the furniture to McCoy's delight

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: SN Khan
commentary: Arshdeep stands in the middle of the pitch and applauds like a man who knows his job. He has messed around with Sarfaraz's bat swing with his changes of pace and that bouncer. Now he bowls another slower ball but on a straighter line. Sarfaraz wants a six down the ground but ends up lobbing it over mid-on. Chahar takes a good catch running back

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: MR Marsh
commentary: into the short leg boundary, but also a mis-hit. Rishi Dhawan runs in from deep midwicket and takes the low catch. Marsh looks to play the pick-up shot over midwicket, but this full ball is just a touch slow off the surface and he gets too far under it and doesn't get the distance

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: RR Pant
commentary: LL with another big wicket. Pant leaves the crease early, Livingstone slips this wide, Pant swings hard but goes wide of the ball and is beaten on the inside edge. Stumped. Pant's innings: 1, 6, out

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: AR Patel

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: SN Thakur
shortText: c Harpreet Brar b Arshdeep Singh
commentary: sometimes missed yorkers work too. A low full toss, but the line takes away him arms. No room at all, and Thakur doesn't get power into his hit down the ground. Caught at long-on

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: Kuldeep Yadav

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: JM Bairstow
commentary: bounced out! Nortje persisted with the short ball plan to Bairstow and after leaking a few boundaries it finally comes off. Back of a length at 152 kph on fourth stump, the times he was hit for four he was bowling low to mid 140s but gets some extra mustard behind this to hustle Bairstow and produce a top edge that goes to deep square leg. Simple catch for Axar five yards inside the rope at chest height.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: LS Livingstone
commentary: Livingstone comes down the track and has a waft aimed over long on, misses and is stumped! It was slow and on a good length, it went away and Livingstone was not to the pitch of the ball. He swung off balance and looked behind. By then, the wily Pant took a moment and dislodged the bails

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: MA Agarwal
commentary: another arm ball! The previous one was too full and Mayank got away with it thanks to his footwork coming forward. This time it's on a good length on fourth stump and Mayank goes back to cut but it skids on sharply through a massive gap between bat and pad to crash into middle stump.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: JM Sharma
commentary: sensational from Warner, who finally holds on to one charging in at long-off! Knuckle ball from Shardul lands full on fourth stump. Jitesh slams a drive very flat in the air, doesn't get more than 15 feet above the ground but Warner never hesitated charging forward 15 yards off the rope and lunging forward to take this at shin height. There's an awful lot of fielders in Warner's situation who would have gladly let the ball bounce in front to keep it to a single but he went for glory and got a gold medal catch.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: Harpreet Brar
commentary: wrong-un beats him through the gate! Lands this on a good length on fourth stump, Brar plays for orthodox turn away prodding forward to defend and leaves a big gap between bat and pad. Faint inside edge before continuing on to the stumps.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: RD Chahar

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: K Rabada
commentary: Rabada can't repeat it to another slower ball at 118 kph! This is quietly a brilliant spell from Shardul. Slower ball on sixth stump back of a length, Rabada with another big cross-bat heave but it flutters gently to Rovman who takes a head high catch 10 yards in from the rope at long-off.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: Arshdeep Singh
Abhishek Sharma
commentary: Skied and taken at mid-off! Full ball around fourth stump, Abhishek looked to loft it over mid-off but it went off the bottom and he looked despondent immediately. Markande waited and settled under the ball before taking it well

name: N Pooran
commentary: Taken by Markande low down at deep-backward square. The third umpire will check this though It looks like the ball hit the palm and went towards the ground as Markande tumbled forward. The fingers were underneath the ball and it's given out. It came off a full ball on the stumps and Pooran flicked it aerially. Markande went diagonally to his left and looked to be slow on it but managed to reach it with a lunge on the move

name: AK Markram
commentary: Taken at long-off! Full ball was going away with the angle and Markram slashed at it. It ballooned off the edge and long-off took it to hand Ramandeep his third wicket

name: Washington Sundar
commentary: Full toss on middle and off knocks over off! Sundar slouched and looked to go over midwicket but missed. 250 wickets for Bumrah in T20s!

name: RG Sharma
shortText: c sub (J Suchith) b Washington Sundar
commentary: charges again and telegraphs it too early allowing Sundar to adjust dragging the length back on off stump, Rohit continues with the heave for midwicket anyway and splices it to the sub fielder Suchith, who takes a neck high catch while crouching ten yards inside the rope.

name: Ishan Kishan
commentary: superb effort running back from the ring at mid-on! Umran bowls this full on the stumps, Kishan goes to chip this over the ring but doesn't middle it and Priyam judges this well going 20 yards backward from the ring and takes a sharp over the shoulder catch on the run.

name: NT Tilak Varma
commentary: bouncer on off stump, Tilak is hustled sharply for pace going to pull and a top edge swirls over cover for a basic catch just inside the ring by Williamson.

name: TH David
commentary: Natarajan gets his redemption! Length ball angled across eighth stump, David slaps this off the toe to Natarajan's right and he gets a hand on it to knock it down trickling toward the non-striker's stumps. Ramandeep turns down the run initially wanting to let David fight another battle in the next over at some point but David is determined to keep the strike and pays for it. The ball stopped inches from the stumps. It was a footrace between Natarajan and David to pick it up and get the bails off and Natarajan wins it by a yard.

name: T Stubbs
commentary: yorker on middle, David jams this out back to Bhuvi who deflects this back onto the non-striker's stumps. Stubbs is cheating several yards out of the crease even before the ball is delivered, so he's a no-hoper when it comes to the deflection produced by Bhuvi. He appeals and the umpire stands there motionless so he picks up the ball and presses it to the stumps ripping out one chunk of wood for insurance.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: Q de Kock

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: KL Rahul

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: VR Iyer
commentary: Quinton de Kock is everywhere at the DY Patil Stadium tonight! He pulls off a screamer with the mitts on now. Oh my word! How does he get so much energy after playing the kind of knock he just did. Venky looked to tonk the length ball down the ground. But the ball nipped back in, took the inside edge and was flying to de Kock's right. But he dived full length to pluck that out of thin air to send decibel levels in Navi Mumbai soaring.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: N Rana
commentary: Gowtham roars as the noise levels soar! He gets his little revenge against Rana who walks back for 42 off just 22. Looks to continue his assault but holes out to long-off, where Stoinis takes it comfortably.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: SS Iyer
commentary: slower ball finally fetches Stoinis a wicket! Floats this full on fifth stump, Shreyas gets under this too much trying to clear deep midwicket. Deepak Hooda ranges 5 yards to his left and avoids a collision with the long-on fielder while tumbling left for an adventurous catch.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: AD Russell
commentary: Mohsin reels in the KKR whale! Slower ball at 123 kph lands on a good length on fifth stump, Russell tries to slap this on the up over long-off but doesn't get the power behind this. Hooda balances himself a foot in front of the rope at long-off and reaches up above his head to take this safely without needing a self-relay flick.

venue: Dr DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai
name: SP Narine
Shubman Gill
commentary: screamer from Maxwell! A length ball outside off. Gill pokes at it and gets an outside edge that goes to the right of Maxwell. He dives full length to his right and grabs it with one hand. The slip was brought in just before this ball

name: MS Wade
commentary: given lbw but Wade wants to review this. A length ball angling towards the stumps. Wade moves across, gets low and tries to sweep it fine. There's no bat involved and the ball-tracker shows it would have gone on to hit middle and leg. So the on-field decision stands

name: DA Miller
commentary: sharp return catch at head height! Floats this up full on middle, Miller unleashes a blistering drive but Hasaranga is in a solid position to have his hands ready in front of his face and intercepts it to prevent a trip to the dentist and/or some rhinoplasty.

name: R Tewatia
commentary: length ball on fifth stump, very thin bottom edge but clearly audible on a misfired pull, mild appeal for caught behind is given. Tewatia walks off with an uncommon failure.

name: V Kohli
commentary: googly accounts for Kohli! He came charging trying to smash another six to end the game in a hurry but doesn't get anywhere close to the pitch of this ball dragged back on off stump that spins through the gap between bat and pad and past leg stump. Wade is waiting for the ball shuffling left before taking the bails off.

name: F du Plessis
commentary: flighted full on middle, Faf goes to slog sweep this and a stock legbreak results in a top edge that swirls over cover. Hardik runs back from the ring in controlled fashion and takes a crisp over the shoulder catch.

name: DP Conway
commentary: Misses the sweep, given LBW! Conway reviews It was full and Conway was on one knee, the offbreak came in and hit the front pad. He was in front of the stumps and the leg swept to the side in the follow-through. No bottom edge found on Ultra-Edge. It's clipping leg stump and Conway has to go. It hit the outer half of the pad and looked to be going further down leg than it seemed on first view. Just over a quarter of the ball was clipping the stumps but that's all that was needed

name: MM Ali
commentary: Whacked straight to long-off! Moeen has to leave without a century! It was full, outside off. The back of the hand slower ball took the bottom of the bat and went flat and slow to the fielder

name: N Jagadeesan
commentary: Low full toss is sliced to the left of mid-off, taken around the knee by Parag! It was a slow ball and Jagadeesan made room to open up the off side. The ball went off the bottom of the bat. Parag underarmed it nonchalantly after taking it. RR are back in this now!

name: MS Dhoni
commentary: Taken at long-on! Flighted outside off, the good length ball was heaved at with a cross bat. It took the top-edge and went straight up. Taken easily by long-on. Chahal does rather well in the 19th over. MS has to leave after being unable to make up after chewing up balls in attempt to rebuild the innings.

name: YBK Jaiswal
commentary: another half-tracker outside leg, another mistimed heave trying to go for six and he picks out Pathirana at deep backward square leg, who runs 10 yards to his left, almost overruns the ball before recovering to reach back to his right for a theatrical catch 10 yards inside the rope.

name: JC Buttler
commentary: edge to Moeen at slip! Similar ball in the seventh stump channel that moves away a touch at 134 kph, pushes with hard hands at this and no footwork, thick edge flies to Moeen's left at chest height and he reaches across his body to snatch this.

name: SV Samson
commentary: superbly athletic catch from Santner! He flights this a touch more on a fullish length on off, the ball stops a touch as Samson tries to smash this through mid-off and instead he whacks it back toward Santner above head head. But he's in a great reactionary ready position to leap up high and pluck this reaching with both hands above his head to intercept it.

name: SO Hetmyer
commentary: floated full outside leg stump, goes for a massive slog sweep but skies this off a top edge to long-on. Hetmyer looked scratchy for his brief stay barring the boundary off the previous ball. This one hangs in the air forever as Conway comes in 20 yards off the rope before settling on his knees to cushion the take at rib height.

name: MR Marsh
commentary: Test match bowling, finds the outside edge and it's taken at slip! Marsh played at it with an angled bat facing mid-off, it took the edge a bit like it did with Joe Root at Lord's and Rohit took it low. He played it with hard hands and the catch was taken low to the right

name: RR Pant
commentary: 9-ball over ends with wicket with what should have been another wide! It was on a length outside the off side tramine, Pant reached out a bit too far and tried to drive it square with a horizontal bat. It took the edge, off the toe-end, and went to the keeper.

name: SN Khan
commentary: Big appeal for a caught behind, it's given! Sarfaraz has a chat with Rishabh and leaves! It came off a good length ball outside off, the legbreak spun away and Sarfaraz went for the late cut. The ball took the edge and Kishan took it and went up straight away

name: SN Thakur
commentary: Whacked, got a good piece of it but not enough to clear Tim David at long-on. It was a slot ball but around eighth stump. The ball was travelling in the air. Tim waited as the ball took a while to come back down, settled, and took it at head-height with fingers pointing up

name: Ishan Kishan
commentary: flighted full on the legs, goes to slog sweep this over the leg side but skies this to wide long-on where Warner takes a simple catch 10 yards inside the rope with hands moving to the side of his face at head height.

name: RG Sharma
commentary: Rohit is mercifully put out of his misery. This innings sums up his season. Half-volley on fourth stump, should've disappeared straight back down the ground to the rope but he scuffs a tame chip that floats in slow motion to mid-on. Basica catch at thigh height by Shardul on the ring.

name: D Brevis
commentary: fullish length and wide near the guideline outside off at 129 kph, goes to drive this through cover but drags a bottom edge into the stumps. The drop by Pant only costs 12 runs in the end. Shardul with a peculiar non-celebration. Did he want Brevis to stay there and soak up singles and dots so that Tim David spends more time in the dugout? Hmmm....

name: NT Tilak Varma
commentary: back of a length on middle, leans back trying to uppercut this over third man, huge nick behind. Pant and Nortje appeal. Umpire takes an eternity before giving it out.

name: TH David
commentary: goes for yorker on the legs at 134 kph, winds up as an ankle high full toss that David tries to flick over the leg side, leading edge floats up to extra cover on the ring. Simple catch by the fielder stepping forward Prithvi Shaw.

name: Abhishek Sharma
commentary: very well taken by a leaping Livi at long-on! He had to time his jump well there, and he did that. Livi leaps, and grabs that with both hands firmly while finishing just inside the rope. Full delivery just outside off with a hint of flight, as Abhishek tries to slog that over the fence in an attempt for another maximum. Sunrisers losing momentum in search of quick runs

name: AK Markram
commentary: appeal for stumping! I think Markram is short of his crease since he lost balance there. Yup, his back foot is short of the crease. Sharp turn and bounce beats the batter there. Brar tossed it up on a good length on leg stump, as the ball turned away to end up outside off while also almost shooting off the surface to surprise Markram. The third umpire also check for an edge there, as he takes the help of UltraEdge. Markram lost balance because of the dual problems, as the keeper collected it close to his chest there

name: N Pooran
commentary: edged, and taken by the keeper! This was angling away after pitching full and on fifth stump. It was more of a Test-match line and length, almost tempting the batter to drive and play away from his body. Pooran is attracted by that full length, as he goes fishing outside off while trying to drive, but gets an outside edge right into the keeper's gloves. Kings taking out one after another here

name: Washington Sundar
commentary: dolly for short third man! Sundar is foxed by that slower-ball bouncer. Ellis lands that short on middle, as Sundar has to leap while trying to get on top of the bounce there. He waits and waits for the ball to come - perhaps not expecting to rise that much, considering the lack of pace - and ends up spooning it to the fielder

name: B Kumar
commentary: Bhuvneshwar is run-out at the keeper's end! And it's a no-ball even as the players rush back to the dressing room. That looks like a tight call for no-ball though. A yorker sent firing just outside off, as Shepherd has a swing but is beaten but the length. The batters try to steal a bye, as Bhuvi is way of the crease while Jitesh destroys the stumps after running forward

name: JM Bairstow
commentary: End of the Bairstow show! And there are some words exchanged as well. Full and straight, Bairstow looks to thump it but misses and sees his stumps disturbed. Farooqi leaps to let his glee known. Malik would be heaving a sigh of relief that the drop hasn't cost them much.

name: M Shahrukh Khan
shortText: c Washington Sundar b Umran Malik
commentary: And Malik gets on the board early! Ambitious from Shahrukh, who looks to tonk the length ball - a bit on the shorter side - over long-on. It was hurled at 144 clicks and he ended up getting it high on the bat. It went a long way up and Washington, running back from mid-on, ensures he has his eyes on it and takes it safely. End of Shahrukh's cameo.

name: MA Agarwal
commentary: Mayank's painful, literally, stay in the middle comes to an end. He was rattled early on and didn't look his self at all. Goes back to this short ball but pulls it straight down deep midwicket's throat. Didn't get enough power due to the pain. Washington strikes.

name: JM Sharma
commentary: taken on the edge of the rope at long-on! Jitesh's fun ends. Garg takes a well-judged catch, with the rope barely a step away from his boot. A short of a length ball on middle and off, as Jitesh's eyes light up on seeing a possible six opportunity. But he ends up sending that right where the man was placed

name: YBK Jaiswal
commentary: has him nicking and Jaiswal walks! This is a lovely delivery. Not there for the drive, and Jaiswal ends up wafting at that with no feet. The thin nick is snaffled by Saha. They all went up in an appeal, but Jaiswal didn't wait for the umpire to lift his finger.

name: JC Buttler
commentary: Buttler is run-out off the last ball, as he falls short of an obvious attempted second! He is way short of the crease even as he tries to dive. But, but, but... as cruel as it gets for Buttler and Royals, it is a no-ball. Tries to swipe that length ball to long-on, as he clears his front leg. They run for the second - as they always would have considering this was ideally the final ball - and Buttler is short

name: SV Samson
commentary: dragged to long-on! Sai Kishore has his man. Clever piece of bowling. Gave it air - quite brave of him to do so - got the ball to dip on Samson as he made room to go inside-out over long-off but ended up dragging it to the leg side and an easy catch to Alzarri.

name: SO Hetmyer
commentary: taken at long-on! Hetmyer gets one in the slot - the ball was full on middle, and shaping to angle in - as Hetmyer went for the slog over the on side but failed to connect. Crucial wicket for Titans, even if Hetmyer wasn't entirely going on very rapidly

name: R Parag
commentary: another run-out, this time Parag! Parag and Ashwin end up at the keeper's end. And the free hit stays. It is a slower ball which is bowled full and wide of off. Ashwin lets that go since it is a wide, but Parag wants a run, leaving Ashwin stuck in his crease, as he never seemed interested for it

name: Shubman Gill
commentary: hara-kiri with the running and that's a massive wicket! Gill works this full delivery to the man coming in from deep midwicket and turns for the second, only to see Wade take a couple of steps for the second and then freeze. It gave Padikkal the extra second to send in a superb throw on the bounce to Hetmyer who did the rest at the bowler's end. Unfortunate end to an innings that promised the moon

name: MS Wade
commentary: Wade has fallen in trying to pull one to the longer boundary. He hit it well alright but found the lone man at deep midwicket. Buttler leapt high and kept his balance as he took the catch comfortably a yard or two inside the rope. The double-strike could hurt the Titans.

name: V Kohli
shortText: c Mohsin Khan b Avesh Khan
commentary: this would probably have been wide for height had Kohli not caught up with it. Kohli backs away early, Avesh bounces him, Ko0hli looks for the upper-cut, but gets only the toe end and is caught at third man. It has to be a big mis-hit at Eden for it to be caught because it is such a small ground. So this has missed the good part of the bat by a long way

name: F du Plessis
commentary: what a superb delivery. Lovely bowling. It shapes in a little, setting du Plessis up to play for the inswinger, and then pitches on a length and leaves him ever so slightly to take the edge. Golden duck for du Plessis. Mohsin has been the find of this IPL, and here he is on the big occasion sending back someone reputed to be the big-match man

name: MK Lomror
commentary: and a wicket immediately after the break. Bishnoi goes wide and full, Lomror reaches out, drives it hard, but doesn't bother keeping it down or try to go over the extra cover. Low catch for Rahul there

name: Q de Kock
shortText: c du Plessis b Mohammed Siraj
commentary: It's celebration time for the RCB and the Eden Gardens makes its loyalties known with the huge cheer as de Kock falls cheaply. Not the start Super Giants would have wanted. He looks to lift this over mid-on but the bat turns his hands. Easy catch for du Plessis running to his left.

name: KL Rahul
commentary: Is this the telling blow? Eden Garden erupts. Hazlewood is down on his knee with his fist up in celebration. Shahbaz Ahmed has run half a lap after timing his jump to catch that. And Rahul has all sorts of emotions on display as he walks back after making a half-century that could well be not enough for his side. He gets across to play the scoop to a low full toss outside off. But does not get it over the short fine leg fielder, who times his jump perfectly.

name: DJ Hooda
commentary: Is there life in this or what? This game is going one way and then the other, see-sawing like only T20 can. Hasaranga gets his man after being hit for a couple of maximums and lets everybody at the Eden Gardens know who the boss is. Bowls the wrong-un with a flatter trajectory, Hooda looks to go over cow corner. Misses it completely and sees the stumps shattered.

name: MP Stoinis
commentary: Fielder, fielder, takes it! Rajat Patidar is the man at deep extra cover and he couldn't have been found any more perfectly. They check for the height - if it is below the shoulder - and it well is. It was another back of length offcutter outside off. Stoinis hopped and slapped it straight to the fielder, who didn't have to move an inch.

name: KH Pandya
commentary: Two in two and is that it for Super Giants? You need to look at Kohli to know in whose favour this game is. It was a full toss at a slower pace. All Krunal could do was to spoon a catch back to the bowler. Walks back for a golden duck.

name: V Kohli
commentary: Kohli walks back! Short of length outside off, just holding its line after pitching. Kohli follows it with his hands, looking to work it behind point but gets an outside edge. Samson grabs it with reverse mitts

name: F du Plessis
commentary: this time it goes towards Ashwin who takes a good, overhead catch. A length ball angling across. du Plessis looks to smash it through covers but gets an outside edge that flies towards short third man. Ashwin leaps and takes it with both hands. du Plessis walks back for 25 off 27

name: RM Patidar
commentary: taken right at the edge of the long-off boundary. A length ball around off, Patidar looks to go down the ground this time but doesn't the required power behind it. Buttler takes it overhead and for a moment, he lost his balance and and looked like he would step outside the boundary. So he lobs the ball up, regains his balance and pouches it again. They are checking upstairs but Buttler never stepped outside

name: MK Lomror
commentary: 123.8kph. Short of length outside off, goes for the cut but ends up slicing it towards backward point where Ashwin moves to his left and takes it

name: PWH de Silva
commentary: middle and leg stumps have been flattened. A 147kph yorker from Prasidh, right in the blockhole, and Hasaranga had no answer to it. Fails to get his bat down in time. Prasidh on a hat-trick

name: HV Patel
commentary: fuller on the stumps, Harshal moves across and looks to help it over short fine leg but misses and the ball hits the off stump

name: YBK Jaiswal
commentary: and he gets a wicket immediately but not in the manner we were imagining it to be. He is extremely lucky here. Bowls a wide half-volley, which Jaiswal usually puts over the infield but this time he finds Kohli at point

name: SV Samson
commentary: Hasaranga has got Samson again. He tries a really ambitious charge vat Hasaranga, who goes wider with the wrong'un, beats him, and gets the stumping

name: YBK Jaiswal
shortText: c Sai Kishore b Yash Dayal
commentary: Another short ball, dragged away to Sai Kishore at deep square leg. Another short ball at the body, Jaiswal goes back and ends up splicing it to the outfielder. Dayal provides GT with the breakthrough. Jaiswal never really got going tonight

name: JC Buttler
commentary: Pandya takes out Buttler. Pandya has the massive crowd behind him. Pandya hits that back of a length once again. This straightens away as opposed to angling into Buttler. The batter aims to dink it down to third man. Instead, he can only guide the ball right into the gloves of Saha. Terrific bowling from Pandya

name: SV Samson
commentary: Pandya strikes second ball to dismiss his opposite number. Pandya hits an in-between length outside off. Samson shapes up for the pull but can only sky it to Sai Kishore who runs forward and then across to his right to short third man to snag another catch

name: SO Hetmyer
commentary: Pandya pockets a return catch and wheels away. Catch him if you can! He has taken out Samson, Buttler and Hetmyer. Suspect it was the back-of-a-length cutter from Pandya. Hetmyer eyes up the midwicket region and ends up plopping a return catch via a leading edge. Pandya ends with a potentially title-winning 4-0-17-3

name: R Ashwin
commentary: Sai Kishore has his TN senior and hero Ashwin holing out to long-on. Ashwin advances at Sai who held this back and shortened his length. Ashwin aims for the loft, despite not getting closer to the pitch, and picks out Ferguson in the deep. Ashwin is gone for 6 off 9 balls

name: R Parag
commentary: Shami yorks Parag to cap the innings. 145.1ks yorker, squeezes underneath Parag's swinging bat and clatters into off stump. Fine finish from Shami. Titans need 131 to win the IPL title

name: TA Boult
commentary: Sai slows this up on middle, finds some grip and turn, Boult can't quite get underneath the length. So, he ends up slicing the ball to wide long-off. Good comeback from Sai after conceding a six although that was a mis-hit as well

name: MS Wade
commentary: taken at short midwicket. Fuller length on the pads, shaping away a bit. Wade goes for the flick but looks like the ball got stuck just a little in the pitch. That means he ends up hitting it in the air. Parag leaps and takes it with both hands, just over his head

name: HH Pandya
commentary: Chahal strikes! A classic legspinner's dismissal. Pitching on middle and leg and spinning away. Hardik was anyway staying leg side of the ball. Gets an outside edge and straight into the hands of slip. Chahal wins the purple cap

name: DP Conway
commentary: Shami breaches the defences of Conway. That's his 100th IPL wicket. Nice and full, angling in from round the stumps, seaming in at Conway who aims for a booming drive and misses. Bowled through the gate. The ball clatters into middle stump

name: MM Ali
commentary: Rashid strikes back, Moeen has nicked off. Easier decision for umpire Khalid. Rashid takes this wrong'un away from the left-handed Moeen who throws his bat at ball once again. He edges it behind to Saha who clings onto the catch

name: S Dube
shortText: c Rashid Khan b Mohammed Shami
commentary: throat-high short ball angling across Dube. He goes for another hook, this time he holes out to Rashid Khan at deep square leg. Another mis-hit and this goes to the fielder. Dube has been put out of his misery

name: RA Jadeja
commentary: Joseph goes for the yorker, marginally misses it, but has Jadeja holing out to deep midwicket. Mighty full on the pads, Jadeja picks it up and picks out the fielder on the boundary

name: Shubman Gill
commentary: this one is an easy catch for eep midwicket. Tries the pull but it goes off the toe end of the bat as he is dragging it from wide outside off. Taken at deep midwicket this time

name: B Sai Sudharsan
commentary: oh not the best delivery to get a wicket. Short and wide, Sudharsan looks for the cut, but it is too wide for him, and he just toe-ends it to Dhoni

name: HH Pandya
commentary: Jadeja has not given Pandya anything so far. So he goes down to look to sweep. Jadeja keeps doing what he has been doing. Look to hit top of off. Pandya sweeps from around off, misses it completely, and Jadeja hits somewhere near the top of off

name: V Shankar
commentary: big wicket. Looks like a slower ball. Shankar is looking to go in the air and down the ground. Gets the right length, right in the slot, but he seems to have bowled a slower ball. Shankar ends up toe-ending it for a catch half way between mid-off and long-off. CSK still alive

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: Prabhsimran Singh
commentary: Southee has the last laugh as this good length ball angles down leg and Singh can't help himself but try to flick it fine. This time it gets an edge and Gurbaz moves to his left to gobble it up. Singh's cameo is over, but it has given the Kings a positive start

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: JM Sharma
commentary: too much experience in Southee and he produces another good ball after being hit for a six. Slower ball just outside off stump. Jitesh loses his shape while trying to slog it over the leg side, but this sliced shot produces a simple catch for Umesh at short third

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: SM Curran

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: M Shahrukh Khan

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: Rahmanullah Gurbaz
commentary: Ellis pings off stump! This time Ellis had good pace on ball, changing up after that slower delivery last ball. Gurbaz, again trying to be adventurous, makes some room, and also skips out of the crease. He hacks at this good length ball on off - rather it was on the shorter side of a length - and missed, as the stumps lit up

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: AS Roy
shortText: c Sikandar Raza b Arshdeep Singh
commentary: spooned to midwicket! Arsh gets two in this over. I think the extra bounce came into play again, and so did his pace. Another short delivery from Arsh, and this one's line was a bit wide of off, much further away from the middle-stump line he cramped Anukul with previous ball. Anukul pulled, but found the fielder, who didn't have to move at all

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: VR Iyer
commentary: ramped to point! Another bouncer from Arsh earns him a wicket. The bounce was a bit too much for Iyer to handle. Arsh banged it in short around off, with Iyer almost leaping a little to get on top of it. But he ended up half-ramping it - and without any control - to point

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: N Rana
commentary: slashed straight to point! Raza is relieved after going for some runs in his first two overs. That was there to be hit, but Rana ended up gifting the fielder an easy catch. Short and slightly wide of off, as Rana went hard at that only to end up walking back. So the time-out breaks his momentum

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: AD Russell
commentary: finds deep midwicket! Could be exactly the wrong time for KKR, given there is rain around. Curran went short again, except that this one was an offcutter, which rose sharply after pitching middle and leg, while also angling across. Russell swat-pulled but only off the higher side of the bat

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: SN Thakur

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: SP Narine

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: KL Rahul
commentary: slower ball trap works! 118 kph delivery back of a length on the legs, flicks this loosely to deep backward square leg where Axar gleefully takes the catch five yards inside the rope at chest height.

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: KR Mayers
commentary: goodness what turn! Axar digs this into the pitch on a good length on fifth stump, Mayers goes to cut and the ball jacknifes back into off stump. If that happened on a highway there'd be a 20-car pileup and three lanes of traffic closed while the police investigate the cause of the crash. Extraordinary delivery.

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: DJ Hooda
commentary: flighted a lot more on a full length on fourth stump at 81 kph, Hooda is through the shot too early and skies the drive to Warner at long-off. He waits under it for a simple catch taken on his haunches seven yards in from the rope.

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: KH Pandya

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: MP Stoinis
commentary: length ball angled across sixth stump at 134 kph, Stoinis chases at this flat-footed and slices a thick edge through to the keeper at waist height. Khaleel charges off and celebrates wildly like a man who definitely and most certainly did not drop a different batter on 14 who went on to make 73...

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: N Pooran
commentary: fullish length ball outside off, smokes a drive flat to Prithvi in the ring at extra cover. He catches it in self-preservation at waist height. Absolute howitzer off the bat of Pooran but he couldn't have picked out Shaw any better.

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: A Badoni
commentary: slower length ball on fourth stump, Badoni shuffles across trying to scoop this over fine leg but is thrown off by the lack of pace and it pings the gloves before ballooning to the left of Sarfaraz, who shuffles five yards and reaches forward for a simple take.

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: MR Marsh
commentary: double bullseye! Another 147 kph bullet leaves a big dent in off stump about three-quarters of the way up! Marsh shuffled across to cover his stumps and goes for an expansive drive to a ball pitching on fifth stump but is beaten by the sharp inward movement.

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: SN Khan
commentary: Sarfaraz can't escape the short ball from Wood! Banged in short at 137 kph, Sarfaraz ducks and almost falls over getting out of the way of the ball... before bizarrely changing his mind in a split second as he's losing his balance and tries to attack this with an uppercut over the keeper. He gets plenty of bat on it, enough for it to sail to deep fine leg who is fielding very straight and takes a chest high catch three yards in from the rope.

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: AR Patel
commentary: It's Wood's night for sure! Leg stump half-volley flicked effortlessly but picks out deep square leg. Sub fielder Mankad takes a simple catch at chest height four yards inside the rope. Not as spectacular a delivery as his previous three wicket-taking balls but still effective nonetheless.

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: Kuldeep Yadav

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: C Sakariya
commentary: Wood has a five-for after making the switch to LSG! Length ball outside leg stump, Sakariya tries to heave this over the leg side but is rushed and it flutters up off the splice of the bat to Krunal at short midwicket inside the ring. Simple catch.

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: Mukesh Kumar

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: YBK Jaiswal
commentary: holes out to deep square leg! Farooqi has another! Banged into the track on middle and off stump. Jaiswal looks to heave it away but does not quite get hold of it. In fact, he toe-ends it and Mayank does the rest on the fence! Vital wicket for SRH!

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: JC Buttler
commentary: cleans him up! Farooqi breaks through! Back of a length delivery at the stumps that skids through and might have just nipped back a shade. Buttler tries to punch on the up but the movement means that he gets a feather of an inside edge that cannons into the top of middle and off stump! Much-needed respite for the Sunrisers Hyderabad!

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: SV Samson
commentary: taken brilliantly at deep mid wicket! Slower delivery on a back of a length outside off stump, and this one just sticks in the surface a little. Samson has a swipe at it but is early into the stroke. He shanks it towards deep mid wicket, where Abhishek settles himself, catches it and then tiptoes his way out of trouble beautifully!

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: R Parag
commentary: taken at short third man! Natarajan gets Parag! Back of a length delivery outside off stump. Parag has a hack across the line but the angle means that he always has to fetch it. The ball also gets a little big on him and he ends up top-edging it towards short third man, where Farooqi settles under the high ball and completes the catch!

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: SO Hetmyer

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: R Ashwin

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: Abhishek Sharma
commentary: cleans him up with an absolute peach! Sizzling yorker that seems to start on middle and leg stump before swerving away at the last moment. Abhishek is completely flummoxed by it and can't lay bat on it as the ball flattens off stump! What a special delivery!

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: MA Agarwal
commentary: holes out to long off! Flighted devilishly on a full length on off stump. Mayank looks to smash it into next week but is beaten in the flight. Like Phillips, he has to reach out for it and toe-ends it miles up into the air towards long off. Buttler settles under it and completes the catch comfortably!

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: Washington Sundar
commentary: straight up and taken by Hetmyer! Back of a length delivery on middle and leg stump that climbs onto Sundar. He has a hack across the line but because the ball gets big on him, he can only spoon it towards Hetmyer at extra cover! SRH in all sorts of trouble now!

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: Abdul Samad

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: B Kumar
commentary: cleans him up! Googly on a length on middle and off stump. Kumar has no clue about it and looks to crash it through the off side. The ball rips back into him, cuts him in half and then disturbs the furniture! RR just two wickets away from victory now!

name: RG Sharma
commentary: The drop doesn't prove costly. Was a length ball outside off and Rohit pushed at it without moving his feet. The ball seamed away to take the outside edge. Karthik accepted it gleefully

name: Ishan Kishan
commentary: taken at deep third. That's the pressure of two dot balls. Was a length ball at the stumps and Kishan looked to pick it up over square leg only to get an outside edge that flew to Harshal

name: SA Yadav
commentary: short of length outside off. Surya goes for the cut but ends up hitting it straight to Shahbaz at backward point. It was at an awkward height but Shahbaz leapt in the air and took it comfortably

name: N Wadhera
commentary: third time unlucky. Full on the stumps, Wadhera goes for another big hit but fails to get under it this time. Ends up hitting straight towards Kohli at long-on

name: TH David
commentary: huge, huge wicket. Full on the stumps. David goes for the slog sweep but misses it altogether. The ball clatters into the stumps

name: F du Plessis
commentary: Slower length ball that is ballooned straight up and David ain't dropping those! Angled across Faf, who is not able to get the pace off the surface. Loses his shape and ends up lofting the ball to long-on.

name: DP Conway
commentary: Krunal and Wood team up to put a leash on Conway! Short ball on the stumps, hustled for pace trying to pull over midwicket, it hangs up in the air long enough for Krunal to charge in off the rope covering 20 yards before putting in a diving effort to latch onto this at ankle height. Superb effort!

name: S Dube
commentary: googly flighted full on leg stump giving it more air again at 93 kph, Dube goes for another booming slog and top edges this spinning away from him with the ball swirling off the bat toward short third man. Very basic catch taken by Wood this time on the ring waiting for the ball to come down at head height.

name: MM Ali
commentary: Moeen charges a good length ball and keeps running to the dugout. Nowhere near the pitch of this googly on middle and it spins past Moeen's heave as well as off stump for a simple stumping by Pooran.

name: RA Jadeja
commentary: length ball outside leg stump at 143 kph, Jadeja goes to pull and skies this to deep midwicket. Simple catch taken by Bishnoi jogging in from the rope before settling on his knees 10 yards in for a catch on his haunches.

name: MS Dhoni
commentary: Dhoni's innings is short and sweet! Length ball wide outside off on the guideline, tries to repeat his first ball slash over third man for six but comes off the toe and swirls to Bishnoi coming in off the sweeper rope for his second catch of the over, taken on his knees 10 yards in from the rope.

name: KL Rahul
commentary: fullish length ball on middle, Rahul slog sweeps this to wide midwicket. Doesn't come close to getting enough juice on the shot and Gaikwad ranges to his left to take this 15 yards inside the rope at rib height.

name: KR Mayers
commentary: full on off, slog sweeps this flat but picks out deep midwicket five yards inside the rope at head height. A ferocious half-century comes to an abrupt end. Conway hardly had to move for the catch.

name: DJ Hooda
commentary: fullish length on fifth stump, tries to slog this over wide long-on but Stokes covers 15 yards to his right, settles and reaches up to snatch this at head height two yards in from the rope. If he wasn't there it was most likely landing just over the rope for six.

name: KH Pandya
commentary: fullish length on fifth stump, charges and lofts a drive down the ground but doesn't middle it that well, comes off more of the inner half of the bat and picks out Jadeja two yards inside the rope at head height for a basic catch.

name: MP Stoinis
commentary: bullseye! Good length ball on the stumps, Stoinis backs away from leg trying to heave over wide long-on and misses. The ball spins in and leg stump is knocked back just under the bails.

name: N Pooran
commentary: goes for wide yorker again, doesn't get it full enough and Pooran smokes a blistering drive flat to wide long-off. However, he doesn't get the elevation on it. Stokes on the boundary comes in initially before going back and takes a head height catch two yards in from the rope. If he's not there, it's thudding into the ads for six.

name: A Badoni
commentary: full on middle, skies a drive high in the air swirling wide of Dhoni's right. He shuffles over toward the edge of the ring at short third to take a simple catch.

name: MR Marsh
commentary: Cleans him up, and Shami has his second! Length ball with a bit of shape away from the batter. He looks to push through the off-side, without any foot movement and is caught on the crease. Can only manage a thick inside-edge as the ball rattles his off and middle poles.

name: SN Khan
commentary: Goes for a sweep again, gets a top-edge and Little makes no mistake at deep backward square leg. Slightly slower through the air, outside off. He lines up for that sweep, but can only mistime it this time around. The ball goes high up and Little, I think misjudged the catch initially, but held on.

name: Abishek Porel
commentary: Takes two balls to create an impact! What a bowler, what a cricketer! Fuller, bowled at 94.3kph. Porel plays all around it, and sees his stumps in a mess. Was nowhere near it. Lost his balance, lost his footwork. Can't keep Rashid out of the game for too long.

name: AR Patel
commentary: On a length outside off and Axar hits it straight to David Miller at deep cover. Was in Axar's slot, but he was a touch legside and slightly off-balanced when looking to cream that away. Does not get enough connection and Miller gobbles it up.

name: Shubman Gill
commentary: Jaffa! Nortje is well and truly making his presence in the IPL! It is a fireball at 148.8 kph. Gill, even in the excellent form that he is in, has no answer to that. It was speared in on a length around off, jags back in a touch. Gill was on the back foot and looks to defend but is beaten by the movement and is knocked over.

name: HH Pandya
commentary: And Khaleel gets the big fish Pandya! This is what Capitals wanted from Khaleel and he has delivered. Lands it on a length and gets it to come back in to off. Pandya is forced to play at that and pushes at it a touch. Ends up getting a thick outside edge and Porel has his first catch in IPL cricket.

name: V Shankar
commentary: Hugeeee appeal, the umpire stays unmoved again! Marsh cannot believe it and asks Warmer to review it. It is the slower one bowled full on middle and leg, Vijay is done in by the lack of pace and is early into his flick and misses the ball. Is hit what seems to be adjacent. The ball tracker confirms the same - three reds! The Arun Jaitley Stadium has just found its voice back.

name: Prabhsimran Singh
commentary: Buttler with a sensational diving catch diving forward after charging in from long-off! Length ball on the wide guideline, Prabhsimran tries to pull this to the leg side but it comes off the toe and pops up over mid-off. Buttler has 25+ yards to cover but never hesitates and makes it almost to the edge of the circle before launching himself forward and pulling off the catch at ankle height. 10/10 on the judge's scorecards by the panel from Russia, Brazili, New Zealand, Lesotho, Panama and Iceland.

name: JM Sharma
commentary: low full toss on fifth stump, charges and tries to slap this over long-off but it comes too low off the bat at ankle height and hangs up for an easy catch taken by Riyan Parag 10 yards in from the rope at chest height.

name: M Shahrukh Khan
commentary: full in the slot slanted across eighth stump, Shahrukh goes to drive this over long-on but comes more off the toe. Buttler puts in another brilliant effort charging in from the rope 20 yards. The previous catch he took diving head first. This time he slides feet first to get his body under control and takes a knee height catch in front of his chest. Technically very different from his first approach, but equally flawless.

name: YBK Jaiswal
shortText: c sub (MW Short) b Arshdeep Singh
commentary: fullish length ball on sixth stump, drives loosely to extra cover. Basic catch taken at waist height. Fielder didn't have to move left or right. Matthew Short, on as a sub for Rajapaksa who was injured batting, takes the catch sliding forward on his haunches in front of his chest.

name: R Ashwin
shortText: c S Dhawan b Arshdeep Singh
commentary: the Ashwin experiment blows up, but not in the way that Rajasthan were hoping. Length ball on fifth stump, tries to pull this over mid-on but the ball climbs on him and produces a top-edge that floats in the air behind mid-on. Shikhar backpedals 10 yards before taking a catch at chest height.

name: JC Buttler
commentary: somewhat bizarre caught and bowled, but very athletic from Ellis! Good length ball on fourth stump, goes to drive straight and an inside edge comes off the pads and balloons up over short/silly mid-off. Ellis charges forward to corral a sliding take at knee height.

name: SV Samson
commentary: full on fourth stump, tries to muscle this flat over long-off but picks out Matthew Short five yards inside the ring to take a catch at head height. Aussie tag team produces a wicket.

name: R Parag
commentary: full pace cross-seam delivery in the slot on fifth stump, Parag tries to launch this over long-off but skews it off the splice and it skies toward long-off. Shahrukh Khan comes in 20 yards off the rope and has to wait an eternity for the ball to come down before taking a simple catch.
