name: SO Hetmyer
commentary: yorker is spot on fifth stump, jammed out to long-off for one. Hetmyer turns back for a suicide two, but Jurel didn't get the memo and stands pat for one. Hetmyer wanted the strike and winds up near the striker's end before giving up. The relay comes back to Curran, who takes the bails off disdainfully.

name: Rahmanullah Gurbaz
commentary: that is one way to get out. Almost nail the reverse-sweep but find short third with it. This is on a length, outside off, and he middles it with the reverse. Except that he has played it finer than he wants to. Straight to short third

name: VR Iyer
commentary: timber. This has jagged back in to hit the middle stump. He has missed this by a long way. Willey stays on a length, just outside off, Iyer likes to play off the back foot, and tries to muscle it through the off side. The seam movement beats him by six inches at least

name: N Rana
commentary: big appeal for a catch as Rana reverse-sweeps first ball. Not given on the field, and they review after much discussion. Replays show this has come straight off the glove. Rana shapes up fort the shot early, Bracewell gets some bounce to go past the top of the bat and take the glove to lob up for catch for Karthik

name: AD Russell
commentary: and his stay is short. Just one ball long. Russell is looking for a six first ball, and he gets a full enough ball, but he has not been able to connect properly at all. Easy catch for long-off, well off the boundary line. Not even close to a six

name: SN Thakur
commentary: finally Thakur finds a fielder. Siraj also bowls to his field for a change. Wide bouncer with the mid-off up. Thakur goes for the pull but there is no way to control it from that height and that line. Gets a big top edge to long-on. That ends a sensational innings from Thakur

name: V Kohli
commentary: gone, the off stump is plucked! Eden is up in celebration. Kohli went to play across the line, but missed this fullish ball which pitched just outside off. It was flicked from the front of the hand, and turned in nicely enough for Narine to strike in his first over. RCB lose an early wicket in a big chase

name: F du Plessis
commentary: another mystery spinner strikes for KKR! Now du Plessis' turn to be cleaned up. Varun tosses it up a bit, and goes on a slightly shorter side of a length outside off. That was bowled from back of the hand, and hinted to turn in, as du Plessis went for an expansive drive but got an inside edge. RCB's chase gets derailed just after they had started well

name: HV Patel
commentary: another inside edge, and bowled him again! That was quicker from Varun too, and was another googly. That is now four bowleds. Bowls from the back of the hand - and the googly - on a good length outside off. Harshal goes pushing hard, as the turn in finds the gap between his bat and pad. This time it is the middle and leg stumps that are disturbed

name: Shahbaz Ahmed
commentary: picks deep point! That was actually well struck, as Shahbaz went reverse sweeping. The fielder in the deep moved a few steps to right to gobble that. Narine went much fuller this time, angling it into the left-hander, as the ball ended on off stump. Shahbaz rolled his wrists and struck nicely, only to unfortunately find the fielder

name: Anuj Rawat
commentary: finds short third! Impact Sub gets his counterpart. Top-edged to the fielder there, as he saw this full delivery tossed up just outside off. It hinted to turn away - as Suyash had good revs on the ball - with Rawat going for the big heave across the line. But he got a top edge, and found the fielder

name: Akash Deep
commentary: very good catch by Varun! He ended up where an imaginary midwicket fielder would have been, diving forward to complete the catch. There were two others rushing towards the ball, but Varun kept his eyes on it, not getting distracted by Gurbaz reaching so close to his left. The ball was flirted up right into the clouds, as Deep went heaving at this full ball, only to get a top edge. KKR win by 81 runs

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: Anmolpreet Singh
commentary: An appeal for lbw and given out. Faster one - 98.6kph - on middle and leg. Anmolpreet was looking to work it on the leg side but gets beaten and pinged on the back leg. Just above knee roll. He has once again reviewed it and the ball-tracking shows it was just clipping the leg stump. So it's umpire's call and Anmolpreet will have to walk back

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: MA Agarwal
commentary: taken at extra cover. On a good length outside off, and Mayank went for the drive on the up. Ended up hitting it in the air. Stoinis leapt and took it overhead with both hands

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: AK Markram
commentary: and he has been bowled first ball. Was fired in around off, fuller than good length. Markram went for the drive but the ball spins away just enough to beat the bat and hit the outer half of the off stump. SRH in trouble, and Krunal will be on a hat-trick at the start of his next over

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: Washington Sundar
commentary: tossed up on the stumps, looked like a googly. Washington's eyes lit up as he looks to clear long-on. Not enough power behind that and Hooda takes at the boundary line

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: Abdul Samad

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: B Kumar

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: KR Mayers
commentary: taken at deep square leg! Some relief for SRH finally. It's a huge boundary there, and Mayers found the only fielder in the deep on the leg side. It was the third pull shot of the over from Mayers, who saw another good length delivery fired at him. He pulled it hard and flat too, but the boundary was too big for the ball to land beyond it

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: KL Rahul
commentary: given out leg before wicket, as Rahul misses his reverse sweep! That looked plumb to the naked eye, but hang on... Rahul has reviewed even as he is walking back. Length ball ball flicked from the front of the hand on leg stump, as it turned away to end around middle. Rahul reverse swept but swung his bat early, with the ball passing his bottom edge. No bat involved there, as he is struck on what seems to be middle stump. Yup, that would have smacked the middle stump, and SRH get a consolation wicket

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: DJ Hooda
commentary: great catch by Bhuvi! No reaction at all from the bowler despite nabbing it one-handed to his left. Bhuvi had to tumble to his left to grab that low, as he stuck his left hand out. He bowled what seemed like a legcutter on a good length on off, with Hooda jumping out again. Hooda ended up fending at it, and giving Bhuvi a catch. SRH fighting here

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: KH Pandya
commentary: outside edge, and the keeper takes it low to his left! Good length ball angled into the left-hander from around the wicket, and was very slightly wide of off. Pandya looked to fence that away, but got an outside edge, as the 149kph ball seemed to rush on to him

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: MP Stoinis

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: R Shepherd
commentary: given out again, and reviewed again! Now this one was a big-turning googly after he got Rahul with a legspinner. This also looked like heading down leg, as Rashid bowls on a good length on off, only for the ball to end on leg stump. Shepherd was playing on off stump, with a massive gap between bat and pad, with the latter around leg. And guess what, it is umpire's call on clipping the top of leg. That was great turn there

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: N Pooran
YBK Jaiswal
commentary: it's a miracle! AKA, a wicket! Back of a length on middle, Jaiswal comes down the track trying to bully another cross bat shot over the leg side but cramps himself coming too close to the ball and this lobs up off a top edge over the middle of the pitch. Mukesh calls for it and takes a simple catch to end a rollicking opening stand.

name: JC Buttler
commentary: yorker on middle at 134 kph, jammed out back to the bowler. Mukesh fires back at Buttler for a runout chance at the stumps that misses. Warner is appealing for a catch believing this was not a bump ball. Indeed the replays show this was not jammed off into the ground but rather bounced first and came off the toe floating back to Mukesh. He juggled it initially before taking the catch on the rebound. It came back to Mukesh at rib height to his left and he stabbed at it initially to pop it further up into the air before taking it on a second effort. Rather than celebrating a catch though, he fired at the stumps for a runout. It was really Warner who appealed for the catch. Bizarre sequence but Buttler's innings finally ends.

name: SV Samson
commentary: floated full on sixth stump, skies this a mile high but well short of the long-on rope. Nortje has a much less adventurous effort required for this compared to the theatrical drop on the run on the Buttler chance earlier. Just waits for it to come down into his hands at neck height 15 yards in from the rope for a simple catch.

name: R Parag
commentary: bullseye! Full on off stump tailing in a touch. Parag heaves across the line and misses. Middle and off stump are split apart halfway up.

name: MK Pandey
commentary: full and swinging into middle, Pandey is hit low on the shins after he misses a flick and this is stone dead. Pandey turns and walks away before the umpire raises the finger. Warner has a chat with him to cajole him into using a review. Pandey's entire body language shows he knew he was very out. This is going to rub salt into the wound. No way was this missing the stumps. Indeed three full reds show up on ball-tracking with the ball crashing into leg stump after hitting him in front of middle. Delhi lose a review and Boult is on a hat-trick.

name: AR Patel
commentary: charges down the stumps to a ball dragged much wider outside off and with the length pulled back too. Axar gets nowhere remotely close to this and flails in vain missing badly. Simple stumping.

name: Abishek Porel
commentary: floated full on leg stump, slogs this out to deep midwicket but not much power behind the shot and it floats to Hetmyer coming in 10 yards off the rope, who takes the catch sliding forward on his knees.

name: A Nortje
commentary: bullseye! Slower length ball skids on low toward leg stump at 109 kph, Nortje flails awkwardly over the ball with his shot and the ball pings the outer half of leg stump 3/4 of the way up.

name: RG Sharma
commentary: Terrific delivery from Deshpande. He is pumped. Knocks over the off bail. On a back of a length, angling in, skidding in to breach the defences of Rohit. It straightens fairly late off the seam to square up Rohit and hit the top of off and middle. The bail landed about 5 yards short of the boundary at deep third, says Matt Roller from Wankhede

name: Ishan Kishan
commentary: Holes out to long-on. Jadeja strikes with his fourth ball. Digs it short, angling across off stump, Kishan dares to pull against the angle. He was aiming midwicket, but ends up mis-hitting it to Pretorius at long-on. Jadeja has been taken down by left-handers in the recent past, but this time he takes down Kishan at the Wankhede

name: SA Yadav
commentary: Dhoni thinks he has his man. Not given out caught behind. Dhoni calls for a review almost immediately. Has SKY nicked it? Pushed through flatter and quicker, skidding down the leg side. SKY shapes for a sweep. Seems like SKY has gloved this behind to Dhoni. UltraEdge detects a spike. Another low score for SKY

name: NT Tilak Varma
commentary: Jadeja has snagged another left-hander. Umpire Gaffney's finger goes up. Varma challenges the on-field out decision. Even quicker and fizzes off the surface, breaks in to beat the inside edge and strike the front pad below the knee-roll. It would've crashed into leg stump. Varma has to depart

name: TH David
commentary: 6,4,6,and out! Deshpande is pumped once again. He has David holing out to Rahane at deep midwicket. Offcutter into the pitch, Deshpande hides it away from David who fetches it and ends up dragging the ball to the outfielder

name: T Stubbs
commentary: Maiden IPL wicket for Sisi. Welcome to the IPL. He takes down his SEC team-mate. Excellent tag-team work at the boundary. Pretorius takes the overhead catch with both hands at the edge of the long-off boundary. He then loses his balance and topples beyond the boundary, but has the presence of mind to lob the ball back to Gaikwad who is in the field of play. Slower full ball from Magala, he takes it away from the swinging arc, Stubbs can only slice it in the air

name: DP Conway
commentary: Chopped-on and Mumbai Indians have struck early! Just what the hosts would have needed. A little bit late on the back of a length ball just outside off. He pushes at it tentatively and ends up inside-edging the ball back onto his stumps. That will get the crowd going.

name: AM Rahane
commentary: Oh, he has flogged that straight to long-on! A hint of turn for Chawla on the length ball outside off. Seems like the ball came a touch slower to Rahane, who was looking to loft it much straighter. Gets it off the bottom of the blade and Suryakumar Yadav makes no mistake coming in.

name: S Dube
commentary: Another chop-on and Dube is a goner! Gets that length ball to skid quickly off the surface, angling into the batter. He stays back looking for a cut, but the ball is quite close to his body. Ends up inside-edging back onto his stumps. Celebrates in his own style, does Kartikeya.

name: Shubman Gill
commentary: holes out to long on! Length delivery hung outside off stump, and it forces Gill to fetch it. He advances and tries to go over long on but because he has to reach out for it, he ends up chalking it. Umesh settles himself under the high ball at long on, and does the rest!

name: B Sai Sudharsan
commentary: and Sudharsan perishes in that pursuit! Back of a length delivery outside off stump that hurries onto Sudharsan. He comes down the track and looks to clear long off but does not get the power or the elevation. Arrows it straight to Anukul at long off, who makes no mistake!

name: A Manohar
commentary: what a ripper! Googly on a length on off stump. Manohar does not quite pick it and goes searching for it. He leaves a gap between bat and pad, and then sees the ball rip through that gap. It kisses the outside of the front pad before smashing into the stumps! Suyash is over the moon, and why should he not be!

name: Rahmanullah Gurbaz
shortText: c Yash Dayal b Mohammed Shami
commentary: gloves it, and taken by Dayal! Back of a length delivery on leg stump that hurries onto Gurbaz. He has a swipe at it but is late on the stroke, meaning that he eventually gloves it. Bharat and Dayal go for the catch and run into each other, but the latter, somehow, manages to cling on diving forward! The communication system broke down there but Dayal did well to keep his eyes on the prize!

name: N Jagadeesan
commentary: holes out to deep square leg! Length delivery on middle and leg stump. Jagadeesan plants his front foot and gets decent contact on his flick. But he does not go through with his shot and ends up arrowing it straight down Manohar's throat at deep square leg!

name: VR Iyer
commentary: holes out to long on! Joseph breaks open the game again! Full and aimed at the batter's toes. It is not the yorker Joseph was looking for but it cramps Iyer for room just enough. He has a swing at it but can't generate the power or the elevation, hitting it straight down Gill's throat!

name: N Rana
commentary: and he sends Rana packing! Full delivery on middle and off stump. Rana plants his front foot and looks to go over mid off but does not display enough conviction. He has a half-hearted push at it and ends up chipping it straight to Shami at mid off!

name: AD Russell
shortText: c sub (†KS Bharat) b Rashid Khan
commentary: googly on a back of a length on leg stump. Russell has a massive heave at it and seems to miss as the ball pops up off the thigh pad and nestles into the keeper's gloves. Bharat, though, is convinced there is some bat involved and GT, in fact, review! Oh, hello! Looks like there is plenty of merit in that review. Russell has absolutely smashed that off the inside edge into his pad. And Rashid gets the better of Russell, just when his team needed him to!

name: SP Narine
shortText: c sub (J Yadav) b Rashid Khan
commentary: one brings two! Rashid has turned this game on its head! Length delivery on middle and off stump. Narine, sticking to his brief of attacking Rashid, has a mow across the line. He gets a good piece of it but it is not good enough to clear deep mid wicket. The fielder comfortably pouches it, a few yards inside the rope!

name: SN Thakur
commentary: hat-trick for Rashid! Oh my word! This is incredible! Googly on a length on middle and off stump. Just like the other day, Shardul does not pick the googly. Against RCB, he got an inside edge that saved him but today, he does not get any bat as the ball smashes into the pads, right in front of middle and off stump. The umpire raises his finger but Shardul wants to have another look! No bat involved, and three reds on Ball Tracking! Rashid has an IPL hat-trick, and GT inch closer to yet another victory!

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: Prabhsimran Singh
commentary: extraordinary finish to the first match and now an extraordinary start to this contest with a wicket first ball! Back of a length delivery nails Prabhsimran above the knee roll but stuck on the crease prodding down the wrong line. He's hit quite high but is deep enough for the umpire to uphold the lbw appeal and Prabhsimran curiously walks straight off without even consulting Shikhar about using a review. It looked quite high on the live shot and the replays too.

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: JM Sharma
commentary: fullish length ball on fourth stump at 140 kph, Jitesh tries to drive this on the up aiming to clear mid-off but it wasn't full enough for the shot and miscues this flat to the fielder five yards inside the ring at mid-off, Markram the captain taking a chest height catch with ease.

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: SM Curran
commentary: charges a fullish length ball floated wider again on seventh stump but Curran gets nowhere near the pitch and a googly spinning away results in a miscued drive sliced tamely in the air ballooning to short third man. Simple catch taken by Bhuvi shuffling to his left along the edge of the ring.

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: M Shahrukh Khan
commentary: googly spins in sharply after being flighted on a fullish length at 83 kph on fourth stump, Shahrukh prods down the wrong line and is struck below the knee in front of middle and off. LBW shout upheld. Shahrukh has a brief chat with Shikhar before calling for the review. The only chance he has of being saved is if this was spinning too sharply to miss leg. But ball-tracking shows it is clipping that outside of leg stump with umpire's call keeping it out. But Kings will not lose a review.

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: Harpreet Brar
commentary: bullseye! Length ball angled into middle stump at 148 kph, Harpreet is just not quick enough getting the bat down in time and this laser destroys middle and leg stump.

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: RD Chahar
commentary: full on fourth stump, shuffles across to sweep and misses, big lbw shout upheld. This looked iffy live. Chahar calls for a DRS review. Main doubt was whether he was hit in line with off stump or outside the line. He was hit low on the back thigh in the crouching position. Ball-tracking shows impact is "umpire's call" so the decision stays on field.

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: NT Ellis
commentary: drag on with a reverse sweep! Great day to be a leggie in the IPL. Googly flighted full on fourth stump spins in, Ellis crouches low trying to reverse paddle this behind point and it comes off the glove, then the thigh onto the stumps. Bizarre shot to play when Shikhar is begging for someone to hang with him.

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: Mohit Rathee

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: MA Agarwal
commentary: flighted on a fullish length on fifth stump, he has to reach a long way forward trying to slog this and winds up skying this a mile high toward Curran at long-on. Curran comes in a touch too far stepping in 10 yards from the rope before taking three steps back and swiveling sideways to take this at rib height before falling to the turf to soften the catch with gravity pulling it back sharply to earth.

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: AK Markram
V Kohli
commentary: finds deep midwicket! Mishra strikes in his first over. Kohli looked to pull this short ball, but didn't really get the ideal bounce off the pitch. Looked it was the wrong 'un, as it angled down middle and leg. Mishra breaks through for LSG, as RCB are checked

name: KR Mayers
commentary: Castled! It's the sweet sound of timber and Siraj is pumped! Bangs it in back of a length on middle, goes across this time but cramps him for room and Mayers chops on. He is the form player and that's a good early wicket for RCB

name: KL Rahul
commentary: And Siraj strikes immediately! KL Rahul picks out deep square! Full on leg and he looked to flick it away. Doesn't get the connection he wanted and it's straight to Kohli, who is thrilled after taking the catch. Rahul falls for 18 off 20 and I am sure he will come in for some slack after this knock!

name: DJ Hooda
commentary: Has he edged that? Dinesh Karthik thinks so and RCB review immediately. Full on middle and going across him, Hooda had a little push at it. He is walking even before the review is completed. And UltraEdge confirms that there was bat on that! Dinesh Karthik took a good low catch to his right and he knew straight away. Parnell has his first of the season!

name: KH Pandya
commentary: And the swing brings another wicket for Parnell! Pitches it up outside off again and this time Krunal gets the edge and DK makes no mistake! What a first over for Parnell!

name: MP Stoinis
commentary: Karn Sharma has his revenge! Brave of him to toss it up outside off again. Stoinis goes for the drive again but ends up slicing it up to deep point where Shahbaz makes no mistake despite a slight wobble! He is thrilled. Stoinis walks back having played a blinder here!

name: N Pooran
shortText: c Shahbaz Ahmed b Mohammed Siraj
commentary: Full toss on leg and Pooran picks out deep backward square! This will be checked for height on the full toss, though. And it will be an umpire review as it's a catch. Pooran looked to flick that away behind square, but spooned a catch to Shahbaz Ahmed in the deep. Ball-tracking shows that was fine and Pooran has to go! Siraj has got the key wicket of Pooran, but is this too late for RCB?

name: A Badoni
commentary: Badoni gets a six, but it won't count be cause his bat has crashed into the stumps! Badoni moves across to scoop this behind. Nailed the shot, but his right hand came off the bat and the toe-end just hit the stumps! Oh my! What drama

name: JD Unadkat
commentary: Holes out at long-on! Banged in short and Unadkat went for the pull. Picks up Faf du Plessis, who takes it reverse-cupped before switching it to a more normal hold

name: MK Pandey
commentary: down the track and lofted to long-off! Pandey took on the legbreak with a skip to the pitch, but he didn't quite get there, and the lanky Behrendorff was well placed back on the rope to end a promising knock

name: AR Patel
commentary: Behrendorff has Axar holing out to deep square. Too full and on the pads, Axar picks it up with his wrists but hits it straight to Arshad Khan. He goes for 54 off 25 balls. Terrific effort with the bat from Axar

name: Abishek Porel
commentary: down the track and holes out to Green at mid-off. Porel gives Benrendorff the charge. The left-arm seamer drags his length back and hits the bat as opposed to bat hitting ball. Spliced to Green who is right at the edge of the circle

name: Kuldeep Yadav
commentary: short and at the body, Kuldeep fends it into the leg side and takes off for a risky single. Wadhera swoops down on the ball from short midwicket and flips it back to the stumps at the non-striker's end. Sharp work from Wadhera

name: A Nortje
commentary: Meredith knocks out the leg stump. Nortje gives Meredith the charge once again. But Meredith nails the yorker and the stumps this time

name: RG Sharma
commentary: Whoa! Sensational catch from Porel. He throws himself to his right and comes out with the ball in his right hand. Given out caught behind. Rohit has asked for a review. Wide yorker from Mustafizur. Rohit jams his bat down on the ball. Not a bump ball. Ultra Edge confirms it. Superbly taken by Porel. Top decision from umpire Gough. Rohit throws his head back and drags himself off the field

name: Ishan Kishan
commentary: pressed into the off-side, and there's the breakthrough! Rohit wanted to get Ishan off-strike to the spinner, and he did so with greater emphasis than he had intended! Ishan was ball-watching as his skipper charged through, point gathered to fling to the non-striker's end and he's not even in the frame! Yadav gathered well, meeting the third umpire's approval.

name: NT Tilak Varma
commentary: pitched-up delivery on off stump, another slot delivery angling into Tilak. But this time he slices it off the toe end to Pandey at deep midwicket

name: SA Yadav
shortText: c Kuldeep Yadav b Mukesh Kumar
commentary: SKY falls for another first-ball duck. Caught at the edge of the fine-leg boundary. Short ball veering down the leg side, SKY picks it up and hits it straight to Kuldeep. This over started with 4,6,6. It ends with a double-strike

name: YBK Jaiswal
commentary: Deshpande strikes and Jaiswal is out of here! It's an excellent back of a length ball, angling away outside off. Jaiswal had his weight on the back foot and looks to heave it to the on side. Ends up getting it high on the bat and slices it towards mid-off where Dube makes no error.

name: JC Buttler
commentary: Buttler loses his off stump! Tosses it up generously and lands it on a length outside off. Dips enough to pass under Buttler's attempted swipe across the line. Uproots the off stump and Moeen has his sweet revenge.

name: SV Samson
commentary: Jaffa from Jadeja and Samson has to walk back! What a ball that. He bowls it on a length around off and middle and gets it to straighten just enough. There good bounce on that and Samson has no time to adjust and gets beaten on the outside comprehensively. The noise at Chepauk is deafening!

name: R Ashwin
commentary: And that's that for Ashwin with the bat tonight! Goes back and across to the length ball. He looks to heave it over mid-off but ends up skying it straight to Magala there. Magala hurt his finger while taking that.

name: DC Jurel
commentary: Another full toss, Akash ends up dismissing Jurel. He skies it high and long-off comes in to pouch that easily. The umpires want to check the height of the ball and it is well below the waist.

name: DP Conway
commentary: Conway departs and things are looking even tougher for CSK now! Tossed up outside off, Conway looks to go over cover but the angle meant he could not reach it properly. Ends up skewing it straight up as Jaiswal backtracks from extra cover and completes a well-judged catch.

name: AM Rahane
commentary: Loud, loud appeal as Rahane misses and is rapped on the pads! The umpire agrees and Ashwin is over the moon. Pre-meditated slog sweep from Rahane, but Ashwin pushed that carrom ball through and on middle and leg. Rahane misses and once that happened it was always going to be trouble. He does not opt for the review. Wasn't too impressed by his shot.

name: S Dube
commentary: Oh, what a bowler, Ashwin! Now, gets the one that fizzes quickly into the pads of Dube. He was just not expecting it and is caught plumb in front of the stumps. The umpire's finger is up in a flash and Dube does not review.

name: MM Ali
commentary: Oh, that's an excellent catch from Sandeep Sharma running across from deep backward square leg. Tossed up ball on middle stump. Moeen goes for the slog sweep, but does not get it anywhere close to the middle. I thought he had placed it well but Sandeep sprinted across so well.

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: Prabhsimran Singh
shortText: c Rashid Khan b Mohammed Shami
commentary: Shami gets a wicket second ball! Innocuous back of a length ball on the hips of Prabhsimran who was looking for a flick. But the ball hits the high part of his blade and lobs straight to Rashid at short midwicket, who takes a simple catch.

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: JM Sharma
commentary: Edged, is it? Saha appeals but does not get a lot of support. Pandya has taken a review in the nick of time. They are already a review down. Back of a length ball just on off that tails away from Jitesh just a touch. He pushes at it and the ball goes past his outside edge. Oh, hello. UltraEdge confirms there was a smidge of an edge on that. What a top review from Saha, who was the only appealing. The on-field umpire has to change his decision, and Kings are four down.

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: SM Curran
commentary: And, now Curran falls! Mohit gets his second wicket of the night. He goes into the pitch with the off-cutter shoulder high with the line outside off. Curran has to wait for it, and while he gets a good chunk on it, there is no pace. The ball goes straight to deep midwicket where Gill takes a simple catch.

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: M Shahrukh Khan
commentary: Fuller and outside off, he spanks it to the right of mid-off. There is a bit of misfield and Shahrukh goes for the second. But Miller throws a flat throw to the keeper who whips off the bails in a flash and finds Shahrukh short of his crease. Never run on a misfield as they say, and that has cost PBKS a wicket.

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: Harpreet Brar

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: Shubman Gill
commentary: Cleaned him up! Is this too late for a late twist? It's full on off and angles in just enough to hit off-stump as Gill swings and gets an inside edge. Late, late twist? Sign me up!

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: B Sai Sudharsan
shortText: c Prabhsimran Singh b Arshdeep Singh
commentary: And Arshdeep has the breakthrough! Bangs it in short on off, Sai Sudharsan swivels and pulls but gets a top edge and it goes straight to deep fine leg!

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: HH Pandya
commentary: Brar gets Hardik! He clears his front leg and wants to go big down the ground but doesn't quite time it. Picks out Sam Curran who takes it reverse-cupped. He lets out a little roar as the Titans skipper walks back

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: DA Miller

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: R Tewatia
MA Agarwal
commentary: and he picks up a wicket first ball. Was short of length around off. Agarwal was on the move, and was cramped. He looked to steer it away only to get an outside edge to Varun at short third

name: AK Markram
commentary: Fuller around off, goes on one knee to clear the deep midwicket boundary only to be caught by Russell near the boundary line. 72-run stand has been broken!

name: Abhishek Sharma
commentary: Length ball around off, Abhishek looks to go big but doesn't get the distant, Thakur runs in from long-on and completes the catch just outside the ring

name: Rahmanullah Gurbaz
commentary: taken at deep third. Full outside off, and Gurbaz looks to go over extra cover. But he fails to middle it and ends up slicing towards deep third where Umran Malik takes a straightforward catch

name: N Jagadeesan
commentary: taken at deep midwicket. Glenn Phillips, the substitute, with the catch. Was full on the stumps. Jagadeesan went for the slog sweep, connected it alright but ended up hitting straight to the fielder

name: VR Iyer
commentary: a soft dismissal. Length ball on the pads. Iyer looked to hit it over the leg side but ended up getting a leading edge towards long-on. Markram came in a few steps and pouched it

name: SP Narine
commentary: and he departs first ball. A length ball at the stumps, giving Narine no room to free arms. He still goes for the big hit but can only hit it in the air towards mid-on. Markram was again completes the dismissal

name: N Rana
commentary: Another full toss but this time it is straight to Washington at deep cover! Rana tries to clear the offside boundary and doesn't get the distance. What an innings this has been from the KKR captain!

name: AD Russell
commentary: Russell departs. A slider on the stumps. Russell backs away, clears his front leg, and aims for the moon. He gets a lot of height on it but not much distance. Jansen comes in from long-on and settles under it

name: SN Thakur
shortText: c Washington Sundar b Umran Malik
commentary: Gone! In the slot outside off, swings his bat, gets a top edge and Washington completes an easy catch at backward point.

name: V Kohli
commentary: of all the balls to get Kohli out.... it's a thigh high full toss! Kohli's eyes bulge out of their sockets as he tries to slaughter this over wide midwicket, but winds up splicing this a mile high before gravity pulls it back to earth into the hands of the fielder standing a yard inside the rope taking this at chest height.

name: F du Plessis
shortText: c Aman Hakim Khan b Marsh
commentary: sensational catch at midwicket! A hush comes over the crowd. Length ball on off stump, Faf whips this toward midwicket and Aman Khan leaps stabbing his right hand out and latches on initially before it pops up out of his hands but he keeps his cool to corral it on the second attempt while stumbling sideways.

name: MK Lomror
commentary: fullish length angled across the wide guideline, Lomror goes to drive and there's a definite noise as it passes the bat on the way to the keeper. Marsh appeals wildly but it is given not out. The keeper Porel doesn't really appeal. But Marsh sells his countryman Warner on using a review. Sure enough on UltraEdge, there's a clear spike. Decision overturned. Porel is getting stick from his teammates now wondering what was he listening to that he couldn't hear the nick.

name: HV Patel
commentary: wild heave to a good length ball on fourth stump, Harshal misses and loses his balance. Porel whips the bails off and runs off celebrating. He's certain this is out and it goes to the third umpire. Bizarrely this has been given out caught behind by Michael Gough on what was originally a stumping appeal. There are a bunch of random spikes on UltraEdge both before and after the ball passes the bat, in addition to a similar one as the ball is crossing the bat. There didn't seem to be any genuine differentiation between the sound waves registered through the entire sequence but the third umpire Gough felt there was enough evidence to give it out. This will be a talking point for sure...

name: MR Marsh
commentary: 139 kph length ball on leg stump, Marsh goes to swat this over the leg side. But it climbs on him and a top/leading edge swirls over cover. Kohli is on the ring and doesn't have to move. Kohli calls off the point fielder and takes it reaching in front of his body at waist height.

name: MK Pandey
commentary: googly spins in from fourth stump into the pads on middle and leg stump beating Pandey playing back. Big lbw appeal from Hasaranga, who is desperate for something to go his way today. Given not out. Not much support on the appeal from the keeper DK who is preoccupied chasing after the ball off the pads deflecting into cover. But it's DK who eventually signals to Faf to call for a review. Was it high, and was it spinning down leg? Nope! It was straight enough taking out the top of leg stump below the bails. Three reds light up on ball-tracking. Decision overturned.

name: Abishek Porel
commentary: slower ball comes in at 120 kph as a thigh high full toss on off stump, Porel is flummoxed by this and miscues a toe-slap aiming for midwicket but it pops up gently to mid-on. Parnell takes a simple catch at head height 10 yards behind the circle.

name: AR Patel
commentary: knuckle slower ball lands full on fifth stump, miscues a drive skying this high over extra cover in the ring. Siraj backpedals and gets his hands up to take this in front of his face.

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: KL Rahul
shortText: c sub (NT Ellis) b Arshdeep Singh
commentary: holes out to long on! Back of a length delivery on off stump that seems to hold in the surface just a tad. Rahul stands tall and tries to go over long on but does not get the timing he is after. Clinks it towards long on, where Ellis does the rest! A 74-run contribution from Rahul but the jury will be out on his strike rate (yet again)!

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: KR Mayers
shortText: c Harpreet Singh b Harpreet Brar
commentary: holes out to deep backward square leg! Dragged down on leg stump, and Mayers could have hit that literally anywhere. This one, though, just sticks in the surface a little and Mayers it early into the stroke. He toe-ends it straight down deep backward square leg's throat, who makes no mistake!

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: DJ Hooda
commentary: huge appeal, and that looks pretty adjacent! Back of a length delivery on middle and leg stump that skids through. Hooda opens up his stance and looks to go over cow corner but is beaten by the extra pace and the slight lack of bounce. The ball pins him on the back leg, right in front of the stumps and Hooda, rightly does not even bother with the review!

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: KH Pandya
commentary: taken excellently at deep mid wicket! Dug into the track outside off stump. Pandya looks to smash it over deep mid wicket but does not quite get it out of the middle. He hits it off the bottom part of the bat and it arrows towards Shahrukh on the deep mid wicket fence. He catches it and then realises that he will step on the boundary rope. Shows wonderful awareness to flick it up into the air, go outside the field of play and complete the catch after coming back!

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: N Pooran
commentary: Pooran departs for a duck! Back of a length delivery just outside off stump that seems to stick in the surface. Pooran tries to mow it over deep mid wicket but does not get hold of it enough. It skews off the bottom part of the bat and Shahrukh does the rest on the deep mid wicket fence!

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: MP Stoinis
commentary: back of a length delivery on leg stump. Stoinis shuffles across and looks to help it fine. He seems to miss it but Curran thinks there is bat or glove involved. He thinks about it before reviewing eventually! That looks pretty close to the glove, although it is very difficult to tell whether it has grazed the glove without UltraEdge. A massive spike as the ball is next to the glove, and Stoinis has to go! Excellent review by Curran, and PBKS have another!

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: A Badoni

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: Yudhvir Singh
commentary: and he perishes first ball too! Shahrukh with more nifty footwork on the fence! Back of a length delivery outside off stump. Yudhvir stands tall and flat-bats it towards long on. Shahrukh judges it to perfection, catches it, flicks it up into the air as he loses balance before coming back in and completing the catch!

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: Ravi Bishnoi

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: A Taide
shortText: c Avesh Khan b Yudhvir Singh
commentary: edged and taken at third man! What a start for Yudhvir and LSG! Back of a length delivery angled into middle and off stump. Taide looks to break free by swinging it over the leg side but is early into the stroke. The ball skews off the leading edge and flies down to third man, where Avesh completes the catch!

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: Prabhsimran Singh
commentary: oh dear, Prabhsimran can't catch a break, much like the person typing out these comments! Full and slanted into the batter just outside off stump. It pitches and decks back sharply, breezing past Prabhsimran's drive, and sending off stump cartwheeling!

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: Harpreet Singh
commentary: straight to deep backward square leg! Full delivery on leg stump. Bhatia gets inside the line and heaves at it. He has to reach out in front, though, and does not get the power or the elevation he desires. Arrows it straight to the sweeper, who dives forward and snaffles it up!

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: SM Curran
commentary: Curran bites the dust! Bishnoi with the breakthrough! Length delivery outside off stump. Curran looks to go downtown but this time, slices it just a touch because of the angle across him. It swirls down to long off, where Pandya settles himself and completes the catch!

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: JM Sharma
commentary: what a catch by Rahul! Salutes to the crowd, and Wood has broken this game open! Full delivery just outside off stump. Jitesh comes forward a little tentatively and has a waft at it. He does not middle it at all and the ball clinks off the toe-end towards mid off, where Rahul leaps low to his left and pulls off a sensational catch!

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: M Shahrukh Khan

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: Harpreet Brar
commentary: edged and taken! Too hot to handle this time! 147.9 kph thunderbolt that pitches on a length on leg stump and straightens ever so slightly. Brar continues swinging but does so without any foot movement, getting a feather through to the keeper!

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: K Rabada
Rahmanullah Gurbaz
commentary: class is permanant! Chawla tosses it up and Gurbaz's eyes light up. Goes for a big heave over cover but it takes an edge. Flies to short third, fielder takes a simple one. Gurbaz was starved of strike for most of the powerplay and now he's gone.

name: N Jagadeesan
commentary: terrific catch from Shokeen! Diving forward, always a chance of the ball popping out when your arms hit the floor. Green pitched it up, Jagadeesan drove on the up - a dangerous shot because of a packed off-side field - and Shokeen has dived forward from cover point to grab it. He looks confident but the umpire wants to go upstairs. There is no soft-signals in the IPL, so whatever the TV umpire says will be the final call. "Fingers under the ball," he declares... Jagadeesan out for a duck

name: VR Iyer
commentary: taken at short third by Jansen. Meredith went full around leg. Iyer turned his stance to play a reverse scoop over short third. Doesn't get the elevation. Venkatesh Iyer gone for a magnificent 104. An innings of pure intent.

name: N Rana
commentary: oh my word! Nitish Rana is out but someone has had a go at him and he has also said something back. Oh it is the bowler Shokeen who said something to Rana, and Rana - angry already with his dismissal - has given it back. Chawla and Suryakumar get in between to cool things down. As for the wicket, Rana tried to hammer this over the long-on fielder but he mistimed it. Oh Rana was very fired up there!

name: SN Thakur
commentary: full ball around off. Gets low and attempts the slog sweep. Tilak this time runs in, half-dives forward, and takes a catch rather low. Shardul begins to walk back and Tilak looks confident but the umpires want to look upstairs.

name: RG Sharma
commentary: Oh that's a screamer of a catch! This time it's Rohit Sharma who gets a leading edge and Umesh Yadav, who was beaten in the last ball, runs to his left and leaps to take it with both hands. This imperious opening stand is broken. The ball was tossed up full on off and Rohit pushed at it, getting the leading edge

name: Ishan Kishan
commentary: Castled! Goes for the slog sweep again, but this time he can only edge this full delivery onto his pads and then onto the stumps. He goes for 58 off 25. But he has given Mumbai Indians a great platform here

name: SA Yadav
commentary: Looks for the ramp but edges behind. Back of a length outside off and he looked to play it over the keeper. Gets a thin edge and it's a simple take for Gurbaz. But this has been a fine, fine knock from a batter in need for some runs and he has scored those runs at a rapid pace too

name: NT Tilak Varma
commentary: He has chopped on! Legbreak on a length on off, he went back to cut and gets an inside edge. That spun in sharply, cramping him for room. Good bowling by Suyash

name: N Wadhera
commentary: Digs it in back of a length on leg. It was going across and Wadhera tried to steer it. KKR have reviewed this. And they have reviewed it smartly! UltraEdge confirms that he got bat on it and Wadhera has to go

name: Shubman Gill
commentary: full on fifth stump, Gill pumps a drive firmly down the ground toward long-off but doesn't get the elevation and rocks his head back immediately in frustration seeing that he's picked out Buttler 10 yards in from the rope. Simple catch taken at thigh height.

name: B Sai Sudharsan
commentary: dropped! And a near runout chance! In fact, not just a chance but a wicket! Charges a full ball on middle and tries to drive this over Zampa, but a tremendous athletic effort from the bowler leaping up one-handed knocks this toward mid-off. The batters try to take a single off the palmed effort but Buttler is extremely alert reacting to the carom coming his way, charges forward to collect one-handed and fires a bullet on one bounce to Samson, who breaks the stumps with Sudharsan just short stretching for the single. Replays indicate though that Sudharsan was slowed down and potentially obstructed by Zampa struggling to run around him when leaving from the non-striker's end after the ball was deflected toward Buttler and cost him crucial distance trying to make the ground.

name: HH Pandya
commentary: full on off, Hardik goes to heave this over long-on but the leg break spins away enough to produce a top-edge swirling over extra cover. Jaiswal is having a torrid time getting his body into the right position backpedaling from the ring while fighting with the ball's swirling trajectory but gets his chest behind the ball at the last moment to hold onto a chance that looked like it was trying to burst back out of his hands.

name: DA Miller
commentary: goes for wide yorker and it turns into a shin high full toss on the guideline at 129 kph, Miller slices a thick edge trying to flay this over point and it skies to Hetmyer on the cover boundary where a catch is taken three yards inside the rope at chest height.

name: A Manohar
commentary: fullish length on sixth stump, Manohar tries to heave another drive over the leg side but the spin produces a top edge tailing toward cover on the ring. Simple catch for Padikkal taking a few steps back from the edge of the circle at head height.

name: Rashid Khan
commentary: slower bouncer on the wide guideline, Rashid flat bats this down the ground to long-on. They turn back initially for two before Tewatia decides he wants the strike for the final ball of the innings. Relay from Buttler comes in to Sandeep with Rashid stretching to get his bat back across the line. It's extremely tight. Will not matter in terms of a run or not since it's at the non-striker's end after one was completed. All that matters here is Rashid's average having another dismissal calculated into it or not. Plenty of time being used on this... Third umpire says Rashid is out. Hmmm... looked like he was in to me but it matters not.

name: YBK Jaiswal
commentary: absurd shot! Jaiswal opens the face to this 136 kph back of a length delivery on fifth stump and guides it straight to slip. Catching practice! Gill takes a basic catch at rib height reaching left across his body. That shot might have made sense with no slip in place but he was asking for trouble playing it in the second over.

name: JC Buttler
commentary: bullseye! Pressure bursts pipe! Buttler tries to break the shackles shuffling across his stumps to a 142 kph length delivery seaming in toward off stump and misses an attempted scoop over fine leg. Off stump is crushed three-quarters of the way up and ripped out of the ground.

name: SV Samson
commentary: maiden IPL wicket for Noor Ahmad! Flighted full on sixth stump, Samson goes for another almighty heave but doesn't come close to middling this one and it is skied off the toe well short of the long-off rope. Miller charges in 20 yards and takes a sliding catch at knee height about 10 yards short of the 30-yard ring at mid-off.

name: R Parag
commentary: goes for the orthodox legbreak full on fifth stump, goes to drive down the ground and it comes off the outer half of the bat. Miller reads it very well off the bat and charges in off the rope taking a catch diving forward at knee height.

name: DC Jurel
commentary: short on the stumps at 141 kph, goes to pull and a top edge swirls behind the keeper. Deep fine leg fielder Mohit Sharma charges 20 yards to his right, and nearly overruns the ball, before stopping his momentum and reaching back left across his body to take an adventurous catch at waist height five yards inside the rope.

name: R Ashwin
commentary: slower ball at 127 kph lands as a half-volley on seventh stump, Ashwin checks his shot trying to just open his wrists guiding this behind point but is not in control of this and it floats off a thick edge to Tewatia at head height on the ring at backward point.

name: DP Conway
commentary: dipping yorker. Harshal has been trying for this all night. And he's been getting closer and closer. Now he nails it. The ball starts out almost lobbing above the batter's eyeline, making him wind up for the big shot. But then 3/4th of the way down the pitch, it dips sharply. Conway isn't ready for it. He loses his shape. He's crouching, now trying just to get bat on it, instead of the six he was looking for. No dice. Beautiful stuff from Harshal. Bravo used to get this dip. This dip is the reason he was so hard to it and its the reason why Harshal is soo good at the death

name: AM Rahane
commentary: Hasaranga gives RCB the breakthrough. Hasaranga lobs up a wrong'un and gets it to break in towards off stump. Rahane swings for the hills but loses his shape this time and does not connect with the ball. Hasaranga hits off stump

name: S Dube
commentary: taken deep deep midwicket, inches from the rope. The on-pace ball really travels here in the Chinnaswamy. Even though it comes off close to the bottom of the bat, because Parnell bowled full and at the pads, Dube gets the ball to travel long. Siraj is on the boundary. He's so cool too. He knows he's in the game. He knows if he stays calm he has a superb catch. And that's what happens.

name: RA Jadeja
commentary: Jadeja steps out to Maxwell once again, this time he holes out to long-on. Jadeja doesn't get close enough to the pitch, and finds Prabhudessai, who is subbing for Faf

name: V Kohli
commentary: That's a novelty drag on! Akash is jumping in celebration. Kohli had made ample room and tried to whip the length ball on leg through midwicket. Got an underedge onto the boot. It deflected onto the front leg and then slid off the back boot, and gently rolled onto the stump. What an unusual way to get out on a belter!

name: F du Plessis
commentary: Top edge, another skier and another catch for Dhoni! Both set batters out, CSK back in the game. Was a length ball at the stumps, Du Plessis went for the slog sweep. Got an edge, went high, just to the left of the leg side of the cut strip. Dhoni came in, waited, reached out a bit and took it

name: MK Lomror
commentary: Dolly for Gaikwad at cover point, Deshpande joins among the wickets. Lomror goes. It was a shortish ball outside off. Lomrom made room and charged, tried to crash it over point but got a top edge off the cut.

name: Shahbaz Ahmed
commentary: Shahbaz slashes, it's in the air and Gaikwad takes it this time at long-off. Was a full ball outside off. Shahbaz went across and tried to smash it but the bat turned in the hand. Ruturaj took it low, threw the ball in the air. Dhoni has a word with his bowler

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: RG Sharma
commentary: clear signs the ball is holding up. Has happened the second time this over. Sharma is looking to flick it into the leg side, and gets a leading edge to mid-off. Another breezy RGS innings but he would love for one of these to go on longer. Replays show Natarajan has bowled the slower offcutter. So well done, TN

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: Ishan Kishan
commentary: and he has taken a wicket first ball. Superb catch from Markram. Jansen sees Kishan is charging at him because the mid-off is up, and he goes into the pitch, draws the edge with the extra bounce, but it is sailing over wide mid-off. Markram runs back, sees it drop over his shoulder, and then puts in a dive to claim the catch. Excellent effort

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: SA Yadav
commentary: Markram is a catching factory. Two excellent catches in one over. Cutter from Jansen, Surya ends up checking his drive, lobbing it wide of mid-off, and Markram runs to his left before diving full length to take this

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: NT Tilak Varma
commentary: clever bowling from Bhevneshwar. A third knuckle ball in the over but this time he goes shorter. Varma is advancing at him but the angle takes the ball away, and he toe ends it to deep cover. A stunning cameo on a difficult pitch

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: TH David
commentary: nails the yorker, and I am glad to see them try a second even though they have hit it straight to deep midwicket. Doesn't make it, but I am perplexed why other batters don't try it

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: MA Agarwal
commentary: another catch for David. Meredith misses the yorker, but the low full toss is good enough, especially when it follows Agarwal and takes his arms away from him. He can manage to send it to long-on for an overhead catch, which David smartly takes

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: AK Markram
commentary: Green is turning everything to gold. Markram clears the front leg and slogs, but I suspect it is the slowness off the surface that draws the miscue. Toe-ended to deep midwicket. Huge wicket in this SRH line-up

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: Abhishek Sharma
commentary: seems to have picked the wrong'un but still doesn't get enough power in the shot. He is possibly looking to go over cover, but the googly doesn't turn that much, and takes the inner half of the bat for a simple catch to long-off

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: H Klaasen
commentary: he looks to hit a six off the back foot. It is a wrong'un, which he has read here, but again it comes slowly off the surface and takes the toe end of the bat. David runs to his left and forward from long-on to take a well-judged overhead catch

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: Abdul Samad
commentary: wide yorker again, toe-ended to deep point, and he runs himself out taking a non-existent second

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: M Jansen
commentary: another catch for David. Meredith sees the yorker has a small margin for error, and goes short and slow. Jansen has no choice but to go four a boundary every ball. Ends up skying it to long-on

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: Washington Sundar
commentary: lazy running. Two yorkers in one ball. Behrendorff bowls one, and Washington digs it out for what looks like a comfortable single to mid-off. David lets a throw rip, catches the stumps on the yorker, and Washington is casual. Not even stretching his arm fully let alone diving

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: B Kumar
commentary: first wicket for Tendulkar in the IPL. SRH bowled out. Misses the yorker by inches, Bhuvneshwar tries to go down the ground, but gets an outside edge to extra cover. What a moment for Tendulkar jr.

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: M Markande
KL Rahul
commentary: straight to long on! Full delivery just outside off stump. Rahul plants his front foot and looks to go downtown. At the last moment, though, he seems to hold back and does not go through with his bat swing. Gets decent contact but because he checked his stroke, he can only arrow it straight down Buttler's throat!

name: KR Mayers
commentary: Ashwin has two! Length delivery fired into middle and off stump. Mayers does not go forward neither does he go back and is stranded in his crease. He has a stab at it but the ball breezes past the bat before smashing into middle and off stump! Ashwin is elated, and LSG are sliding further into trouble!

name: A Badoni
commentary: leg stump flat on the ground! Length delivery outside off stump. Badoni walks across his stumps and takes the cheeky option. Gets into position for the scoop but only ends up deflecting it straight into leg stump! Boult has his first, and the Badoni experiment is cut short!

name: DJ Hooda
commentary: taken by Hetmyer on the fence! Flighted slightly on a length on leg stump from around the wicket, and Hooda is enticed into a massive slog. He swings mightily but does not get the contact he desires. It goes miles up into the air towards deep square leg, where Hetmyer covers some ground to his right before holding onto the chance!

name: MP Stoinis
commentary: edged and taken! Short and wide outside off stump, and this seems to take Stoinis by surprise. He has a hack across the line but Stoinis can only feather it through to the keeper. Sandeep is elated, and he should be! He has bowled a brilliant over so far!

name: N Pooran
commentary: bulls-eye! Length delivery just outside off stump. Pandya swings at it and misses comfortably. Pooran, though, calls him through for the bye. Samson does well to gather it on the bounce and with the glove on, shies at the batter's end, catching Pooran well short of his ground!

name: Yudhvir Singh
commentary: length delivery on off stump. Yudhvir swings at it and shanks it towards deep square leg. They go for the second because, well, why not, and Yudhvir is at least a couple of yards short at the batter's end!

name: YBK Jaiswal
commentary: taken at short third man! Back of a length delivery outside off stump. Jaiswal uses the pace and tries to guide it past short third man. He makes decent contact but hits it straight to Avesh, who swoops down low and completes the catch. He might have hurt his hand in the process, though, but LSG will not mind this breakthrough one bit!

name: JC Buttler
commentary: holes out to deep mid wicket! Oh my! Back of a length delivery on middle and leg stump that sticks in the surface just a shade. He tries to muscle it over deep mid wicket but does not get the power or the elevation he is after. Shanks it towards deep mid wicket, where Bishnoi does the rest!

name: SV Samson
commentary: Samson has been run out! Oh dear! Length delivery angled into the stumps. Buttler turns it towards short fine leg, and then hesitates. Samson eventually decides to run through for the single and dives to make his ground. The throw, though, is accurate enough and catches Samson at least a yard short of his crease! Massive, massive moment in the game!

name: SO Hetmyer
commentary: and he sends Hetmyer packing! Full delivery angled across the batter just outside off stump. Hetmyer looks to slog it over long on but is reaching out for it a shade. He is also slightly early into the stroke and toe-ends it towards long on, where Rahul does the rest! LSG are absolutely elated; RR sink further into trouble!

name: DC Jurel
commentary: taken expertly at long on by Hooda! Full delivery just outside off stump. Jurel sits deep in his crease and clubs it towards long on. Hooda judges it to perfection, times his jump beautifully and then holds onto the chance. Excellent balance on display too, and RR are on the brink now!

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: V Kohli
commentary: is that out? That was a brilliant take either way by Jitesh! Full and darted into middle and leg stump. Kohli gets across his stumps and looks for the paddle. He seems to get it right off the toe-end and the ball spoons up into the on side. Jitesh, anticipating the shot, is already moving in that direction and dives to his left to complete an excellent catch! Kohli is gone for 59 but those runs came off 47 balls, which will evoke plenty of discussion!

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: F du Plessis
commentary: holes out to long off! Length delivery outside off stump. du Plessis looks to go inside out but does not quite get the timing he is after. He slices it off the bottom part of the bat and Curran does the rest on the fence. Massive, massive wicket in the context of the game!

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: MK Lomror

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: Shahbaz Ahmed

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: A Taide
commentary: huge appeal, not given! Full delivery on middle and leg stump that shapes into the batter late. Taide falls over a touch and seems to play all around it as the ball strikes the pads. The umpire says no but RCB review. No bat involved. Is it going over the top, though? No, it is not! Three reds, and Taide has to go!

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: Prabhsimran Singh
commentary: off stump out of the ground! Full delivery angled into off stump. Prabhsimran advances and looks to carve it over the off side. This time, though, he does not make any contact and the ball breezes past the bat before sending off stump flying! Good innings by Prabhsimran and it has kept PBKS in the game, although his team would have wanted him to carry on!

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: LS Livingstone
commentary: length delivery angled into middle and off stump. Livingstone charges, hacks across the line and misses as the ball pings into his pads.RCB have a long discussion before Kohli, Karthik and Siraj decide to go upstairs! That could be a lot closer than it first looked. No bat involved but Livingstone is way down the track. But it does not matter. Ball Tracking shows that the ball would have thundered into the top of leg stump, and Livingstone, despite being bewildered, has to go!

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: Harpreet Singh
commentary: Bhatia has been run out by Siraj! Oh dear! Back of a length delivery at the stumps. Prabhsimran is cramped for room and plonks it towards mid off. Bhatia, in the meantime, has wandered down the track, only to be sent back. That, though, gives Siraj enough time to take aim and throw down the stumps at the bowler's end!

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: SM Curran
commentary: Curran has been run out too! Yorker outside off stump. Curran digs it out into the off side and sets off for the single. He is a little lethargic in his running, though, and in fact, he is short of his ground as the fielder fires a direct hit at the bowler's end! That is the last thing Curran and PBKS needed at this stage

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: JM Sharma
commentary: taken at backward point! RCB win by 24 runs! Slower delivery on a length well outside off stump. Jitesh tries to reach out and looks to go over extra cover. He does not quite pick the variation, though, and swings too early, skewing it off the toe-end towards backward point. The fielder judges it well, dives forward and completes the catch!

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: M Shahrukh Khan
commentary: Shahrukh runs at Hasaranga, and gets stumped by a mile! Googly tossed up on a length just outside off stump. Shahrukh comes down the track and has a wild slog at it. He misses the ball by quite some distance and Karthik does the rest by whipping off the bails!

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: Harpreet Brar
commentary: Siraj gets Brar! Back of a length delivery on middle and off stump that skids through. Brar has a slog across the line and gets nowhere close to the ball as it smashes into the stumps! Siraj with a crucial breakthrough for RCB!

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: NT Ellis
commentary: cleans him up with a corker! Full delivery slanted into leg stump. Ellis is caught on the crease and has a hopeful waft at it. Gets nowhere close to the ball, though, and it sends leg stump cartwheeling! What a spell this has been from Siraj, and RCB are now just one wicket away from victory!

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: Arshdeep Singh
VR Iyer
commentary: edged to slip! It's Test match cricket at the Feroz Shah Kotla! Hits the pitch hard, climbing into a hard-handed drive, a fat edge, and a sharp take at a solitary wide slip from Marsh. Iyer makes a duck, and now KKR are under pressure

name: N Rana
commentary: climbs through the line and holes out to mid-on! No connection whatsoever on his launch down the ground, high on the bat and spooned to Mukesh, running back at the edge of the circle. Another wicket lost in the powerplay, and the pressure is now well and truly on

name: SP Narine
commentary: picks out deep midwicket! Narine comes crashing down too, as Sharma drags his length back, tempts the pull, but again finds enough flick through his wrist to confound those attacking intentions. Warner is inside the rope to pocket the chance, and well, it's time for Russell the Muscle now... KKR have no other options!

name: AS Roy
commentary: Roy is pinned lbw first-ball! It's been reviewed, but this looked mighty adjacent. And if so, this might be the biggest self-own in Impact Sub history... sure enough, it's clipping the top of the bails and off he goes!

name: CV Varun
commentary: swatted off the good length as Mukesh lets slip the slower ball. They set off for the second, as well they might, but Varun gets sent back.

name: MR Marsh
commentary: taken at long-off! Good catch by sub fielder Wiese, who almost lost balance on the boundary after overrunning to his right. The length was perhaps barely a bit too full on off for Marsh to try and belt that down the ground. In the end he did hit hard and flat, only for among the taller of the fielders to gobble it above his head

name: PD Salt
commentary: gifted back to the bowler! A simple catch to his left for Anukul, as Salt chipped back a full delivery going on with the arm on off, and on a full length. Looked like the ball held on to the pitch for once there, with the slowness coming into play

name: MK Pandey
commentary: lofted to long-on! Is there some late twist yet? The ball was tossed up, and full, going on with the angle from around the wicket. It ended around middle and off, and he ended up chipping to right into the fielder's lap

name: Abhishek Sharma
commentary: well taken by Rahane at long-on! He had to cover good ground while running forward. So the time-out breaks Abhishek's momentum. Short ball from Jadeja from around the wicket, pitching just outside off. That didn't really turn, even as Abhishek pulled from middle stump perhaps anticipating it. The ball hit the higher side of his bat, and ballooned to long-on

name: AK Markram
commentary: wow, what a faint tickle! Good take by Dhoni; he hardly had time to react. The ball sticks into his gloves as he leaps a little. It was a short ball pitching outside off, as Markram made room to try and slash it. He got his front leg out of the way too, but ended up getting a little top edge

name: H Klaasen
commentary: another good catch by Gaikwad! Again he dives forward - but this time at extra cover - like he had done to dismiss Brook earlier in the day. Klaasen got a length delivery slightly wide of off from Pathirana, as he saw the width and flashed hard at the ball. The ball ended up being aerial, with Gaikwad timing his dive perfectly

name: MA Agarwal
commentary: stumped by Dhoni! And for what it's worth, the third umpire first wants to confirm if it's a caught behind or a stumping. No bat detected, so Agarwal is out stumped. He ended wayyy down the pitch, skipping out early. Jadeja saw that, and fired one on a short of a length outside off, and also much quicker. Agarwal had to slash but missed, and Dhoni did the rest

name: Washington Sundar
commentary: run-out off the last ball! But it has gone to the third umpire, who confirms it is out. Dhoni takes his right glove off, and hits the stumps at his end to find Sundar short. Length ball heading down to end outside leg, as Jansen swung across the line and missed. Naturally, they took for a potential bye

name: AM Rahane
commentary: rewarded for a superb delivery! Rahane chips down the track but is beaten in flight and off the pitch as he attempts to work it to the leg side. The ball flies off the edge to Markram who leaps at slip to complete the catch on the second attempt.

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: Shubman Gill
commentary: duck for Gill. Skips out of the crease, seems to me he is looking to hit back over his head, but the turn takes the outer half of the bat for an easy catch to long-off. Early signs all point to a low-scoring match here

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: HH Pandya
commentary: slower ball, short into the pitch, Pandya has to go for everything, and this time he ends up hitting straight to long-on. Looking at how others have gone, this has been a good innings. Only man to hit a six so far

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: A Manohar
commentary: lures him, teases him, tortures him with a slow high legbreak. Manohar decides he will go for it. But these boundaries are not small. All the power has to be manufactured by the batter. Clearly he hasn't imparted enough of it although it does take a diving catch to his right from Naveen-ul-Haq at deep cover

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: V Shankar
commentary: Shankar has to take the risk now. He is 10 off 11. Death overs approaching. So he goes for a slog. Naveen stays on target, on a good length, at 131ks. Shankar misses, Naveen hits. His first IPL wicket

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: DA Miller
commentary: slower short ball again, Miller tries to give him the charge and muscle it down, but finds long-on. Just no pace to work with

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: R Tewatia

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: KL Rahul
commentary: and he has got out after turning an easy equation into a tough one. Slower short ball, into the pitch, cutting in, Rahul pulls, gets a top edge, an d is caught at deep square leg. Rahul looks distraught: 0 off the first 6, 30 off the next 12, 20 off the next 20, 18 off the next 23

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: KR Mayers
commentary: Rashid comes back. I think Mayers has played for the wrong'un, but this is a legbreak. He has left a big gap between the bat and the pad for Rashid to bowl him. Rashid is animated i

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: KH Pandya
commentary: walks past it. As expected, Pandya is looking to finish it off in a hurry. This time Noor shortens the length, gets enough turn to beat his outside edge as he looks to play this to leg, and Saha does the rest

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: N Pooran
commentary: Pooran is under pressure, and he perishes. This game is wide open now. Sees a full ball, looks for the slog sweep, but it turns away to take the toe end. Pandya asks Noor to get out of the way, and completes the catch. Clear calling

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: A Badoni
commentary: good from Mohit. He is not greedy. He knows the batters are expecting the slower ball, which is the wicket-taking delivery. He goes for the yorker, Hooda digs it out, they go for the second, and Badoni is run out. The second is never on

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: MP Stoinis
commentary: slower short ball. The lethal slower short ball when you are desperate for boundaries. He has to create all the power. Slaps it down the ground but imparts only enough to reach long-on. Mohit is on a hat-trick, and GT are the favourites now

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: DJ Hooda
commentary: Mohit is not focusing on the non-striker. He nails another yorker, Hooda digs it out to deep midwicket, and goes back for the non-existent second to keep the chances of a tie alive. The throw from the deep is spot on. He is gone. GT win if they don't bowl a no-ball or a wide

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: Ravi Bishnoi
Prabhsimran Singh
commentary: Yorker hits the boot, given LBW. Prabhsimran reviews. Did it hit bat though? The ball had the seam facing fine leg as it came in, hit the popping crease. The batter was pulling his foot back as he tried to nudge it towards midwicket. It went through the blockhole and into pad. It goes to ball tracking and it shows three reds. Arjun Tendulkar pumps his fists.

name: A Taide
commentary: Dragged onto the stumps off the elbow. Taide was on one knee and tried to pull the length ball and missed it. It hit pad and lobbed back, onto the bottom part of the elbow and nudged the stumps. Chawla was turning for the lbw appeal but realised he had Taide bowled. Two wickets in the over. Nice innings by Taide too.

name: LS Livingstone
commentary: Brilliant stumping from Kishan, a la Dinesh Karthik to Michael Vaughan in 2004. Was a legbreak that was flat. Chawla went quicker as he saw Liam charge. The ball beat the flick and went well to the left of the keeper. Kishan leapt across and dislodged the bails in one motion. He took the ball, his back leg was stretched back, facing fine leg but managed to push his hands, reach out and clip the bails.

name: Harpreet Singh
commentary: Yorker knocks over Harpreet . Underedge as he tries to guide it fine, both bails fly and he can't get to fifty. Harpreet was making room and Green stuck to the yorker, got it just outside off, the angle off the bat brought it back onto the stumps to end a 92-run stand.

name: SM Curran
commentary: Archer's raw pace is back, Curran's fending, ball takes the leading edge and Archer takes a return catch. Curran was making room but the shortish ball came crashing into his chest, he took his front hand off and the ball hit the sticker and popped up, Archer took the catch on the move. End of a brilliant 55 from Curran

name: JM Sharma
commentary: Jitesh misses, Jason hits middle stump and is pumped but the 7-ball 25 cameo has taken Kings over 200. Was a length ball angling in, Jitesh made room and heaved across, missed it.

name: Harpreet Brar
commentary: Brar runs back, pushes for two. Throw comes in, Kishan takes off the bails. Sent upstairs. Replays show he was just short. It was a full ball outside off at 130kph that was heaved to midwicket

name: RG Sharma
commentary: Spooned to the bowler's right and Livingstone takes a dolly. Legbreak lands around middle and turns away a bit, Rohit was trying to nudge it across but it popped up off the outside edge. The bowler ran across and took an easy catch

name: Ishan Kishan
commentary: Short runs across from deep-backward point and takes a catch on the move at the cover boundary. Arshdeep has his 50th wicket.

name: SA Yadav
commentary: Flick finds mdwicket. Low full toss, SKY went across and whipped it across, hit with power but didn't manage to Taide around head-height. Sharp catch to end a 26-ball 57. Brilliant over after conceding the six off the first ball. SKY gets a standing ovation from his team-mates, Arshdeep is delighted with the catch.

name: NT Tilak Varma
commentary: Middle stump broken! Came in with the angle, landed on a full length, Tilak looked to slog over midwicket. He missed it and the stump was broken one fourth of the way up. Arshdeep pumps the air, what commendable bowling under pressure!

name: N Wadhera
commentary: The new stump is broken as well, Arshdeep Singh is creating expenses of the right kind for Punjab Kings. Wadhera charged before the ball was delivered, missed the slog, broke the stumps despite the slight deflection off the pad.

name: V Kohli
commentary: why do we even bother asking Boult if he will pick a first-over wicket? He has snapped one first ball. Full, swinging in, on the target, past his inside edge. No discussion even for a review. Kohli walks off. Plumb as

name: F du Plessis
