commentary: Jaiswal manufactures a wicket here. Misses the yorker again. Low full toss drilled just to the right of extra cover. Jaiswal hits the stumps direct, and du Plessis is caught well short. he walks back with his bat raised

name: Shahbaz Ahmed
commentary: wicket with the first ball of the second over too. The shot is on, really. Short ball, angling in, midwicket off the 30-yard ring. All he has to do is pull over him, but ends up hitting it straight to Jaiswal stationed there

name: MK Lomror
commentary: legbreak, pushed through wide, Lomror gets more under it than behind it, and Padikkal takes a smart catch running in from long-off

name: PWH de Silva
commentary: slower bouncer, Hasaranga not awake to the bye to get Karthik on strike. Samson misses the direct hit at Karthik's end but Hasaranga is caught short on the non-striker's end. Called a wide for height

name: V Vyshak
commentary: that sounded sweet off the bat. Vyshak jumped out of the crease and swung this short-of-a-length ball into the leg side. For a moment I thought he had hit six. In actual fact, he is caught at deep midwicket

name: YBK Jaiswal
commentary: Jaiswal has picked out Virat Kohli in the deep! It's a dipping full toss on off and he has a swing. Kohli gets his 101st IPL catch as he reverse-cups it as the pressure tells on the Royals! Kohli blows a kiss to the crowd, who lap it up of course

name: JC Buttler
commentary: Ohhhh he has gone through him with a beauty! Buttler goes for a duck in the first over! Wobble-seam delivery on a length outside off and it seams in and goes through the gap as Buttler pushed away from his body

name: SV Samson
commentary: Hit straight to short third this time! Harshal is pumped and as he should be! It's sort and wide, angling in and Samson cuts hard. It flies to Shahbaz who takes it reverse-cupped in front of his face. An inch on either side of the fielder, that was four more, but instead Harshal has his second wicket of the day

name: SO Hetmyer
commentary: Direct hit! Prabhudessai had one stump to aim at from cover and he nails the throw at the non-striker's! Hetmyer never even stopped running as he knew he was short by a mile. He hit it to cover and ran off a length ball and Prabhudessai's brilliant throw means he is back in the dugout

name: R Ashwin
commentary: Picks out deep midwicket now! It's full outside off, Ashwin looks to swing it leg-side but doesn't hit it well enough. Prabhudessai makes no mistake and now it's 9 off 2 with Jurel off strike!

name: DP Conway
commentary: open-faced drill to long-off! It was in Conway's slot, and he timed it well enough, but his direction was awry and Wiese barely needed to move on the edge of the rope. A fine knock, but it's over too soon for CSK's liking

name: S Dube
commentary: the fun's over this time! Another firm strike through the line, planting the front foot, and crashing hard towards long-off... it was clearing the ropes, but it couldn't clear Roy, right back on the boundary's edge

name: RA Jadeja
commentary: another full toss, and holes out to deep square leg! It's the biggest roar of the night... because look who's padded up in the dug-out... but the umpire is checking for the no-ball... this could be scenes if Kolkata is denied its glimpse of Dhoni! Below waist height! Ecstacy in the stands!

name: N Jagadeesan
commentary: Flashed to square-ish third. Jadeja runs in, slides on his knees, and snags it coolly at deep third. On a length and swings away outside off, Jagadeesan stays leg-side of the ball and slices it in the air. Jadeja makes this catch look ridiculously easy. Deshpande also strikes in his first over

name: SP Narine
commentary: Swing and a miss. Swing and a miss. Castled next ball. Narine dashes out of the crease, swishes and misses the ball by a long way. Slanting in from left-arm over and pinging the top of off stump. KKR's top-order troubles continue

name: VR Iyer
commentary: and Moeen strikes first ball. The finger goes up. Iyer chats with his captain and asks for a review. Chants of "CSK CSK CSK!" ring around the Eden. Full and sliding in at off from round the stumps. A bit too full for the sweep. Ultra Edge doesn't detect any bat on ball. Flat line as ball passes bat. It would've crashed into middle stump

name: N Rana
commentary: Jadeja has been good against left-handers too this IPL. He has another one holing out. Gaikwad takes it at deep midwicket. Rushes onto Rana outside off. He can only drag the slog-sweep to Gaikwad

name: AD Russell
commentary: The young Sri Lankans are doing the tough job for CSK. Theekshana knocked over Roy. Pathirana now has Russell holing out. Dhoni puts his arm on Pathirana's shoulder and has a chat with him. This was the slower ball, he hides it away from the reach of Russell. He still has a hack at it and skies it to Dube at deep midwicket. Game over for KKR?

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: PD Salt
commentary: good length ball in the sixth stump channel at 132 kph, Salt pokes at this flat-footed and gets a healthy edge through to the keeper. Bizarre sequence as nobody really appeals behind the wicket and Bhuvi only gives a belated shout before it's given out. But it was an obvious edge. The stadium is loud tonight.

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: MR Marsh
commentary: good length ball swinging in on leg stump at 127 kph, Marsh defends down the wrong line and is hit above the knee roll. Big lbw shout denied as they scamper a leg bye with the ball trickling into cover. Aiden Markram signals fairly quickly for a review. Height was a major issue, not to mention whether or not it pitched in line. Replays show Marsh was hit above the pads on the back leg but his leg was flexing quite low. Ball-tracking shows the ball indeed pitched in line and was going to hit middle stump below the bails. Three reds light up. Decision overturned.

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: SN Khan
commentary: full on leg stump, another batter falls to the sweep! Sarfaraz was not in control of this. Tries to smash this along the ground but it floats off more of a top edge and sails to the deep backward square leg fielder for the right-hander. It's Bhuvneshwar out there who comes in five yards for a much simpler chance taken upright at thigh height.

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: MK Pandey
commentary: short on fourth stump, pulled to deep midwicket for one, fumble by the fielder opens the door for them to attempt a dicey second but Sundar recovers well enough to pick up on the second attempt and fire a bullet straight over the bails to Heinrich Klaassen with Rimal's diving effort for the second run to stay on strike coming up two feet short.

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: AR Patel
commentary: bullseye! 132 kph yorker on middle and off stump, Axar plays over the top of this trying to flick through midwicket and the ball ricochets off the toe into the stumps.

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: A Nortje
commentary: yorker on middle at 134 kph, flicked out to deep midwicket for a long single. Major confusion on whether or not to come back for a second taking on Umran Malik's arm. Nortje stopped for one, then belatedly took off after seeing Ripal charge halfway down the wicket for the second. But then Ripal stops and turns back leaving Nortje to be toast for the relay over the stumps to Klaasen. Bails taken off with Nortje five yards out.

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: Kuldeep Yadav

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: I Sharma

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: MA Agarwal
shortText: c Aman Hakim Khan b AR Patel
commentary: flighted more on a good length on middle, Agarwal charges and is beaten badly in flight, reaches a long way in front of his body to get bat on his but winds up shoveling this straight up to Aman at long-on. Simple catch taken 10 yards in from the rope at waist height.

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: Abhishek Sharma
commentary: charges a length ball but gets nowhere close to the pitch and punches with hard hands in front of his body at knee height straight back to Kuldeep at head head by more in line with his left shoulder. He reaches up to calmly take this return chance. It was hit firmly but not blistered by any stretch.

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: AK Markram
commentary: drag on! Good length ball skids on from fifth stump toward off, Markram goes back to cut but is cramped for room and a bottom edge cannons on to leg stump.

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: H Klaasen
shortText: c Aman Hakim Khan b Nortje
commentary: full and wide, Klaasen blisters a drive flat to the cover sweeper but picks him out too well. Sharp catch taken at knee height reaching forward five yards in from the rope after jogging 10 yards to his right.

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: Washington Sundar

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: M Jansen
Shubman Gill
commentary: Kartikeya gets the big wicket of Gill! Full on middle from wide of the crease, Gill goes downtown and like Pandya, he can't clear SKY at the boundary, but this time at long-on and well inside the rope

name: HH Pandya
commentary: wicket first ball for Chawla! He gets the captain out straightaway. Pitches it up fearlessly with long-on at the rope, Pandya goes after it with a big swing but he can't clear long-off. SKY leaps just inside the rope and takes it with both hands above his head. Well judged by him

name: V Shankar
commentary: Shankar holes out to long-on too! The Titans batters are perishing while trying to clear the straight boundary off the spinners. Shankar wants to clear long-on off the flighted and full ball, swing down, but can't clear David's bucket hands there

name: DA Miller
commentary: Too high off Miller's pull as Behrendorff pitches it slow again, Miller swings because that's the only way to go now, mistimes it, and SKY settles under it after coming inside from the rope

name: A Manohar
commentary: nobody gets it right and Manohar falls! Meredith doesn't get the yorker right and sends down a low full toss on the pads. On another day, Manohar would have smoked it over the ropes on the leg side but he decides to go inside out and down the ground, doesn't get much distance and finds long-off

name: RG Sharma
commentary: Hiiiiigh in the air and Hardik makes no mistake as he gets his opposite number. Rohit moves leg-side to this length ball on middle and leg. The ball is swinging in but straightens after pitching and Rohit jabs at it. The ball takes a leading edge and goes high in the air and with many fielders converging Hardik calls for it, settles under it and takes the catch

name: Ishan Kishan
commentary: Kishan's difficult stay comes to an end. Looks to go for a big heave and ends up skying it. Josh Little takes it above head height. It was high in the air and a tough catch, but he made it look simple. It was a wrong'un and dropped short but Kishan couldn't quite time it

name: NT Tilak Varma
commentary: Huge appeal from Rashid Khan as Varma presses forward to defend. This time Hardik Pandya opts for the review, and in some style! It's pitching full on leg and brushes the front pad before hitting the back pad. No bat on it. Ball-tracking shows the ball pitching just in line and hitting wickets. Rashid Khan has two in the over!

name: SA Yadav
commentary: Has he got him? Yes he has! On a length on off, SKY punches it straight back to him and Noor takes a good low catch! It was to his right and dipping on him and he dived in front with both hands to hold on to that

name: TH David
commentary: Mumbai Indians are falling like a house of cards! David goes for a huge swing across the line to a full toss and doesn't quite time it. The ball goes high in the air and Abhinav Manohar settles underneath it to send David packing for a two-ball duck

name: N Wadhera
commentary: Mohit Sharma gets a wicket now. Full outside off, Wadhera goes across to ramp it and picks out Shami at short fine. Mohit casually asks for the ball back. That's about as nonchalant a wicket celebration in a winning cause as I have seen as Wadhera walks back having made 40 off 21

name: AS Tendulkar
commentary: Picks out long-on now and that will be the end of Arjun Tendulkar's debut innings in the IPL. Full and wide and he reaches out and looks to muscle this away but didn't get a clean connection at all. It's a simple catch for Josh Little

name: N Jagadeesan
commentary: caught at deep midwicket. Vyshak bowls the bouncer, gets good height on it, Jagadeesan pulls, doesn't get all of it, and finds the deep midwicket

name: VR Iyer
commentary: and when Iyer tries the slog sweep, it turns out to be the wrong'un. Bowled two legbreaks when they were playing for the wrong'un, and now slips in the wrong'un. Goes a mile in the air, and Maxwell takes it at long-on. Once again turning the momentum around is Hasaranga

name: N Rana
commentary: finally they catch Rana. It is Vyshak who does it. Another reverse sweep but Hasaranga is bowling legbreaks and not wrong'uns. The top edge goes towards short third who dives forward to take the catch

name: AD Russell
commentary: lovely yorker speared into the leg stump. Russell is bowled as he clears the front leg to hit. Superb comeback from Siraj after going for 14 in the first three balls

name: V Kohli
commentary: is this the game? Digs one in short, and Kohli smacks a pull clean, only for Iyer to take a stunning catch at deep midwicket. This is travelling flat, Iyer dives to his left, the ball hits his hands high but he recovers to prevent it from wobbling out of the hands even as he rolls over

name: F du Plessis
commentary: and Suyash has the wicket. Wow what a challenge, and he has aced it. Wrong'un, flighted, and du Plessis makes room to go over long-on, but doesn't get all of it. A smart overhead catch by Rinku at the fence

name: Shahbaz Ahmed
commentary: that is out one of the two ways, and Shahbaz walks off. The fullest Suyash has bowled today, and Shahbaz is beaten on the sweep. He is dead plumb but just in case he has got an edge, it has lobbed up for a catch too. In the end he is out lbw. Replays show he got a bit of a feather, but it doesn't matter

name: MK Lomror
commentary: smart bowling. Varun pushes this wide on the line. Lomror has to drag this one. Gets just the toe end, and is he caught at the fence on deep midwicket. Another twist

name: PWH de Silva
commentary: wow it is KKR's day, it would appear. A slower ball, wide half-volley, Hasaranga goes down on a knee and lofts it with an open face, but he has found Anukul Roy at deep point to perfection

name: YBK Jaiswal
commentary: 129kph. A length ball outside off, was angling away from Jaiswal who is standing deep in the crease. He clears his front leg and looks to go aerial. Fails to middle it and Rahane settles under the swirler after taking a couple steps back at backward point

name: JC Buttler
commentary: Jadeja provides the breakthrough. Buttler uses his feet, looking to clear long-on but the bat face closes, resulting in a simple catch for Dube

name: SV Samson
commentary: and he strikes immediately. It was an offcutter at 126kph. A length ball around off that Samson looked to hit over long-on. Didn't middle it and Gaikwad takes an easy catch well inside the long-on boundary

name: SO Hetmyer
commentary: chopped on. Was a carrom ball, just outside off. Hetmyer went for the cut but there's hardly any room. Gets a bottom edge onto his stumps

name: DC Jurel
commentary: 151.4kph. Full but down the leg side. Padikkal fails to get his bat on it but they look to sneak in a bye. Dhoni nails the direct with an underarm throw to find Jurel well short

name: DP Conway
commentary: picks out mid-off! Conway just didn't look a part tonight. Got to the pitch and hit it flat but found Sandeep Sharma.

name: AM Rahane
commentary: Rahane has hit this straight down long-on's throat. He steps out to loft, but Ashwin bowls a drifter into him, Rahane thinks he's hit it into the gap but sees Buttler cover good ground to his right to get to the ball and take an excellent catch.

name: S Dube
commentary: picks out long-on! Buttler couldn't have asked for an easier catch. Kuldip finishes the over with another excellent ball. Full, wide and away from Dube's hitting arc, he goes for it and toe-ends it to the fielder. Royals have done the double over CSK this season, they go top of the table.

name: MM Ali
commentary: there's the thick underedge and Moeen is gone. This is pushed through quicker and away from Moeen as he reaches out to belt that through the off side. Excellent catch from Samson.

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: KL Rahul
commentary: clever from Rabada! Sends this down a touch wider on seventh stump and drags the length back a fraction on a fullish length to tease Rahul into another drive, but there's no footwork playing well away from his body and a thick edge flies to Shahrukh who is standing at a floating wide slip/gully (or a very fine short third, depending on your perspective) position and catches this reaching to his left at shoulder height.

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: KR Mayers
commentary: back of a length back on middle at 140 kph, Mayers opts to straight bat this driving over mid-on rather than go for a pull and pays the price. He was cramped with the length being dragged back, nowhere near a drivable length and a tame shot comes off the splice floating to Shikhar five yards inside the ring at mid-on for a basic catch.

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: A Badoni
commentary: fullish length on middle, Badoni tries to repeat the slog sweep over the leg side but hits it much too flat and picks out Chahar charging in 10 yards from deep square leg to take a thigh height catch. Livingstone erupts in celebration capping off the antagonism between the two throughout the early part of the over.

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: MP Stoinis
commentary: full and angled wide on eighth stump, Stoinis shuffles across trying to paddle short arm jab flick this over fine leg but winds up top edging it swirling between the keeper and short third. The keeper calls off the ring fielder for a simple catch to end one of several fiery knocks tonight from an LSG batter.

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: N Pooran
commentary: full on leg stump, Pooran shuffles across to scoop over fine leg and looks like he middles this straight to the keeper taking a sharp catch diving to his right. It's somehow given not out on field. Arshdeep calls for DRS immediately gesturing to Shikhar and they go for the review. There was a clear deflection but replays show it misses the bat and flicks the pad on the way through to the keeper. However, Pooran was so far across his stumps that it flicked his pad right in front of middle stump and ball-tracking shows this ball was ripping out middle and leg stump halfway up. So the decision is overturned to out. Just not how it looked like it would have been initially.

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: DJ Hooda

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: KH Pandya

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: Prabhsimran Singh
commentary: length ball on middle at 133 kph, whips a pull straight to the sub Daniel Sams at deep backward square leg. Picks him out 12 yards inside the rope taking this simply at chest height.

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: A Taide
commentary: full on fifth stump, goes to sweep this and top edges it straight up over the pitch. Bishnoi backpedals into short mid-on and gets his body and hands into an awkward position before adjusting to get two hands around it while tumbling backwards. Bishnoi's body was in a total tangle and made that catch far tougher than it should have been but made up for it with his hand-eye coordination and athleticism.

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: LS Livingstone
commentary: full on the stumps, Livingstone misses a slog and a huge lbw shout is upheld. Livingstone calls for DRS but simply because they've got two reviews left and only 28 balls left in the chase. This one is stone dead hitting him on the back leg in front of middle and off. Ball-tracking shows this ripping out the middle of middle stump. If the review was called for in a different context with the match still very much alive, it would go down as one of the worst reviews of all time. Regardless, it is still a shockingly bad review for a ball so obviously out.

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: SM Curran
commentary: slower ball lands full on middle, Curran checks a drive fooled by the lack of pace at 107 kph and the ball balloons over extra cover. Simple catch for Badoni taken crouching down at thigh height.

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: JM Sharma
commentary: knee high full toss outside leg, Jitesh shuffles outside leg trying to create room to drive inside out but picks out mid-off on the ring at head height. KL Rahul takes the catch after doing a little bunny hop to get his hands and head in line with the ball.

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: M Shahrukh Khan
shortText: c Ravi Bishnoi b Yash Thakur
commentary: knee high full toss on the legs at 137 kph, Shahrukh tries to heave this over the leg side rope but picks out the fielder at deep midwicket who settles under this three yards inside the rope for a basic catch at chest height to seal a 56-run win.

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: RD Chahar
commentary: 139 kph full on fourth stump, shuffles across to heave this to the leg side and picks out the long-on fielder 10 yards inside the rope for a simple waist height catch.

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: K Rabada
commentary: bullseye! Slower ball at 107 kph lands full on middle, Rabada is through a drive very early and middle stump is struck two-thirds of the way up knocking it out of the ground.

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: Arshdeep Singh
N Jagadeesan
commentary: huge appeal, and given! Full delivery angled into off stump. Jagadeesan walks across his stumps and then plays all over it as the ball smashes into the pads. Shami goes up and so does the umpire's finger Jagadeesan wants to have another look at this but if the impact is in line, it could be curtains! No bat involved and that hit him just below the knee roll of the front pad. Impact is in line too, and Ball Tracking shows the ball smashing into the middle of middle stump. Jagadeesan has to go!

name: Rahmanullah Gurbaz
shortText: c Rashid Khan b Noor Ahmad
commentary: holes out to deep mid wicket! Very full delivery at the pads that seems to cramp Gurbaz up for room. He has a swing at it anyway but cannot generate the power he is after. Arrows it towards deep mid wicket, where Rashid comes in from the fence and completes a smart take diving forward! What a knock this has been, though! It has, almost single-handedly, kept KKR in the hunt for a score in the 180-190 region!

name: SN Thakur
commentary: taken brilliantly by Mohit running back from mid on! Full and angled into the stumps. Shardul plants his front foot and looks to go over mid on. He does not time it very well, though, and the ball goes miles up into the air. Mohit judges it, runs back and then takes a sensational catch over his shoulder!

name: VR Iyer
commentary: huge appeal, and that looks very adjacent! Full and slanted into middle and off stump. Iyer, who is standing a foot outside leg stump even before the ball has been bowled, now decides to walk across and lap it. He misses it and is pinned on the back leg, right in front of middle and leg stump. The umpire has no hesitation in raising his finger but Iyer, for some reason, wants to have another look. This already seems a very poor review. Indeed it is. No bat involved and that is thundering into middle stump. KKR have burnt both of their reviews and they have been very ordinary, to say the least!

name: N Rana
commentary: straight to backward point! Back of a length delivery outside off stump that seems to stick in the surface just a tad. Nitish has a waft at it but does so without any conviction, spooning it straight to the fielder in the circle! KKR lose two in quick succession, and Gujarat are right back in this!

name: AD Russell
commentary: taken at deep point! What an anti-climax! Full and hung outside off stump. Russell throws the kitchen sink at it but the ball is just too far away from his arc. Slices it eventually and hits it straight down deep point's throat!

name: Shubman Gill
commentary: skies it and Narine bags Gill! Given just a hint of air on a full length outside off stump. Gill comes down the track and tries to tonk it into next week. Does not get to the pitch of the ball, though, and shanks it off the toe-end towards deep mid wicket. Russell does the rest and the noise at the Eden Gardens now is deafening!

name: HH Pandya
commentary: Harshit breaks through! Full and slanted into middle and off stump. Hardik shuffles across and then plays all over it. Gets pinned on the pads and the umpire has no hesitation in raising his finger! Hardik does not even bother with the review and that is the right call. That was plumb in front!

name: Abhishek Sharma
commentary: full on sixth stump to encourage a slog, Abhishek obliges heaving this flat to wide long-on with a miscued shot and Warner judges this exceptionally well charging in 15 yards off the rope on the straight boundary to intercept this at thigh height before some major fist pumping. Huge wicket in the context of this innings. Axar continues to stamp his authority as Delhi's most valuable player this season.

name: MA Agarwal
commentary: bounced out! Banged in short on fourth stump at 130 kph, Mayank tries to shuffle across to get inside the line to hook but is hustled and gloves this through to the keeper.

name: AK Markram
commentary: back o fa length ball on fourth stump at 127 kph, sits up for Markram to pull but doesn't get the elevation on this and picks out the boundary rider Axar Patel at wide deep midwicket who takes this catch at head height two yards inside the rope.

name: Abdul Samad
commentary: back of a length slower ball wide outside off at 120 kph, goes to cut this and is defeated by the lack of pace getting a thick toe edge that barely carries to the keeper at ankle height.

name: PD Salt
commentary: sensational low return catch! Half-tracker on eighth stump, Salt goes back to smash this through cover and it sticks in the wicket a touch so he's through the shot a touch more than intended and sends it straight back to Markande at shin height reaching low to his right. The bowler reacts exceptionally well and lunges right to snatch on. The umpires want to refer this to the third umpire to check for a clean catch but Salt marches off knowing it was held.

name: MR Marsh
commentary: bold from Hosein! After getting destroyed on the previous ball, he flights it up once more with a touch more air on a similarly fullish length. Marsh tries to repeat the six down the ground but the ball grips and turns to produce a skied outside edge swirling over extra cover. Fielder backpedals five yards from the edge of the ring to take this at chest height.

name: MK Pandey
commentary: flighted and drags this back on a good length to beat Pandey charging. He runs straight past this as it turns enough to beat a flick. He's given up way down the track as it passes just outside off stump. Simple stumping for Klaasen. Third umpire is reviewing this to make sure Klaasen didn't reach in front of the stumps before collecting. His hands are way behind the stumps. Two minutes of our lives we'll never get back...

name: SN Khan
commentary: bullseye! Yorker length on off stump, Sarfaraz tries to heave this over the leg side rather than jamming it out with a straight bat and pays the price.

name: S Dube
shortText: c Shahrukh Khan b Arshdeep Singh
commentary: slower ball does the trick. A wide slowie, on a length, Dube goes for it, tries to clear long-on, but ends up toe-ending it straight to long-on. Another ounce of muscle in this, and it would have cleared the man. Shows you how difficult it is for bowlers

name: MM Ali
commentary: and he has a stumping. Moeen jumps out of the track, Chahar gets appreciable turn on a flat legbreak, it beats his slog on the inside, and he is gone

name: RA Jadeja
commentary: Jadeja's strange innings comes to an end. No boundary intent so far, and when he tries to hit this leg-stump full toss for six, he ends up mis-hitting it straight to deep midwicket. On this pitch, Jadeja will have a lot to say with the ball, though

name: Prabhsimran Singh
commentary: and Jadeja strikes. Just enough turn. Prabh jumps out of the crease to go straight, the ball turns away ever so slightly with a puff of dust, and he is miles outside the crease when Dhoni collects the ball. Dhoni pauses a milli-second, the crowd goes berserk, and then he stumps Prabh

name: A Taide
commentary: Jadeja has fun after taking an easy catch. He just keeps bowling wide to Taide, who finally has a go at it and offers a straightforward return catch. Jadeja completes the catch, sees Livingstone is outside his crease, and then pretends to drop it to run him out instead. All for fun. Don't think Taide will be thrilled

name: LS Livingstone
commentary: oh he has pulled at a wide ball. You can't control them from this height. Deshpande has got this bouncer really high, Livingstone really just throws his bat at it, and deep midwicket takes a safe catch

name: SM Curran
commentary: oh this match is producing big plays after big plays. Again that natural inswing from that low angle beats the outside edge of Curran who things he has got a slot ball that he can loft back over his head. The off stumps is pegged back

name: JM Sharma
commentary: has he touched the rope? Another slower ball, wide outside off, grips the surface, Jitesh looks to go straight, and toe-ends a skier towards long-on. Shaik Rasheed is the fielder who seems to have misjudged and come in too far. He goes back, takes it overhead and then trips. His back foot gets stuck and doesn't slip, which seems to have stopped it from hitting the boundary triangle. The third umpire takes a long look, and his reasoning for giving this out is "I don't see the board move". The other angle, I thought, showed him touch the triangle slightly, but I could be wrong. The third umpire is the expert here. He is not doing commentary while making the decision so we have to trust what he is seeing more than what I thought I saw

name: YBK Jaiswal
commentary: Highish full toss at 141kph has been caught and bowled. But this is likely to be reviewed for no-ball. It is, Rohit and SKY have a chat with the umps, who smile and console him. Suds says Rohit was seen gesturing to the batters to let them review if they want. The full toss is over the stumps. Jaiswal was well standing inside the crease, it's around waist-height but his back-leg was bent. That goes against him and the ball is deemed to be fair. End of an amazing 124 from Jaiswal, he raises his bat high while getting a nice send-off.

name: JC Buttler
commentary: Buttler looks to continue his cut-loose mission but is caught at long-on. Chawla breaks through again. The batter mistimed it and knew his went down immediately after the bat turned after making contact with the toe-ender. He tried to whack it across over long-on but found Ramandeep.

name: SV Samson
shortText: c Tilak Varma b Arshad Khan
commentary: Soft-ish dismissal as Samson chips it straight to deep midwicket. Length ball on the pads, Samson was kinda stuck in between powering it and chipping it uppishly. In the end, he spooned it to Tilak, who took a comfortable catch.

name: SO Hetmyer
commentary: Heaved in the air, finds SKY at deep midwicket. Slower bouncer over middle and leg, Hetmyer stayed leg side and pulled, it went flat but not in the gap or far enough. Went straight to the breadbasket, and SKY skies it after taking a dolly

name: DC Jurel
commentary: Pulled in the air, Tilak takes a low catch at deep midwicket. Sent upstairs but that's taken with the fists just over the grass. Slower bouncer outside off and Jurel dragged it across, got decent timing but it went flat and towards the fielder

name: RG Sharma
commentary: Novelty dismissal, it's a variant of that Maxwell-leave. Sandeep knocks off Rohit's off bail with a knuckle ball. Rohit made room and shaped to go over cover, but he saw the slow length ball at 111kph and decided to let the ball go. He was withdrawing his bat as the ball came in and disturbed furniture.

name: Ishan Kishan
commentary: Kishan's struggle is over, Ashwin has him holed out to deep point. Slow ball on a length close to the off-side tramline. Kishan reached out and tried to whack it over point. Went through with the swing, bottom hand came off which affected the timing. Kishan whacked his pad in frustration right after he hit it and Boult took an easy catch

name: SA Yadav
commentary: Sandeep Sharma takes a potentially match-changing catch running back! He's on the floor for a while before his team-mates swarm him. Third umpire will check. He ran back from short fine leg, took the catch on the move and took it with outstretched hands, clung on while sliding over the grass. Magical catch. Was a shortish ball outside off that SKY tried to pull across. Sandeep covered 19 metres there.

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: V Kohli
commentary: Bishnoi gets Kohli! Googly on a length outside off stump. Kohli comes down the track and tries to go inside out. He does not get to the pitch of it, though, and the ball just sneaks past the flailing blade. Pooran does the rest behind the stumps and Bishnoi breaks RCB's opening stand!

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: F du Plessis
commentary: sliced and taken by Krunal at cover! Looped up nicely on a length outside off stump and du Plessis' eyes light up. He tries to go over the bowler's head but the ball spins away just enough to take a chunk off the outside edge. The ball spoons up towards cover where Krunal holds his nerve and completes the catch!

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: Anuj Rawat
commentary: holes out to deep mid wicket! Dragged down outside off stump and Rawat looks to clear the shorter fence. The ball, though, just sticks in the surface enough to muddle with Rawat's timing. He shanks it and Mayers does the rest on the fence! LSG keep chipping away!

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: MK Lomror
commentary: huge appeal, and given! That looks very adjacent! Length delivery slanted into off stump. The ball skids through and keeps a little low. Lomror is late on the stroke and is pinned on the pads. The umpire raises his finger and Lomror, after a long discussion with Karthik, reviews. Looks like the ball struck the back pad and if the impact is in line (or even Umpire's Call), this could be curtains for Lomror. Umpire's Call on Impact and the ball is going on to hit the stumps. Lomror has to go!

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: PWH de Silva

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: Mohammed Siraj
commentary: oh well! Back of a length delivery outside off stump. Siraj shuffles across his crease and looks for the scoop first up. Seems to miss but Pooran thinks there is a nick. In fact, LSG have reviewed this one! The ball does pass close to the bat and there is indeed a slight spike as the ball is next to the gloves. Inspired review, and Naveen is now on a hat-trick!

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: JR Hazlewood

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: KR Mayers
shortText: c Anuj Rawat b Mohammed Siraj
commentary: Siraj gets Mayers! Oh my! Back of a length delivery on off stump that seems to hurry onto Mayers. He has a swipe at it but splices it badly. The ball lobs up towards mid on, where Rawat does the rest! Massive, massive moment in the game!

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: A Badoni
commentary: Badoni bites the dust too! RCB are all over LSG! In the slot outside off stump and Badoni's eyes light up. He tries to go inside out but does not quite get the elevation he is after. Hits it in the air in the extra cover region and Kohli does the rest, leaping up into the air and snaffling it!

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: KH Pandya
commentary: Krunal bites the dust! Given just a bit of air on a length outside off stump. Krunal comes down the track and is not to the pitch of the ball. In the end, all he does is chip it straight to Kohli at long off, who makes no mistake! LSG in a spot of bother now!

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: DJ Hooda
commentary: appeal for a stumping! Googly tossed up nicely on a length just outside off stump. Hooda goes searching for it and plays all over it as the ball snakes past the inside edge. Karthik whips off the bails in a flash but has Hooda dragged his back foot out of his crease? We go through the entire protocol of checking whether there is a nick. No bat involved but Hooda's back foot is on the crease when Karthik takes off the bails! LSG are in disarray, and RCB, at this stage, can do no wrong!

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: MP Stoinis
commentary: Stoinis perishes! LSG are imploding here! Flighted on a length outside off stump. Stoinis looks to go inside out but is nowhere close to the pitch of the ball. Slices it a mile in the air and Prabhudessai does the rest at long off!

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: N Pooran
commentary: taken at deep square leg! Pooran goes! Full delivery outside leg stump and Pooran tries to hit that into next week. The ball, though, just holds in the surface a little and he gets it right off the toe end of the bat. The ball goes miles up into the air and when it comes down, Lomror does the rest on the fence!

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: Ravi Bishnoi
commentary: dropped! But Bishnoi has run himself out, this is just crazy cricket! Length delivery outside off stump. Bishnoi reverse-sweeps it hard towards backward point, where Hazlewood puts it down. Bishnoi then, for some reason, thinks the second is on and despite the dive, is at least a yard short of his ground!

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: KL Rahul
PD Salt
commentary: half-volley drilled to extra cover first ball! Shami sent this down at a juicy tempting width on sixth stump and Salt lashed this flat to Miller at rib height. Didn't have to move standing eight yards in from the ring.

name: MK Pandey
commentary: Shami can do no wrong, especially when he gets a tremendous flying catch from behind the stumps by Saha! Full length swinging away a touch on fourth stump, there to drive but Pandey is a touch late with no footwork and the late movement finds the edge to produce a chance flying low to Saha's right at knee height. He dives across first slip and snags a one-handed stunner while completing the sideways tumble on his hips to ensure his elbows don't hit the ground to jar the ball loose.

name: AR Patel
commentary: full on off stump at 129 kph, Axar drives to shovel this back over the bowler out of the ground but gets under it too much and skies it high in front of the sightscreen. Rashid scampers over 20 yards to his left before settling on his knees five yards inside the rope to take this catch.

name: Shubman Gill
commentary: half-volley sliced straight to cover! Nortje channels the first ball wicket to start the match by Shami to Salt! This was sent down on seventh stump and Gill opens the face to drive this toward point but it is spliced low to cover at knee height and the catch is taken lunging forward.

name: V Shankar
commentary: bullseye! 119 kph slower ball foxes Shankar flicking across the line too early. It's a knuckle ball that would make Tim Wakefield and Charlie Hough proud landing very full on off stump line before cannoning into middle and off halfway up.

name: DA Miller
commentary: bullseye! Bowled behind the legs after an audacious shuffle across! Miller tries to play a release shot walking all the way across his stumps to scoop over fine leg and the early dot ball pressure results in a third-ball duck as middle and off stump are pinged back.

name: A Manohar
shortText: c Aman Hakim Khan b Ahmed
commentary: Manohar's struggle comes to an end. Length ball on off stump at 139 kph, yet another attempted pull is miscued but this one finally has enough pace on it off the bat to reach a boundary fielder. Aman jogs in 10 yards off the rope at long-on for a basic chest high catch.

name: R Tewatia
commentary: 119 kph slower length ball on fifth stump, Tewatia tries to muscle this over the leg side again but miscues it off the splice fluttering gently to Rossouw five yards inside the ring at extra cover and he backpedals a few steps for a simple head high catch.

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: KR Mayers
commentary: Moeen does the season double over Mayers! Gets him both times in the league stage. Full ball outside off. Just a bit of turn away with Mayers looking to go big down the ground. Ball went high up, and Gaikwad ran in from long off to seal the deal. Kisses the ball too. Oh, how good it is to be in a post Covid world.

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: KS Sharma
commentary: ...and this sticks! Full ball around off and Karan got forward to hammer this with some high velocity. But Moeen sticks his hands up and the ball is in his grasp! Even MS Dhoni is impressed from behind the stumps. A second one for Moeen

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: KH Pandya
commentary: and straight to slip! Rahane scoops this up from way down low after Krunal poked at it. This was full-ish outside off, tempting Krunal to feel bat on ball. The plan has worked magnificently with replays showing Rahane's fingers under the ball holding on to a real grubber. Golden duck for KP, Theekshana on a hat-trick

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: MP Stoinis
commentary: flight, turn, and bowled! Jadeja bowled fullish outside leg. Stoinis didn't expect it to turn a lot, so brought the bat down for a straight drive. But he's shocked to hear the stumps get rattled, and looks back to see that the ball turned all the way across to knock off stump back. Incredible.

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: N Pooran
commentary: Pooran gone for a 31-ball 20 having failed to clear cover this time. This was full outside off and the drive didn't have the elevation to beat the infielder. Timing also average. Story of his innings.

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: A Badoni

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: Prabhsimran Singh
commentary: now tickled to the keeper! And Prabh walks back himself. It was almost a carbon copy of the previous ball: short, pitched on middle and off, and angling away from the right-hander. Prabh went for the slash away from his body, trying to reach out for the ball. But the outside edge settled well into Kishan's hands, and at a comfortable height

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: LS Livingstone

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: JM Sharma

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: RG Sharma
commentary: And he is gone! Picks out deep third. Rohit was skipping out and backing away towards leg, but Dhawan bowls it full outside off. Rohit reaches out but doesn't get enough bat on it and Punjab Kings have the early breakthrough without any runs on the board. Rohit's 15th duck in the IPL

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: Ishan Kishan
shortText: c R Dhawan b Arshdeep Singh
commentary: Arshdeep gets Ishan Kishan again! Bangs it in short on off, Kishan looks to hook but picks out deep fine leg, who takes it comfortably. Kishan has had the better of him today, but Arshdeep has the last laugh. Can this wicket turn the tide, though, coming so soon after SKY's dismissal?

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: SA Yadav
commentary: And the impact player gets the impact player! On a length around off, SKY crouches and cuts this hard but didn't quite get under it enough. It goes hard to Arshdeep at short third and it bobs out of his hands initially as he catches this above his head and tries to bring it in control, but holds on in the second attempt

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: TH David

venue: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium, Mohali, Chandigarh
name: NT Tilak Varma

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: Rahmanullah Gurbaz
commentary: length ball on off at 133 kph, Gurbaz tries to flick this over midwicket but Jansen hustles him for pace and he's a fraction late on the shot producing a top edge that swirls over mid-on. Brook settles under this inside the ring and takes a simple chest high catch with his arms tucked into his body.

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: VR Iyer
commentary: bounced out! Bangs this in short on middle at 137 kph, Iyer continues to look uncomfortable v Jansen and tries to hook this defensively while turtling his head out of the way and gloves this up in the air popping up to carry gently through to Klaasen.

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: N Rana
commentary: brilliant caught and bowled by Markram finished off at mid-on! Flighted full on fourth stump, Rana slogs trying to clear midwicket but it's a bit out of reach and produces a top edge swirling toward the edge of the ring. There's nobody in front of square in the leg side ring. The nearest fielder is at long-on. Markram recognizes this immediately and sprints backwards 20+ yards before flinging himself toward the edge of the circle at mid-on to cling onto a stunning effort. The judges from Switzerland, Mexico, Aruba, Fiji, Indonesia, Benin and North Macedonia all hold up 10 marks out of 10 on their cards.

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: AD Russell
commentary: this one is floated full again but much wider on eighth stump, Russell winds up for an almighty heave but can't quite reach it. Spices a top edge fluttering tamely to short third man on the ring, who slides forward to take the catch on his knees.

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: SP Narine
commentary: slower ball at 114 kph on sixth stump on a good length, Narine is badly fooled through the shot early and tries to check his drive but makes too much contact with the shot and sends the ball floating softly to Agarwal on the ring at extra cover, who shuffles a few yards left to take a chest high catch.

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: SN Thakur
commentary: length ball on seventh stump, slapped high toward the cover sweeper Abdul Samad who jogs in casually 10 yards off the rope and takes this catch at thigh height reaching forward in stride.

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: AS Roy

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: Harshit Rana
commentary: slower length ball angled across fifth stump, Harshit misses swishing at this. They try to pinch a bye to get Roy back on strike. Klaasen's flick at the stumps misses badly but Natarajan backs up the throw from Klaasen well and fires at the non-striker's end to catch Harshit a yard short.

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: VG Arora

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: Abhishek Sharma
commentary: the great catching continues in the second innings! Length ball on the hips, Abhishek goes to flick this over square leg but this climbs on him a touch and results in a skied chance toward deep backward square. Russell comes forward for this steepler and settles under it before lunging forward at the last moment to latch on.

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: MA Agarwal
commentary: bounced out! Almost a carbon copy of how Jansen took a wicket in the first innings against Iyer. 139 kph delivery angled into the body on middle and leg stump. Mayank plays an almost identical shot to how Iyer got out, a defensive hook while trying to turtle his head out of the way for safety and the timid stroke attempt results in a gloved chance lobbing up behind the stumps gently to the keeper.

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: AK Markram
commentary: slower bouncer on sixth stump, Markram tries to tennis overhead smash this but pings it high to long-off. Simple catch for Rinku camping under this 12 yards inside the rope at head height.

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: H Klaasen
commentary: 133 kph delivery on a fullish length on fourth stump, Klaasen tries to heave this over midwicket but gets it more off the splice and picks out Russell on the rope at deep midwicket. Catch taken three yards inside the rope at head height.

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: Abdul Samad
commentary: length ball on leg stump, tries to slaughter this over the leg side but doesn't get the elevation and picks out the boundary rider at deep midwicket taking this coolly at head height three yards inside the rope. The crowd went nuts when the ball left the bat thinking it was going for a certain six but Anukul Roy was there to intercept it and the crowd falls into a hush.

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: M Jansen
commentary: 142 kph inswinging yorker on sixth stump, Jansen tries to drive this out and there's a noise through to the keeper diving left. Appeal from the keeper Gurbaz is upheld and Jansen walks. Sure enough on replays there's a thin inside edge which caused Gurbaz to have to dive leg side.

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: B Kumar

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: M Markande
YBK Jaiswal
commentary: chaos and a runout! Short and wide, Samson tries to guide this past backward point but a spectacular diving stop is made by the backward point fielder Abhinav who deflects this directly to the fielder Mohit at short third just a few meters away. Jaiswal set off for a single from the non-striker's end and there looked likely to be a clear single judging by the fact that Jaiswal reached the striker's end standing alongside Samson by the time Mohit let go of a calm throw to Rashid over the non-striker's stumps. He collects and slams the ball into the stumps to end Jaiswal's stay. Samson took three strides down the pitch after contact, but then was guilty of ball-watching and Jaiswal was left sold out. Jaiswal tried to scamper back and wound up running 40 yards in the end winding up four yards short of making the ground again at the non-striker's end.

name: JC Buttler
commentary: slashes this straight to short third! Deep square leg was pushed out after the previous two boundaries and short third is brought into the ring from deep third and it pays off immediately. Length ball on seventh stump at 136 kph, looks like a pretty innocuous delivery that Buttler chases flat-footed and a thick edge flies straight to the fielder on the ring, Mohit, who snaps this up on his left thigh.

name: SV Samson
commentary: fullish length on middle and off at 141 kph, shuffles across his stumps to flick this over midwicket but it climbs on him and results in a top edge swirling over cover. Hardik shuffles forward five yards from the edge of the ring and pouches this reaching forward at waist height.

name: R Ashwin
commentary: bullseye! Rashid was toying with Ashwin throughout that over. Good length ball on fourth stump just skids on flat past an attempted flick by Ashwin through midwicket. He was playing for a googly but this one just came on flat rather than turn in sharply and it goes past the outside edge to smash over off stump.

name: R Parag
commentary: googly spins in sharply from fifth stump and hits Parag in front of middle and off. Deflection goes to slip for a catch as the appeal goes up for lbw and is upheld. Parag looks forlornly at his partner before opting for DRS more so in desperate hope than belief. A frankly terrible review borne out of a plea to justify the move to use him as an impact sub. The ball misses bat and hits him on the back leg just above the knee roll, but flexing some way, in front of middle stump and is going on to smash middle 3/4 of the way up.

name: SO Hetmyer
commentary: full on off, stock legbreak spins in and Hetmyer defends down the wrong line hit on the pads below the knee. Big lbw shout upheld. Royals are out of reviews. He was hit in front of off. Possible it might have skidded to miss off based on the angle but more likely that it would have wound up as umpire's call on ball-tracking. That's irrelevant now though. Hetmyer is gone and Royals are eight down.

name: DC Jurel
commentary: flighted full and on the borderline of leg stump, Jurel prods forward down the wrong line and is hit on the pads stretching forward, big lbw shout upheld. He doesn't hesitate to review this. He's banking on this pitching outside the line of the stumps because otherwise this was stone dead. It pitches well in line with the stumps, more closer to middle and leg and ball-tracking is showing this ripping out middle halfway up. Rajasthan have lost both reviews.

name: TA Boult
commentary: bullseye! 142 kph yorker with a radar locked in on the base of off stump. Boult was backing away trying to drive through cover and is beaten with off stump ripped out of the ground.

name: Shubman Gill
commentary: stumped by Samson! Chahal has a few words to utter out. Now he slows it down to Gill too - like he has done to Saha today - and completely foxes Gill. It was a legspinner, pitched on a length on middle stump. Gill saw that tossed up and danced down the pitch, little realising that it was much slower than the previous balls. He missed, the ball turned well away, and Samson found him short

name: Ishan Kishan
commentary: this is ballooned.... and mid-on settles under it! That went very highhhhh up. Theekshana had his eyes on it for a long time, and finally grabbed it with a reverse cup in front of his face. Kishan looked to flick this good length ball on middle, and seemed to have closed the face of the bat early. That meant he got a leading edge, and the ball skied up for a comfortable catch

name: RG Sharma
commentary: gone, lobbed up to point! The moment Dhoni comes up to the stumps, Rohit tries something different: he went to scoop this good length ball around middle and off, but the length was slightly shorter than what the shot ideally is suited for. Rohit seemed to have played early, and the ball popped up off the splice of the bat before taking his bottom thumb towards point, with Jadeja almost catching it in front of short third

name: N Wadhera
commentary: gone! Hits middle stump! He saw Wadhera get across the stumps early, and Pathirana fired a yorker at 145kph. It was dipping on to the batter, and turned out to be too low and too quick for Wadhera's attempted flick. He was beaten, as the ball hit the base of the middle stump to end a very good knock

name: SA Yadav
commentary: bowled him! Jadeja fires one in at 102kph, and Surya is beaten by the pace. Surya stands after giving himself room, and looks to slash hard at this shortish ball on middle and off. But he is beaten, and the ball zips through to smack the stumps and break a steady stand to bring CSK back

name: T Stubbs
commentary: good catch by Jadeja! Looked like he was stationed at point, but he surely ended at where an imaginary cover-point would have been. Stubbs went slashing at this full ball outside off, while playing with a flat bat. But it was a change-up from Pathirana, as he rolled his fingers through it, and the ball skied up towards the circle

name: TH David
commentary: mistimes it to long-off! Gaikwad on the boundary takes a few steps forward while keeping his eyes on the ball, and gobbles it. It was quite a full ball around sixth stump, as David - who was deep in the crease - tried to loft but mis-hit. I think the length was a bit too full to facilitate that loft down the pitch

name: DP Conway
commentary: Conway goes across and falls as he tries to paddle, ball hits him and deflects to the keeper's left, on its way to the boundary. Rohit and Madhwal have a chat and decide to review. The full ball pitched outside off and Conway's back leg was close to the off-side tramline and the front leg was stuck right in front of the stumps. Hawk-Eye shows three reds and Conway has to go

name: AM Rahane
commentary: Inside edge beaten, given LBW. Rahane reviews with a couple of seconds remaining. Googly landed on a length around off and came in a bit. Rahane took a small stride and tried to block it towards mid-on, missed it and got hit on the knee-roll of the front leg. Hawk-Eye came up with two reds but the graphic didn't play to the end so there's a wait. It comes back after a minute. Hawk-Eye shows the impact is flush on off and it'd go on on to hit the top of middle

name: V Kohli
commentary: Kohli finds short fine leg! That was juggle that could put any circus expert to shame! Kohli flicked the ball straight to Khaleel, and he got both hands to it, then juggled, juggled, and tried and tried, and kept tossing the ball close to his chest before finally clinging on to it. That was about eight to 10 attempts there. Not kidding

name: F du Plessis
commentary: that's the first wicket of the game and it's the more dangerous batter gone! Marsh's slow and wide ball works this time because du Plessis goes after this one on the off side, takes the aerial route, but there's no pace at all, and he finds deep cover

name: PD Salt
commentary: Beauty to end Salt's assault! Tossed up full on leg, Salt goes back and plays all around it as the ball deflects off his pads onto the off-stump. He goes for 87 off 45, essentially having wrapped the match up for his team today!

name: MR Marsh
commentary: Oh, he has picked out deep square leg off the full toss! Low full toss going down leg and Marsh looks to flick it away. It goes up high and falls to Lomror, who takes a simple catch

name: HH Pandya
commentary: Pandya catches Pandya, stinger at cover. Well taken. Mohsin has his arms up, Hardik leaves with a smile, Krunal wrings his hands. Was a slower length ball at 122kph, Hardik set up well and slapped it powerfully but the timing was off.

name: KR Mayers
commentary: Rashid loses control while sliding but manages to take a specky! He came in from deep fine, running to his diagonal right, he was on course to taking it but the slide folded his knees. He, nonetheless, didn't stop going with the momentum, stretched out his hands, plucked it and ran jubilantly in celebrations. He ran 26 metres far, says Mark Howard on air. It was a slower bouncer just outside off that Mayers bunt-pulled and tried to hit it into the ground but got a top edge

name: Q de Kock
commentary: QDK goes across and might be bowled around his legs, Bharat appeals. Anil Chaudhary is unsure and sends it upstairs. Side-on replay shows there's a sound as the ball brushes the stumps. The bails come off and QDK has to go. He premeditated, tried to slog sweep the full ball but was beaten, almost covered the stumps while playing the shot

name: DJ Hooda
commentary: Full toss is hit to deep midwicket, Tewatia runs in and takes the catch. Ball was at the stumps, Hooda wound up and tried to slam it across the line but got it off the sticker. Hooda closes his eyes and almost swings the bat across in frustration while leaving

name: MP Stoinis
commentary: Pace off again, Stoinis aims for long-on and balloons it to short third. Full ball was outside off, Stoinis stayed just a bit away from leg stump, tried slogging it across but it went up, like it does against legspinners and Mohit thanked heavens after Shami took a dolly

name: N Pooran
shortText: c Mohammed Shami b Noor Ahmad
commentary: Pooran tries to slog sweep a legbreak outside off but the length ball turns away and balloons off a top edge to Shami at short third

name: A Badoni
commentary: Slower one finds the top edge off the slog sweep, Noor settles under after taking a few steps across, takes it low to the left

name: KH Pandya
commentary: Back-of-hand slower ball is full and wide, Krunal tries to slog it across but finds long-on. Went off the top edge. Mohit's thanking the heavens again

name: YBK Jaiswal
commentary: Jansen's extra bounce does the job for him. Again slower offcutter, into the pitch, 128ks, Jaiswal is looking to help it over short third, but the ball makes contact closer to his glove than the middle of the bat. Easy catch for Natarajan at short third

name: JC Buttler
commentary: but he has bluffed Buttler this time. Buttler is looking to shuffle to access that wide line, and he spears in a straight yorker/low full toss. Beats him comprehensively, hits him him plumb in front, not given out, and Bhuvneshwar immediately wants a review. The replay confirms it is hitting. Buttler misses out on a century, but walks back with his bat raised to acknowledge the hearty applause he is getting

name: Anmolpreet Singh
commentary: Chahal was sensing something. He goes full and middle and leg again. Just gets a bit of dip on this one, and Anmol gets a top edge on the slog sweep. Caught by Hetmyer running in from deep midwicket

name: Abhishek Sharma
commentary: the problem for SRH is, one six an over is not good enough. Abhishek has to keep taking risks, and Ashwin stays away from his arc. Gets just that bit of turn to take the edge to short third as Abhishek tries to go over long-off

name: H Klaasen
commentary: good as gold, Chahal's slow wide tantalising legbreak works. He pitched short last ball, but this time he has flighted it, pitched on a length, just 75ks, got the turn, and Klaasen ends up mis-hitting it to long-off

name: AK Markram
commentary: beaten on the reverse-sweep, and Im sure he will review the lbw call. he has reviewed it. To me, the only question is, did it straighten enough? Chahal fires in this legbreak, it pitches with in the stumps, beats his bat and glove, hits him in front of leg, and is hitting leg. Just straightens enough to be pitching within the stumps and be hitting the stumps

name: Prabhsimran Singh
commentary: circus catch behind the stumps! Prabhsimran charges a length ball on middle and tries to swat this over the leg side but it climbs on him a touch and he gets a thin top edge. The ball redirects high to the right of Gurbaz behind the stumps. He reaches up to his right above head height and parries it in the air initially before shuffling a few more steps backward to his right to lunge and collect it on the second attempt before the ball can hit the turf.

name: LS Livingstone
commentary: Chakaravarthy sends down a leg break that fools Livingstone on a good length expecting a googly misreading this out of the hand and defends down the wrong line. Ball strikes him on the pads in front of off stump, huge lbw shout upheld. Livingstone reviews this. Main doubt was height. But he was clearly struck in line with the stumps. Glitch on ball-tracking. It shows the ball hitting him in line but the projection stops and the drama builds.... Finally the projection is showing the ball clipping the bails on umpire's call. The decision stays out.

name: JM Sharma
commentary: good length ball on sixth stump, Jitesh tries to run this with an open face through third man but gets a very thin edge held onto by Gurbaz behind the stumps.

name: SM Curran
commentary: fuller and wider on sixth stump, Curran goes to slog sweep this and top edges this swirling over midwicket in the ring. Gurbaz runs out from behind the stumps and calls off Suyash before taking this tumbling forward. There was no fielder in the ring on the leg side so one of the two had to go for it.

name: Rahmanullah Gurbaz
commentary: full and straight again with back of the hand slower ball landing on middle beating a flick from Gurbaz, huge lbw shout this time is upheld as Ellis chases after the ball in short cover attempting to deny a leg bye just in case. Gurbaz reviews this but it looks stone dead and he already starts wandering off before the replays are done. Three reds light up on ball-tracking. Review lost.

name: N Rana
commentary: good length ball outside leg stump, Rana hops around to reverse swat this high and deep over wide cover. He gets under this far more than intended and it hangs in the air long enough for Livingstone to cover 30 yards running across to his right from long-off and take a superb sliding catch on his knees going feet first to latch on in the breadbasket at thigh height.

name: VR Iyer
commentary: googly lands on a fullish length on middle and spins away from Iyer's charge down the track. He shovels this in the air off the toe and a chance floats to long-off where Livingstone holds onto a basic chance eight yards in from the rope.

name: AD Russell
commentary: full and wide yorker, Russell misses trying to jam this out through point. Rinku alertly tries to steal a bye and gets a great start. Russell is very late leaving his ground though and the relay from the keeper to Arshdeep results in Arshdeep gently tossing the ball into the stumps from five yards away with Russell a few feet short.

name: V Kohli
commentary: big appeal for caught behind, but the umpire is unmoved. Rohit reviews immediately, I suspect Kohli's got a big edge on that. Yup, yup, he has! Kohli was looking to give him the charge and heave it across the line but ends up nicking through to Ishan Kishan. What a big, big wicket that. Drama early on at the Wankede.

name: F du Plessis
commentary: taken at the third attempt! Du Plessis attempts to ramp this from wide of off stump, miscues towards short fine leg, where the sub fielder Vishnu Vinod juggles the ball once... twice... but holds on! Looked like a slower ball and Faf couldn't generate the required power. Big wicket for MI

name: Anuj Rawat
commentary: What was Rawat doing? Looks to scoop but Behrendorff bowls this a touch wide, the extra movement away means Rawat is now forced to abandon his shot and try to steer it instead but ends up getting a top-edge as Cam Green running back from slip gobbles it up with his big hands.

name: MK Lomror
commentary: bowled 'im! Kartikeya slides one through Lomror. This is a stock fingerspinner at good pace, Lomror again drops back but only manages to deflect the ball into his leg stump. Two wickets in as many overs for Mumbai

name: Ishan Kishan
commentary: what a comeback, Hasaranga gives RCB some breathing space! Bowls one through quicker, the ball skids through on a length and cramps Kishan for room as he looks to slap it through the off side, gets the thinnest of edges that Rawat pouches superbly.

name: RG Sharma
commentary: big appeal for lbw as Rohit is struck on the pad as he steps out to work it to the leg side. RCB have reviewed the not-out call. No bat involved as Ultra Edge confirms. The only point of contention is ball tracking and it comes back to show all three reds. Rohit is gone, what an inspired appeal that. Rohit is gone and it's a double-strike that has brought RCB back into the game.

name: SA Yadav
commentary: slower ball, swung to leg... but it won't clear the man! Vyshak claims the wicket of SKY but not before the damage has been done. Couldn't get enough power on this one and it plops well inside the rope to be taken safely by Jadhav. SKY walks off, having made his highest IPL score, to a rousing ovation from the Wankhede faithful

name: TH David
commentary: chipped straight to long-off! Vyshak takes two in two. Full outside off, pace off again and David picks out Maxwell running in for a simple catch. Drama? Probably not quite yet

name: DP Conway
commentary: another early sweep, beaten again, and he is given out. Conway has a chat with Gaikwad, but decides not to review. The only question perhaps is, has he got himself outside the line? I think it is umpire's call on impact. Other than he has missed the length completely, and also chosen the wrong line. In fact he has not chosen anything. He has premeditated

name: AM Rahane
commentary: sensational catch. He has dived to his right, up to where the non-striker stands. Even the umpire has made an O with his lips. One-handed skimmer. However, if he had dropped it and collected it, Dube would have been run out because he is so far down the pitch

name: MM Ali
commentary: that's probably why they are not taking risks. Moeen skips down the wicket, Kuldeep goes slow again, 84ks, and again goes wide. Moeen has to reach out, and the turn takes the edge for a catch to backward point

name: S Dube
commentary: he has the field set for this ball, and finally bowls the ball for that field. Short of a length, at his body, 118ks, Dube pulls it into the big side of the ground, and toe-ends it to deep midwicket

name: RA Jadeja
commentary: same play. Same ball, Jadeja tries the same shot, but this is the slower legcutter, and not full enough. All he manages is a mi9is-hit to long-on. The crowd goes quiet

name: MS Dhoni
commentary: caught at long-on. pin-drop silence. Just no pace to work with. Slower-ball legcutter, into the pitch, and Dhoni goes down the ground and finds long-on

name: PD Salt
commentary: what do we know? Short at 126ks can work. The line is a little wider, and the ball stops in the pitch. Salt has to drag the pull, and also the slowness of the pitch makes him check his shot. A top edge ensues, and he is caught at mid-on

name: MR Marsh
commentary: they are both at the same end. Pandey has sold a dummy to Marsh. Looks to drop and run, takes a start, and then turns his back on Marsh, who is gone too far down the pitch. Rahane does well to not throw. Imagine you are Pandey, you don't play every game, how easy or difficult is it to sacrifice yourself?

name: MK Pandey
commentary: as plumb as an lbw can get. Pandey is sitting deep in his crease again, looking for an error in length, but Pathirahna bowls a 149ks yorker. You don't really need an umpire to give this one out. A forgettable night for Pandey comes to an end

name: AR Patel
commentary: lovely deception. Slower ball, 132ks, and Axar is early into it even though the length is right in the slot. It is just too good. Axar ends up checking the shot, and is caught at wide long-on

name: Rahmanullah Gurbaz
commentary: Ripper taken by Sandeep at mid-off and Gurbaz has his bat on his head. It was a full ball that Gurbaz made room to and tried to thump over mid-off, it went flat and to the right of Sandeep, who tumbled and took it mid-air. Even Boult went 'wow' at that.

name: VR Iyer
commentary: Chahal goes wide, Iyer chases and skies it to Boult, who takes a tumbling catch at cover. It was a full slider at 92kph just inside the tramline and Iyer stayed in his crease and tried to go over cover. It hit wide and high on the bat. Chahal has two

name: N Rana
commentary: Swept flat in the air, Shimron lunges down and takes it at deep-backward square. Chahal is now the highest wicket-taker in the IPL. Chahal upper cuts the air in celebration. He got there with a legbreak spinning a bit towards off stump, Rana got on one knee and hit it decently but the placement was off. Didn't look like Hetmyer spotted it at first but managed to go down and take it.

name: AD Russell
commentary: Cut on the move, Ashwin takes a catch just over the head at backward point. Russell was on the move and was cramped by the hard-length ball angling in. He went through with the cut but got it off the edge. Ashwin's alert as ever, Asif is pumped as Russell walks back. The change of length works for Asif

name: SN Thakur
commentary: LBW appeal turned down, Chahal's signalling the T. Samson calls for the review later. Thakur misses the sweep there and got hit just under the knee-roll of the bent front foot. It was a full ball that hit him right in front. No edge. Was it bouncing over? No, it's hitting middle and top, three reds and Thakur has to go.

name: SP Narine
commentary: Overpitched just outside off, Narine launches an off-drive and is unable to clear Root at long-off. It's a bread-basket catch taken with ease

name: JC Buttler
commentary: Direct hit from Russell finds Buttler short. Buttler took a step or two and put his gloves up to indicate he wanted to stop. Jaiswal was over halfway down the pitch for the run. Buttler went through but had no chance of making it with the direct hit. It was a back-of-length ball that came in to beat the back-foot defence, the ball hit the thigh pad and rolled towards cover point. It seemed like there was a run but a late start and hesitation cost Buttler.

name: Ishan Kishan
commentary: wrong ball to sweep. Full and on the stumps, and Kishan probably just premeditated this. Sneaks under the bat and straightens to smack his back thigh - the back pad is fully on the ground. Rashid only appeals because it's required of him, and Kishan doesn't even review. Ball-tracking has it hitting middle and off halfway up

name: RG Sharma
commentary: and they get it! Caught at slip no less. Quick legbreak, turns away from a perfect length on off stump. It would be a testing delivery in a Test match, and Rohit, going forward to defend, nicks it. Good catch from Tewatia, had to move quickly to his left and he made it look simple

name: N Wadhera
commentary: wicket No. 3! Legbreak, doesn't straighten towards the stumps but keeps going with the angle from right-arm over. Looks to make room to cut, and the ball doesn't bounce as much as he expects. Thick inside edge into the stumps

name: Vishnu Vinod
commentary: now he goes for the off-stump line, and ends up bowling a full-toss while probably targeting the yorker. I don't think it's above waist height, though the umpires have a look. Vinod looks to slog it away and ends up slicing it high, caught by deep backward square leg coming in off the rope. Vinod is walking away, but the umpires ask him to wait. Yup, it's not above waist height

name: TH David
commentary: four wickets, and what a fourth wicket! David goes early. Full legbreak on off stump, and David is out for the softest of dismissals. Was looking to work it to long-on, but the ball pitches shorter than he expects, and he ends up squaring up and slicing the ball back to the bowler at stomach height

name: Shubman Gill
commentary: sends Gill' off-stump cartwheeling! Was Gill expecting it to bounce a lot more? Madhwal bowls a skiddy length ball on off, Gill attempts his trademark short-arm jab that he plays so well but misses it altogether and sees the stumps rattled. Madhwal is pumped and takes off on a celebratory run.

name: HH Pandya
commentary: that's a very un-Hardik like shot and he's gone! Bowls length again but in trying to angle it across, he has Hardik attempting a big bow into the leg side but ends up getting a nick through to Ishan Kishan. Hardik didn't even wait umpire to signal that out; was on his bike straightaway.

name: V Shankar
commentary: magicball from Chawla, legend! Unleashes a googly first up, draws Shankar forward but the ball dips and spins back in to beat the inside edge and crash into the stumps. Dream ball. The old dog still has plenty of miles left. He's absolutely rolling the clock back. Terrific, terrific display of proper legspin bowling.

name: DA Miller
commentary: Miller has been given out lbw but he reviews immediately. It's a well-disguised cutter that angles back in from around the stumps to cramp Miller for room, he's hit flush on the back thigh and ball tracking comes back to confirm this would've crashed into leg stump.

name: A Manohar
commentary: gets him first ball! Rush of blood from Abhinav Manohar as he goes back to pull. Kartikeya gets this to drift in and skid through from around the stumps, beats Manohar's swing.

name: R Tewatia
commentary: the wheels are coming off! Tewatia attempts the slog sweep, hits it really well but it's almost as if he forgot there was a fielder patrolling the longest boundary at deep backward square. Cam Green covered good ground to his right to take the catch.

name: Noor Ahmad
commentary: another one for Kartikeya with that simple policy of 'you miss, I hit.' Bowls length into the pitch from around the stumps, Noor steps out to swing this over deep midwicket but misses it altogether and is bowled.

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: Anmolpreet Singh
commentary: takes the return catch and throws the ball into the pitch. Mishra again slow in the air, has the ball on a string, nice dip, and then turn. Anmol, who has left the crease, knows he is beaten in the flight and just looks to turn it to leg for one, but gets a leading edge back to Mishra

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: Abhishek Sharma
commentary: he has got him with extra bounce. Or has he? Because the umpire doesn't think so. LSG say they will check with the third umpire. Excellent delivery. Not only has it kicked at him, it has also cut back a little, and taken a good chuck of the top glove on the way. The umpire has to change his decision

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: AK Markram
commentary: oof. What a delivery. He has got him stumped while defending. What turn. High pace, pitched on leg, closing Markram with the angle as he looks to defend, and the ball turns away sharply. Markram is dragged out of the crease as he is not close to the pitch of the ball. Easy stumping for de Kock

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: H Klaasen
commentary: full toss for a wicket. Hit straight down the throat of long-on. Oh plum full toss at 132ks, and Prerak Mankad takes the catch and immediately looks back to stare the at the stand, which is right behind their dugout
