venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: Abdul Samad

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: B Kumar

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: KR Mayers
commentary: Stunning catch from Markram! Philips strikes immediately. Full delivery on middle, Mayers lofts it towards mid-off where Markram dives to his right and completes a catch

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: Q de Kock
commentary: Oh, why would you do that? Fuller on off stump, turns away a little but de Kock has already attempted a reverse sweep to that delivery, gets a top-edge and is caught by Abhishek at backward point. Markande has come back well in his second over after going for 14 in his first over

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: MP Stoinis
shortText: c Abdul Samad b Abhishek Sharma
commentary: Abhishek strikes now! Goes wide and full, it was in the slot to be hit and Stoinis looks to go over long-off only to be caught by Samad

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: N Pooran
Prabhsimran Singh
commentary: Mukesh gets Prabhsimran! Low full toss angled into leg stump. Prabhsimran tries to be too funky and looks for the reverse ramp. Does not have the room to work with and in the end, the ball pings off his pads into the stumps. Take nothing away from this sensational innings, though!

name: LS Livingstone
commentary: and he knocks Livingstone over! Length delivery slanted into middle and off stump. Livingstone comes down the track and looks to smash it into next week. Loses his shape completely, though, and gets nowhere close to the ball as it cannons into middle and off stump! Ishant waves Livingstone goodbye and PBKS are two down in the powerplay!

name: JM Sharma
commentary: Jitesh bites the dust too! Slowed up nicely on a length on off stump. Jitesh backs away and looks to go inside out. He is nowhere close to the pitch of the ball, though, and has a swish at it. The ball breezes past the bat and then thuds into the top of off stump!

name: SM Curran
shortText: c Aman Hakim Khan b Dubey
commentary: Curran perishes! Back of a length delivery on middle and leg stump that spins back into Curran and cramps him for room. He tries to advance and clear long on but just does not have enough room to work with. Splices it towards long on, where Aman does the rest!

name: Harpreet Brar
commentary: soft dismissal! Length delivery on middle and off stump. Brar is caught on the crease and then ends up offering the simplest of catches to extra cover. Not sure what Brar was trying there but either way, this PBKS experiment has fallen flat on its face!

name: M Shahrukh Khan
commentary: swing and a miss again! And Shahrukh is also run out at the bowler's end! Slower delivery on a back of a length outside off stump. Shahrukh swings like a rusty gate and does not make contact. Raza calls him through for a bye but Shahrukh is nowhere in the frame as Salt relays it to Khaleel, who takes off the bails at the bowler's end!

name: PD Salt
commentary: knocks him over! Slightly quicker and this might have been a little shorter than the previous one. This skids through, meaning that Salt, who is trying to force it through the off side again, is a tad late on the stroke. Goes past the bat and thunders into the stumps!

name: MR Marsh
commentary: huge appeal, and given! Full delivery fizzed into off stump. Marsh looks for the cute, little paddle again but this time, gets into a slightly awkward position. Does not make contact as the ball pins him on the pads. After deliberation, Marsh decides to review! The impact might be the only thing under consideration here. Nothing on UltraEdge, as expected. It is Umpire's Call on Impact and the ball is just about clipping the off stump! Marsh has to go!

name: AR Patel
commentary: one brings two, two brings three! Full delivery on middle and leg stump. Axar has a swish at it but is not quite to the pitch of the ball. It goes past the bat and pins him on the front pad. Axar has reviewed this and he would hope it has spun enough to miss leg stump. No bat involved, as expected, but Ball Tracking shows that the ball would have kissed the outside of leg stump. Umpire's Call and DC are now five down!

name: MK Pandey
commentary: and he cleans Pandey up with an absolute corker! Fired into the track on a length on middle and leg stump. The ball drifts into Pandey and he thinks he can work it to leg. The ball, though, grips and spins square past his bat. Smashes into the top of off stump and DC are in all sorts of trouble now!

name: P Dubey
commentary: Dubey misses, Ellis hits! Full, fast and this zones in on middle and leg stump. Dubey tries to tonk it into tomorrow but gets nowhere close to the ball. He is, in fact, beaten for pace and hears the death rattle soon after!

name: V Kohli
commentary: Asif takes out Kohli! Slower delivery on a length on middle and off stump. Kohli is completely flummoxed by it and after advancing, tries to reach out in front of his body. The ball spoons up in the air. Jaiswal calls early from extra cover and completes the catch! Asif is delighted, and Kohli departs for a 19-ball 18!

name: F du Plessis
commentary: Asif takes out another big fish! Off-pace delivery on a full length just outside off stump. du Plessis tries to force it past extra cover but does not quite get the timing he is after. He neither gets it past Jaiswal, nor gets it over him. Offers a simple catch off the toe-end, and this is a massive, massive moment in the game!

name: MK Lomror
commentary: he tries, but perishes! Length delivery hung outside off stump. This might have been a googly but Lomror tries to hack it away anyway. Slices it badly and Jurel, running in from deep mid wicket, completes the catch after a bit of a juggle!

name: YBK Jaiswal
commentary: straight to mid off! Siraj breaks the game open! Full and just outside off stump. Jaiswal plants his front foot and tries to go over mid off. Does not quite get under it, though, and ends up drilling it straight to Kohli at mid off! Just the start RCB were after; just what RR did not need!

name: JC Buttler
commentary: Buttler departs for a duck too, would you believe it?! Short, wide and on most days, Buttler would have thrashed it past point. Today, though, the ball just sticks in the surface enough to make Buttler lose control of the stroke. Chips it towards cover point, where Siraj does the rest!

name: SV Samson
commentary: RR are three down, oh my word! Back of a length delivery on off stump that holds in the surface again. Samson tries to swipe it away but is way too early into the stroke. The ball skews up and goes miles up into the air. Rawat calls early and does the job!

name: SO Hetmyer
commentary: Hetmyer perishes! Given just a bit of air on a length on middle and off stump. Hetmyer tries to smash it over long on but for a change, mistimes it completely. In fact, it comes off the toe-end of the bat and Bracewell has the simplest of catches at long on!

name: DC Jurel
commentary: holes out to deep mid wicket! This is a procession! Dragged down on off stump and that ought to have travelled the distance. Such is RR's plight, though, that Jurel, despite picking the length early, hits this long-hop straight down Lomror throat at deep mid wicket!

name: R Ashwin
commentary: short and wide outside off stump. Hetmyer rocks back and cuts it towards deep point. Ashwin wants the second but is sent back. He stretches out to regain his ground but Rawat has done well to flick the throw back onto the stumps. Has he done well enough, though? Looks like he has! Ashwin is just inches short of his ground when the bails come off, and RR, to add to their woes, now have a run out as well!

name: DP Conway
commentary: Thakur's golden arm strikes. A third short ball, 131ks this time. All different pace, and different bounce from the surface. This time he gets a top edge on the pull, and is caught at deep square leg

name: AM Rahane
commentary: Rahane with the right intent but his success rate against spin is not as good as against pace. He skips down the pitch looking for a straight six, but the carrom ball takes the thick outside edge for a catch to long-off

name: MM Ali
commentary: the carrom ball, and he gone right through. Moeen is watching for the offbreak, but Narine has slipped in a quicker carrom ball. Beats the inside edge, and takes off and middle. One more wicket to Dhoni's batting

name: RA Jadeja
commentary: the knowledgeable Chennai crowd roars. Jadeja has ramped a bouncer straight to short third. Now they get to see MS Dhoni for two balls

name: Rahmanullah Gurbaz
commentary: Chahar keeps finding ways to take wicket. This is a wide half-volley, just too wide, which means Gurbaz can't quite middle his square-drive, which he has no plans to keep down. There is a deep point in place, and it it going high to him. Deshpande runs to his right to complete the high catch inches inside the boundary. Had the ball been a touch straighter, Gurbaz would have made a better connection and got six

name: VR Iyer
commentary: wow. Chahar has again got a wicket in a manner you don't really plan. This is short of a length, sufficiently wide for Iyer to throw his hands at it, and he end sup playing it finer than he wants to. Caught at short third of all places

name: Shubman Gill
commentary: In the air and taken! Fuller wide of off, tries to go over covers where Samad bends forward and takes a good low catch to dismiss the centurion

name: B Sai Sudharsan
commentary: Taken! Fuller wide of off, he swings his bat at it and Natarajan goes back a few yards from backward point to complete the catch. Sai gone for 47!

name: HH Pandya
commentary: Straight to backward point! Full around off, Hardik tries to slice it in the gap for four but is caught by Tripathi

name: DA Miller
commentary: Taken! Full length around off, heaves it towards long-off, doesn't get the elevation as Markram completes an easy catch to remove his country-mate

name: R Tewatia
commentary: Excellent catch by Jansen! Slow ball, a length ball outside off, Jansen runs back a few yards from mid-on and takes the catch. Make it four wickets in four overs

name: Rashid Khan
commentary: Out for duck! Full length outside off, pace off, Rashid swings his bat hard at it with no timing, gets a thick outside edge as Klaasen collects the ball calmly to give Bhuvneshwar his fourth wicket

name: Noor Ahmad
commentary: <b>Gets a wicket but it's a run-out!</b? Fuller and wide of off, a swing and a miss from Noor. He looks to get a quick run but Klaasen throws the ball quickly to Bhuvneshwar who then effects a direct run out at the non-striker's end

name: Mohammed Shami
commentary: Five wickets for Bhuvi! How good was that? Pace off this time, short of full length pitched wide of off, Shami throws his bat at it but can't clear Jansen at long-on

name: Anmolpreet Singh
shortText: c Rashid Khan b Mohammed Shami
commentary: Top edge off the pull is in the air... Rashid runs across from deep third and takes it. Rashid trodded a few steps to his diagonal left, waited a second and took it around waist height. It was a back-of-length ball that Anmol tried to swipe across to go through square leg

name: Abhishek Sharma
commentary: Outside edge off the flashing cover drive and taken by Saha. It was a length ball that pitched around fourth stump and seamed away big time. Sharma went through with the waft and got it off the toe of the bat. Saha takes a dolly, Dayal runs and leaps to punch the air in celebration

name: AK Markram
commentary: Outside edge hovers in the air and Shanaka takes it at forward point. It was a 145kph length ball around off, Markram tried to close the face and clip the ball through mid-on. Shanaka took an easy catch and Titans' celebrations continue

name: H Klaasen
commentary: Miller doesn't spot it right away but is able to take the catch over his right shoulder while bending his knees. It was an overpitched ball that Klaasen tried to thump over long-off again but found the fielder. Shami has a four-for. Miller turned and shook his head after taking the catch, Mohit, coming in to high-five had an end-to-end smile

name: Abdul Samad
commentary: Picks the slower one and whacks it straight to Mavi at midwicket. Mohit joins his hands and looks at the heavens in celebration. Titans' playoff spot seems all but sealed. Samad was waiting on the back foot, having charged last ball. Mohit did indeed go short and Samad tried to muscle it with all his might and lost his shape. It went flat to the fielder

name: M Jansen
commentary: Launched but it's too high, Hardik settles underneath at mid-off and takes it. It was a length ball on middle, Jansen was standing just outside leg as he tried to drive it through the line, he finished with the bat way high but got it off the bottom. No big celebrations, GT know they have this game and a playoff spot almost in the bag

name: B Kumar
commentary: Rashid runs in from deep point and takes the catch, Mohit has four. It was a wobbling length ball on fourth stump, Bhuvi made room and tried to flay it over cover. It may have been a bit too short for the shot. It went off the flashing outside edge, Rashid covered quite a few metres, decelerated close to the finish and took it comfortably.

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: DJ Hooda
commentary: David makes no mistake this time. Hooda gives charge to Behrendorff but is still some distance away from the pitch of the ball. He goes for the shot, the ball hits high on the bat and goes up towards mid-on. A simple catch for David on this ocassion

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: Q de Kock
commentary: and he strikes first ball. It was a googly, pitching full around off. De Kock went for the drive only to edge it to Kishan. A mild appeal, followed by even milder celebrations after the finger goes up

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: KH Pandya
commentary: length outside off. Looks to steer it between backward point and short third but ends up it just to the left of backward point. Stoinis sends back Krunal who makes it hobbling. And he is walking out retired hurt

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: MP Stoinis

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: N Pooran

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: Ishan Kishan
commentary: Bishnoi now has both the openers! Googly on a short of a length, Kishan once again tries to use the slowness of the surface to go back and play the pull shot. Ends up heaving it aerially to Naveen-ul-Haq, who gets to his left from deep midwicket. Arms up in celebration for Bishnoi.

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: RG Sharma
commentary: And Bishnoi gets Rohit to hole out. Slower through the air this time, on a length around off and middle. Rohit gets down on a knee and slogs it over the on side. Doesn't get it off the middle and Hooda gets around from long-on and takes it comfortably.

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: SA Yadav
commentary: Thakur gets SKY! Massive strike that and he is rightly pumped up. It is bowled on a length outside off at 120.5 kph. Suryakumar gets across to play the scoop over short fine leg. But because there's not enough pace on the ball, he is through with the shot a tad early. As a result, gets a thick inside edge onto the stumps. He sits disappointed next to the stumps, before getting up and walking back.

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: N Wadhera
shortText: c sub (K Gowtham) b Mohsin Khan
commentary: Wadhera finds deep square leg! Mohsin has his arms aloft. Short ball, perhaps the wise choice given he has the longer leg side to bowl to. Wadhera sees the opportunity and pulls it. Doesn't get the distance and finds substitute Gowtham

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: TH David

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: Vishnu Vinod
commentary: Pooran, calm and collected as he can be, takes a well-judged catch! Celebrations go up in the LSG camp. Thakur banged it short, Vinod goes for the pull and seems to have connected it well. Pooran, though, has enough time at deep square leg to set himself and jumps to catch that.

venue: Himachal Pradesh Cricket Association Stadium, Dharamsala
name: PD Salt

venue: Himachal Pradesh Cricket Association Stadium, Dharamsala
name: Prabhsimran Singh
commentary: Prabh feels the pressure from Taide not doing much. Runs at Axar looking to go with the angle and over long-on. However, he gets the inside half of the bat and is caught at long-on

venue: Himachal Pradesh Cricket Association Stadium, Dharamsala
name: A Taide
commentary: low full toss again, but it is dipping, and he can't get under it. Just the single to long-on

venue: Himachal Pradesh Cricket Association Stadium, Dharamsala
name: LS Livingstone
commentary: hit straight to long-off. Gone for 94

venue: Himachal Pradesh Cricket Association Stadium, Dharamsala
name: JM Sharma
commentary: Jitesh had been set up to fail here. He has to go after every ball with no time to get in. Tries to pull, gets a top edge, and is caught by Khaleel at long leg

venue: Himachal Pradesh Cricket Association Stadium, Dharamsala
name: M Shahrukh Khan
commentary: mis-hit by Shahrukh. Straight to long-on. If he had responded to Livingstone and taken the second, Livingstone would have been on strike and wouldn't probably have hit a length ball straight down the throat of long-on. Sloppy batting from PBKS batters other than Livingstone

venue: Himachal Pradesh Cricket Association Stadium, Dharamsala
name: SM Curran
commentary: shuffles across early, I am expecting him to move back but he doesn't. Nortje goes full and straight on the leg stump. Knocks the leg peg out

venue: Himachal Pradesh Cricket Association Stadium, Dharamsala
name: Harpreet Brar
commentary: no he is not looking for the single. The short ball is outside off, and beats his waft. Livingstone rushes through for a bye, makes it in with a dive, but Brar doesn't. Nortje has run him our

venue: Himachal Pradesh Cricket Association Stadium, Dharamsala
name: RD Chahar

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: Abhishek Sharma
commentary: Loosener is slapped straight to Lomror at cover point. It was short and wide at 92kph, Abhishek went back and tried to cut it hard. The ball looped off the bat without gaining elevation, Lomror went on one knee and took a low catch. A dejected Abhishek walks back.

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: AK Markram
commentary: Markram misses the reverse and Shahbaz hits the off pole. He's chuffed with his comeback. Length ball around off straightened a bit, Markram tried to rise with the ball and lifted his back leg as he lost balance. He noticed the ball was too close and tried to close the gap between his wrists and his torso but missed it completely and the stump was rattled.

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: H Klaasen
commentary: Dipping yorker beats Klaasen's swing, hits the base of middle. Harshal turns back and applauds. Brilliant innings. He leaves to a rousing reception from the crowd and team-mates alike. Slower one, Klaasen tried to make room as he tried to swipe it across

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: V Kohli
commentary: Hundred and out, Kohli's skied a pull and it's taken by Phillips at deep forward square. It was head-high bouncer that Kohli tried to launch in front of square. Phillips was well within the cushion. Kohli walks back with a smile into the dugout

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: F du Plessis
commentary: Slow and full, Faf tries to launch it away but holes out at long-off. He screamed in agony right after hitting it. Swiped across and saucered it high. Tripathi falls back on the ground with the ball nestled in front of his chest.

venue: Himachal Pradesh Cricket Association Stadium, Dharamsala
name: Prabhsimran Singh
commentary: poked back to the bowler! Boult leaps to his right in his followthrough, closes his fingers around a popped chance, in the air for an eternity. A full-length inswinger, stopping on the batter and Prabhsimran's hard hands do the rest. A huge scalp in his first over, yet again, for Boult!

venue: Himachal Pradesh Cricket Association Stadium, Dharamsala
name: A Taide
commentary: too easy at midwicket! A soft dismissal, but Taide's beans were pumping. Back of a length, and spannered to Padikkal on the edge of the circle, high on the bat so little power through the pull. A timely wicket for RR after a rowdy few overs

venue: Himachal Pradesh Cricket Association Stadium, Dharamsala
name: LS Livingstone
commentary: swings for the hills, stumps splattered! And a chastising exit for Livingstone, after his recent heroics. Never at the races tonight, and lost patience with that attempted launch across the line. Kings in a hole now

venue: Himachal Pradesh Cricket Association Stadium, Dharamsala
name: SM Curran

venue: Himachal Pradesh Cricket Association Stadium, Dharamsala
name: JM Sharma
commentary: slower ball, straight up in the air! Oh no, that's curtains for Jitesh. A tricky catch, tracking back in the covers from the sub fielder Ferreira, but well judged to dent Kings' hopes of that grandstand finish

venue: Himachal Pradesh Cricket Association Stadium, Dharamsala
name: M Shahrukh Khan

venue: Himachal Pradesh Cricket Association Stadium, Dharamsala
name: YBK Jaiswal
commentary: A difficult shot to play and get out! Ellis gets Jaiswal. It is s low full toss on middle and off, he looks to play the switch-hit over the off side. Gets it off the middle of the bat to Rishi Dhawan at backward point. Either side of that fielder or above him it would have been four.

venue: Himachal Pradesh Cricket Association Stadium, Dharamsala
name: JC Buttler
commentary: Pinned in front! No second though from the umpire and he raises the dreaded finger immediately. Spears it on a length outside off, angling in on off and middle. Buttler's feet are cemented as he stands on off to flick it, almost falls over. Misses the ball and is hit right on the pads. Rabada and the Kings are ecstatic. A hat-trick of ducks in IPL 2023 for Buttler.

venue: Himachal Pradesh Cricket Association Stadium, Dharamsala
name: SV Samson
commentary: Catching practice to deep square leg! Nicely tossed up, full on the pads. Samson gets his front foot down and looks to heave this nicely over deep square leg. Flicks this right down Rishi Dhawan, the sub's throat in the deep. Chahar fold his hands in celebration.

venue: Himachal Pradesh Cricket Association Stadium, Dharamsala
name: SO Hetmyer
commentary: Shikhar Dhawan has pulled off a stunner, or has he? He dives forward to catch that and then slaps his thigh in celebration. The umpires would want to have another look. Curran bowls it outside off, Hetmyer slaps it aerially towards cover, where Dhawan has his fingers under the ball.

venue: Himachal Pradesh Cricket Association Stadium, Dharamsala
name: R Parag
commentary: Rabada has the last laugh. Nails the yorker on middle and leg, he looks to whip it away but gets a leading edge to backward point. Taide makes no mistake and Parag walks back dejected.

venue: Himachal Pradesh Cricket Association Stadium, Dharamsala
name: DC Jurel

venue: Himachal Pradesh Cricket Association Stadium, Dharamsala
name: TA Boult
DP Conway
shortText: c Aman Hakim Khan b Nortje
commentary: Conway bites the dust too! Slower delivery on a length on off stump. This one just holds in the surface a touch and Conway does not get the timing he is after. Slices it and Aman runs to his right at long on and completes the catch comfortably! Another excellent Conway knock, though - it has set the foundation for another massive first-innings score!

name: S Dube
commentary: caught at long on! Slot ball on middle and leg stump, and Dube could have hit that anywhere. But on this occasion, he swings a little too early and gets it right off the bottom of the blade. The ball spoons up towards long on, where Lalit does the rest! Dube has done his job, though!

name: PD Salt
commentary: Salt falls! Cutter on a length just outside off stump and Salt's struggles come to an end. He tries to force it over the off side but because of the lack of pace, he can only slice it straight to extra cover! Chahar has a smile on his face but more importantly, he has another powerplay wicket for CSK!

name: AR Patel
commentary: Chahar gets Axar! Slower bumper outside off stump and this one just does not arrive. Axar has a swipe at it and tries to muscle it over long on but the lack of pace means that he picks out Gaikwad to perfection! DC slip further!

name: Kuldeep Yadav
commentary: and Kuldeep bites the dust! Full delivery on middle and leg stump that nips back sharply. Kuldeep has no clue about it and plays down the wrong line. The ball thuds into the front pad and the umpire raises his finger! Kuldeep has reviewed this but he is already walking back to the pavilion! No bat involved, and as expected, the ball would have smashed into the stumps! Theekshana is now on a hat-trick!

name: KS Sharma
commentary: First wicket. A short ball angling towards the bower. Karan goes for the pull but the ball hits him high on the bat and goes straight up. Shardul takes a couple of steps forward from short midwicket and settles under it

name: Q de Kock
commentary: De Kock departs as well. It was full around off, a googly. De Kock went for the slog sweep but got more height on it than distance. Russell moved to his left from deep midwicket, settled under it, and completed the catch by falling on his back

name: MP Stoinis
commentary: another short ball, much nastier than the previous one. Much higher as well. Stoinis once again fends at it, gets a glove and the ball lobs towards the flying slip where Iyer takes an easy catch

name: KH Pandya
commentary: Narine strikes in his first over. Bowled at 99.6kph, on a length around leg stump. Krunal goes for the slog sweep but fails to middle it. Rinku runs in to his left and takes the catch near deep square leg

name: A Badoni
commentary: Narine bowls this one wide outside off. Badoni looks to repeat the shot but gets a top-edge and Shardul from deep midwicket moves to his left to take the catch

name: N Pooran
commentary: full but further outside off this time. Pooran throws his bat at it but fails to middle and holes out to wide third man

name: Ravi Bishnoi
commentary: a length ball around off. Bishnoi swings across, misses, and the ball hits the top of the off stump

name: VR Iyer
commentary: chipped to extra cover! Simple catch for Bishnoi. Iyer looked to punch at this ball, which was short and turning away. But looked like the stopped before coming on to him, even as it bounced comfortably enough for a back-foot punch. Anyway, Gowtham strikes after a rapid powerplay for KKR

name: N Rana
commentary: ballooned to extra cover! That is 100 T20 wickets for Bishnoi too. Even so, signs of the ball stopping on the pitch before coming on to the batter. So Nitish is beaten by the slowness of the pitch, as much as by the delivery itself. It was shortish outside off, and turning away while bouncing considerably. Nitish half-pulled and half-poked, as the ball took off from the top edge

name: Rahmanullah Gurbaz
shortText: c Ravi Bishnoi b Yash Thakur
commentary: juggling catch by Bishnoi! One, two and in at the third attempt. He was placed at point, where Gurbaz skied the ball after trying to loft at this length ball which was slightly wide of off. But Thakur had taken pace off there, and so Gurbaz was beaten. There was no timing, as the ball flew up for Bishnoi to gobble it eventually

name: AD Russell
commentary: the off stump is gone! Russell was eyeing the leg-side boundary this time, hoicking hard across the line but getting beaten completely. Bishnoi pulled his length back to go on a short of a length around off, as the ball shaped to turn in from outside off. Russell swung early, and had to walk back

name: SN Thakur
commentary: taken at deep backward square! The longer boundary comes into play, and Thakur fails to clear it. It was a short delivery pitching on middle, and climbing nicely after pitching. Thakur looked to pull, but the bounce got the better of him, as he almost swivelled while pulling. The ball got high on to his bat, and flew towards the fielder

name: SP Narine
commentary: low full toss, angling into off. Sliced to point, who gets a hand while diving to his right. And, and... there is a quick throw to the keeper, who breaks the stumps quickly. LSG appeal for run-out, and the third umpire finds Narine well short. The ball had dribbled towards deep third after tickling the point fielder's hands, as the throw arrived bang close to the wicket

name: MA Agarwal
commentary: Edge off the pull goes to the keeper. Mayank is disappointed, looks up to the sky as he walks back. Madhwal points his finger up in celebration. It was a shortish ball dug into the pitch, angling away over off stump. Mayank cleared his front leg and tried to lump it across the line. Got an outside edge and the keeper took a dolly

name: H Klaasen
commentary: Through the cross--batted heave and rattles timber. Length ball angled in after landing in line with off stump. Klaasen tried to launch it across, over square leg. Missed it completely. Rohit is jubilant right after

name: Ishan Kishan
commentary: Kishan balloons a pull, Brook dives at midwicket and takes it Shortish ball just outside off had Kishan swivel to try and power it. It hit low on the sticker and flew to the left of Brook, who dove across and took a good, low catch

name: RG Sharma
shortText: c Nitish Kumar Reddy b Dagar
commentary: Nitish flies across at point and takes a mid-air blinder! Rohit made room and tried to carve it off a length but it ballooned off a thick outside edge. Nitish went across to the right and threw himself, reached out with his right hand and, somehow, it stuck. He caught it mid-air and hid the ball close to the chest to ensure it didn't pop out after he landed

name: F du Plessis
commentary: huge appeal for the catch at slip! And Faf knows he's edged that. A wild swipe, and it looked as though it cannoned off the bottom of his bat, past the off stump, into the keeper's right knee and up into the hands of Tewatia. The Chinnaswamy is silenced!

name: MK Lomror
commentary: dragged out of the crease, and Saha whips off the bails! Lomror went for a gallop, Noor fired it past the pads at pace, that's an excellent pick-up and take-down. RCB are faltering now!

name: V Shankar
commentary: now taken at deep midwicket! Kohli there had to go low to his right while grabbing that. It was on the shorter side of a length around middle, as Shankar looked to keep smashing and clearing the boundary. But this seemed to have come off the inside half of the bat, and flew to the fielder

name: MD Shanaka
commentary: Harshal strikes! Shanaka finds deep midwicket too. Change of pace from Harshal, who went fullish and outside off. Shanaka seemed to be beaten by the lack of pace, and hoicked across the line. But the ball took off from what seemed like very close to the inside edge, and kept flying towards deep midwicket. Simple catch for sub fielder Prabhudessai

name: DA Miller
shortText: c sub (SS Prabhudessai) b Mohammed Siraj
commentary: sliced to deep point! RCB keep striking. Prabhudessai, stationed right at the edge of the rope, holds it reverse cupped over his face. Miller's stay was short, and he didn't get going at all. This ball was quite full - following on from a good length ball - and wide of off, while also shaping away. Miller opened the bat face and lofted, only to find the fielder on the rope

name: DP Conway
shortText: c Rashid Khan b Mohammed Shami
commentary: Conway's struggle ends with a catch to midwicket. Slot ball, closer to length than half-volley, but very much hittable from fourth stump. Conway tried to whip it across the line, it ballooned and parachuted into Rashid's hands.

name: S Dube
commentary: Dube's leg stump is knocked over! Noor sends across the goog on a fullish length, lands around off and spins in. Dube tries to launch it over wide long-on and misses it completely. Two wickets in successive overs.

name: AM Rahane
commentary: Another soft dismissal off Nalkande's bowling, no no-ball this time. No celebrations from the bowler this time. It was a length outside off that Rahane tried to steer off the back foot with an open-faced bat. Ended up spooning it to backward point

name: RA Jadeja
commentary: Slower one beats the heave, stumps are rattled! It was a slot ball but the cutter snuck in. Jadeja cleared his front leg out the way and went for an almighty heave across the line. Tried to go square, missed it completely.

name: MS Dhoni
commentary: Slower one is ballooned to cover and the volume goes from 100 to 0 as Hardik nonchalantly takes it around head-height and throws it towards the bowler. It was wide around a length and MS reached out and tried to slap it aerially

name: Shubman Gill
commentary: Chahar has got his man! This was a short ball at only 111kph. Gill got set for the pull but he's a bit too early into the shot. Ball in the air and Conway at deep square leg runs around to grab that catch. The crowd erupts, Chahar looks up to the skies.

name: HH Pandya
commentary: and that protection pays off! Forced Hardik to go over the infield because it was so packed. Theekshana with a length ball on a fourth-stump line. Hardik miscues the cut. Jadeja at point jumps up and holds on. Theekshana with a passionate celebration.

name: MD Shanaka
commentary: reverse sweep doesn't work! Two dots and then the wicket. Shanaka tries to lift the reverse sweep from middle stump, but he doesn't time it well. He got tangled slightly too, and Theekshana runs to his right from conventional short-third to take an easy catch. In the end, the Shanaka experiment doesn't really work out

name: DA Miller
commentary: Jaddu-gar He is a magician. Earns his reward for bowling stump-to-stump. Ball skids straight through with Miller kinda late on the punch with minimal footwork. Ball squirts through the bat-body gap after taking an edge, and then turns around to see his stumps rattled.

name: V Shankar
commentary: Gaik-waddafielder! Full ball around leg stump. Shankar flicked it high in the air off his toes. Gaikwad runs in quickly from deep midwicket and then puts his body on the line with a dive forward. Clean catch, Shankar is outta here!

name: R Tewatia
commentary: let's start the whistle-podu! Theekshana with a second and brings out his trademark celebration. Good length ball around middle and off. Tewatia tries to guide it to the leg side but this 103kph delivery goes past his edge.

name: Rashid Khan
commentary: MS magic again? Dhoni strengthens the off side before this ball, moving Conway towards deep point, and the ball has gone straight to him. Another low full toss outside off and Rashid sliced the shot straight to him. Nehra gives Rashid a pat on the back as he walks back for a 16-ball 30

name: DG Nalkande
commentary: run-out for a golden duck! Full ball around off. Drilled to mid off too hard and still he tries the single. Sub fielder Senapati with a clean pick up and throw from mid-off, and Nalkande is a few metres out of the crease.

name: Mohammed Shami
commentary: A tenth IPL final for CSK in 14 seasons of their history. And what a way to seal it, with a catch running square from mid off. Short ball around off, slapped high up in the air, and mid-off was committed for the catch even though the win was decided. One-handed from Chahar and then pulls up, maybe a strain

name: Ishan Kishan
commentary: Thakur strikes with his second ball.M Banged in short, and angling across the left-hander. Kishan looks to cut it over backward point but manages only an edge to Pooran. Both openers are back in the hut

name: RG Sharma
commentary: 136.5kph. A length ball bowled from wide of the crease, Rohit jumps out of his crease and looks to go over extra cover but fails to middle it and ends up hitting it straight to Badoni. Naveen does shut-out-the-noise celebration

name: SA Yadav
commentary: The slower one does the trick. A legcutter bowled at 107.3kph, on the fuller side of good length. Surya looks to hit down the ground but the lack of pace means he ends up playing a touch early and chipping it instead of hitting it. Gowtham from long-off runs to his left to gobble it up

name: NT Tilak Varma
commentary: 105.3kph. Slower ball. Length outside off, and Varma flat-bats it towards long-off, just to the left of Hooda. Hooda charges in and slides to complete the catch

name: TH David
commentary: a high full toss outside off. David goes for the slog but ends up hitting it to long-on where Hooda pouches it. But the umpires want to send it upstairs to check the height. Touch-and-go, and the TV umpire thinks it would have been just below the waist height if the batter wasn't crouching. David isn't happy, though

name: N Wadhera
shortText: c Ravi Bishnoi b Yash Thakur
commentary: a wide full toss. Wadhera throws his bat at it but ends up slicing it somewhat. Bishnoi at deep extra cover takes it comfortably

name: KR Mayers
commentary: lofted to mid-on! Looked like he was done in by the skiddy nature of the ball. Jordan bowled a cross-seam ball on the shorter side of a length just outside off. The ball hinted to angle in, while also bouncing considerably. Mayers looked to pull, but got it off the higher side of the ball. Easy catch for Green on the edge of the circle

name: KH Pandya
commentary: jumps out of the crease... but finds the long-on fielder! That went quite high, but didn't have timing. The time-out breaks LSG's momentum. It was a full delivery from Chawla, enticing the batter to go for the big hit. That's what Pandya did by stepping out early, only to end up lofting it right to the fielder

name: MP Stoinis
commentary: Stoinis is run-out! This could be the game. The two batters had collided in the middle of the pitch after both were busy watching the ball. They went for a second run there, and bumped into each other, before Stoinis tried to make his way back to the striker's end. The throw from long-on was there to the keeper by then, and the stumps were whipped. It was a full ball on off - change of pace too - as Green chipped it down

name: A Badoni
commentary: the off stump is gone! Three dots in a row forces Badoni to swipe across the line, and he is beaten again. Good length ball on off stump, which is angled into the batter. But the ball looked like going straight on after pitching, as Badoni pulled and missed. LSG losing their way in the middle overs

name: N Pooran
commentary: tickled to the keeper! Pooran goes back for a golden duck. Pooran. The man who has carried LSG so far. No run. LSG in trouble. Madhwal on a hat-trick. This time it is the seam movement that gets him a wicket. Madhwal is on top. He bowled on a length from around the wicket, angling it into the left-hander. The ball landed on the seam, and moved away just enough to entice Pooran to have a poke. He obliged, and ended up edging it

name: DJ Hooda
commentary: third run-out of the innings! And Green and Rohit are involved again. LSG's hurt going deeper and deeper. They both ran to the striker's end, and ended up providing MI another wicket. Now this is what happened: Naveen guided this good length ball outside off with an open face of the bat, as Green - at backward point - dived to his left to stop that. He threw at the keeper's end, by which time both batters were there. Madhwal, who had reached there by then, threw to Rohit at the bowler's end

name: Ravi Bishnoi
commentary: fourth wicket for Madhwal! Bishnoi swipes one right to the long-on fielder. Madhwal bowled on a good length around fifth stump. Bishnoi, for all his might, looked to hoicked across the line. But the ball seemed to have skidded through, and was a bit too quick for his liking. In the end, a comfortable catch for the fielder

name: Shubman Gill
commentary: Madhwal has Gill holing out. End of a sensational innings from Gill. Madhwal goes for another yorker. Speared on off stump, just short of a yorker length, shovelled in the air. He can't clear the outfielder. No mistake from David this time. Gill walks off to a standing ovation. The incoming Hardik wraps Gill up in a hug

name: B Sai Sudharsan
commentary: full-toss outside off, Madhwal chases Sai who was backing away. Sai misses the scoop and wears it on his pads

name: RG Sharma
commentary: Top edge off the flick goes right up... Little settles underneath and takes it. Back-of-length angling into the body, Rohit tried to help it along behind square, he was cramped for room and maybe beaten for pace too. Little did well to keep his eyes on the ball

name: N Wadhera
commentary: Wadhera is caught behind trying to pull. Hard-length ball outside off, Wadhera stood leg side and tried to whack it square. Didn't move his feet, the ball hopped off the edge and ballooned to the keeper.

name: SA Yadav
commentary: Leg stump is on the ground, is that the game? Mohit joins his hand in prayer, SKY grins, closes his eyes and has head facing the heavens for a while. It was a fullish ball, SKY went across, his back leg was braced as he tried to paddle it fine. Missed it completely.

name: NT Tilak Varma
commentary: Slog sweep beaten, middle and off stump rattled. Tilak's short, spicy stay at the crease ends. It was on a legbreak on a fullish length that came in, Tilak was on one knee and tried to muscle it again. With Rashid's stump-to-stump bowling, there was no getting away with missing. He curses his lungs out on his way back.

name: Vishnu Vinod
commentary: Spooned straight to cover, Mohit has a two-for in his first over. Length ball just outside off sticks in the pitch a bit, Vinod is early on the punch, can't check his shot. Soft dismissal is completed with Pandya taking a dolly.

name: TH David
commentary: Rashid's celebrappeal for lbw is upheld, David reviews immediately. David went back deep in his crease, his back leg was outside off and front leg way down leg. He crouched as he tried to pull it. The ball was on the shorter side and was the topspinner. Is there an underedge? No, this looks dead. Yup, three reds it is. David goes.

name: Shubman Gill
commentary: Has Jadeja got rid of Gill? Yes, indeed. Fast turn for Jadeja. Flatter and breaks away outside off, the fuller length draws Gill forward and the turn takes it past the outside edge. Dhoni collects the ball and whips the bails off in a flash. Bam! Gill can't drag his back leg in time as he loses his balance. CSK strike after the powerplay. 300 T20 dismissals for Dhoni in his 250th IPL game

name: B Sai Sudharsan
commentary: Sai has been given out lbw. This looks out. He goes for a review. Just short of a yorker length and slings in to ping the front pad just above the boot. Struck in front of middle. The tracker returns three reads. Sai has to go. Nevertheless, this was a sublime innings. The incoming Tewatia Rashid taps his gloves

name: Rashid Khan
commentary: Holes out to long-on last ball. Slot ball outside off, but Rashid can't clear the outfielder. Rashid clears his front leg and hits it in the air. Gaikwad makes no mistake

name: DP Conway
commentary: Noor bags his second of the over. What an over. Back of a length, and this one turns away from Conway, and it stops on him as well. Lets out a yell of frustration after mishitting it straight down long-off's throat. Was probably looking to hit it over extra-cover off the back foot, not an easy shot to play at all

name: AM Rahane
commentary: gone. Mohit's lack of pace gets another wicket. Back of a length, outside off, it's there for the slash over point in everry way except it's the back-of-the-hand slowie. Stops on Rahane, and he ends up punching it in the air and straight to the man at deep point

name: MS Dhoni
commentary: and he's gone first ball! He was out caught at extra-cover in Qualifer 1, and he's out caught at extra-cover in the final. Full, it's there to drive over extra-cover, but he doesn't get the elevation he wants, and the ball ends up in the safe hands of David Miller. What a moment

name: V Kohli
commentary: tiki-taka catch between Rahane and Ravindra at midwicket. Looks like a slightly slower ball, which is why Kohli has not got all on this pull, and Rahane takes the diving catch at deep midwicket and lobs it to Ravindra before sliding into the boundary cushions

name: F du Plessis
commentary: The Fizz strikes in his first over this IPL. The cutter from Mustafizur. Bowls at 130ks, on a length, outside off, du Plessis early into it, and ends up miscuing it to deep point

name: RM Patidar
commentary: double strike for the Fizz. He has been done in by pace again. A Test-match dismissal. On a good length, wobble-seam, just going with the angle. No feet from Patidar, slightly late on it, edges it through

name: Anuj Rawat
commentary: wide yorker again, DK has shuffled across so it won't be called. Rawat tries to steal a bye, and Dhoni catches him short with a direct hit. "You still got it"

name: R Ravindra
commentary: He's gone now! One shot too many for Rachin. Was a length ball on middle and leg. He sweeps eyeing another six, but doesn't connect as cleanly. Deep backward square leg settles himself and takes a comfortable catch. Lovely debut for Rachin who falls for a 15-ball 37.

name: AM Rahane
commentary: Excellent catch by Maxwell at square leg and Rahane's sprightly knock ends. Back of a length ball but the line was crucial here. It was outside off, which meant Rahane had to fetch it and he could not control his pull. Maxwell is a few steps in from the boundary. But he adjusts well, and takes a reverse-cupped catch over his head

venue: Maharaja Yadavindra Singh International Cricket Stadium, Mullanpur
name: MR Marsh
commentary: wide on the crease, wide half-volley, Marsh square-drives, but finds short cover-point to perfection. Arshdeep has got lucky with this one

venue: Maharaja Yadavindra Singh International Cricket Stadium, Mullanpur
name: SD Hope
commentary: Rabada comes back. This length ball, bowled at 141ks, might have just stopped a touch on him and Hope almost plays a check-drive straight and low to short cover

venue: Maharaja Yadavindra Singh International Cricket Stadium, Mullanpur
name: RR Pant
commentary: Patel makes up for the drop catch. And again with the slower short ball. Bowled at 123ks, Pant looks to ramp it behind square, but the lack of pace means he ends up lobbing a catch to backward point. Pant is furious with himself

venue: Maharaja Yadavindra Singh International Cricket Stadium, Mullanpur
name: T Stubbs
commentary: looks for the inside-out chip over extra cover, but again the lack of turn for Chahar perhaps means he ends up hitting it much straighter than desired. Shashank makes a good run to his left from long-off and completes a diving catch. And credit where it's due: this ball does drift in to hit him on the inner half of the bat

venue: Maharaja Yadavindra Singh International Cricket Stadium, Mullanpur
name: AR Patel
commentary: Close call on the run-out. The third umpire comes in. Short of a length, slower ball, angling in, Axar bunts it into the leg side, wants a second to keep strike but the substitute fielder Tanay's throw from deep square is on the money. Axar is caught short. No dive either

venue: Maharaja Yadavindra Singh International Cricket Stadium, Mullanpur
name: Sumit Kumar
commentary: now Arsh goes full, 134ks, seam slanting towards slip, and there is a bit of movement in the air to take the edge on the attempted big hit from Sumit

venue: Maharaja Yadavindra Singh International Cricket Stadium, Mullanpur
name: Abishek Porel

venue: Maharaja Yadavindra Singh International Cricket Stadium, Mullanpur
name: Kuldeep Yadav
commentary: finally tries the yorker, ends u bowling a low full toss, hit down to long-on for one. They try the second but Kuldeep ends up sacrificing himself

venue: Maharaja Yadavindra Singh International Cricket Stadium, Mullanpur
name: JM Bairstow
commentary: Has Ishant tipped the ball in follow-through to run Bairstow out? Yeah, poor YJB. This was more unlucky though and he resigns with a smile while walking back. Was a slow ball into the pitch to which Prabhsimran charged and cuffed with a cross-ish bat. It poured along the pitch, Ishant went low to the right and got just a fingertip to it. Smart replays with multiple angles confirms and it and YJB, diving back, couldn't make it in time.

venue: Maharaja Yadavindra Singh International Cricket Stadium, Mullanpur
name: Prabhsimran Singh
commentary: Kuldeep gives it air and reaps rewards. Googly at 83ks is full and outside off, Prabhsimran tried to thrash it over the fielder but found Warner at long-on. Looked to have dipped a bit

venue: Maharaja Yadavindra Singh International Cricket Stadium, Mullanpur
name: SM Curran
commentary: Heaves across the line, Curran misses but not Khaleel. Offcutter on a length, lands outside off and comes back in. Curran was too early on the shot and it hit top of off. Preity Zinta applauds and is all smiles as Curran walks back

venue: Maharaja Yadavindra Singh International Cricket Stadium, Mullanpur
name: JM Sharma
commentary: Another appeal for stumping. Pant is all smiles. Is Jitesh walking? Sent upstairs. Was a length ball down leg, Jitesh went for the expansive reverse sweep. He missed it and was mid-jump and out of the crease. Meanwhile, Pant lunged low, stretched his upper body as far forward as he could and pushed the ball into the stumps. That oughta reaffirm thoughts around his keeping

venue: Maharaja Yadavindra Singh International Cricket Stadium, Mullanpur
name: LS Livingstone

venue: Maharaja Yadavindra Singh International Cricket Stadium, Mullanpur
name: Shashank Singh
commentary: Shashank has been bounced out first ball, Khaleel on a hat-trick. Pacy and at the armpit, the batter was done for pace and cramped for room as he tried to pull. Ball popped off the glove for Pant to take a dolly. Khaleel and Pant share a glance of understanding, this seems to be a plan coming off

venue: Maharaja Yadavindra Singh International Cricket Stadium, Mullanpur
name: Harpreet Brar
PD Salt
commentary: Salt swings to leg, but holes out to Jansen in the deep! Salt has shaken his last after giving the KKR fans a taste. Stepped away as Markande pushed one down the leg side, didn't quite get hold of his shot - but it still needed good work from Jansen running around to his right and diving full length for the catch

name: SP Narine
commentary: banged into the deck, Salt fends it away to backward point... and Narine has run himself out looking for one! Direct hit from Shahbaz Ahmed and he's nowhere in the frame! Salt had no intention of running there but Narine got about halfway down before noticing. Barely bothered to try and make his ground, with Jansen lurking to complete the run-out if needed

name: VR Iyer
commentary: larruped... straight to cover! Bit of a slot ball, really, and Iyer was looking to cash in with a hard-handed drive. But he couldn't have picked out the fielder better, Jansen staying low to clutch the ball to his chest. Second wicket of the evening for SRH

name: SS Iyer
commentary: chipped to mid-off! Superb take above his head by Cummins, and SRH have taken a third wicket inside the powerplay. Full from Natarajan and Shreyas was looking to loft it over the off side, almost cleared the man on the edge of the ring... but his opposite number is a tall bloke and reels in the catch. First down payment on that INR 20.5 crore fee from Cummins

name: N Rana
commentary: googly, Rana reverses... straight to backward point! Simple head-height catch for Tripathi and Rana can only offer a rueful smile as he walks off. Funky stuff fails to land, Markande strikes with his third ball. KKR slip deeper into the hole

name: Ramandeep Singh
commentary: chipped to cover, Cummins strikes! Hit high on the bat as he looked to drive on the up, well held by Markande diving across to his right. Ramandeep won't be taking this deep. The KKR recovery takes a hit

name: MA Agarwal
commentary: taken at deep backward square leg! Harshit offers a stare to Agarwal, who walks back, but not without looking back at Harshit! Short delivery at sharp pace around off, as he gets good bounce. Agarwal pulls but off the higher side of the bat. The longer side of the boundary comes into play, as Rinku takes it low

name: Abhishek Sharma
commentary: taken at fine leg! The man in the deep had to move a few steps to his left, and end at where an imaginary straightish fine leg would have been. Russell dug one a short at Abhishek, with the ball climbing nicely towards the batter's throat on middle and leg. He went for the pull, but top-edged it, with the extra pace getting the ball to zip through off the pitch

name: AK Markram
commentary: nabbed at deep midwicket! He had to take a few steps forward, and then bend to take it low. Rinku was the fielder there. Varun bowled full and on the stumps, as Markram went slog sweeping across the line, but ended up playing down the longer side of the ground. He departs, as SRH's required rate keeps rising

name: H Klaasen
shortText: c Suyash Sharma b Harshit Rana
commentary: great catch by Suyash! Someone listen to the deafening roar at Eden. Suyash, placed at short third, had to half-turn around while moving back and keeping his eyes on the ball. It arrived on him over his right shoulder, and he tumbled to take it. Another slower ball from Harshit, as he rolls his fingers over it. Bowls on a hittable length, though, and just outside off. Klaasen has a go at it aiming for the leg side, but plays early and gets it off a top edge

name: Abdul Samad
commentary: straight to deep midwicket! The fielder was placed right on the edge of the rope, and that arrived at a comfortable height, in front of his stomach. It was a full toss which was dipping just outside off, as Samad wasn't able to time his swing. He waited for a few seconds to confirm if it wasn't an above-waist no-ball or not, before heading back. But he's asked to wait, as the umpires take a look again. And replays confirm it arrived at him just below the waist, so it is not a no-ball

name: Shahbaz Ahmed
shortText: c SS Iyer b Harshit Rana
commentary: finds long-on! The crowd at Eden suddenly wakes up from sleep. Shahbaz falls, leaving this on the knife's edge. Harshit went on the shorter side of a length, while angling it across. The ball arrived around middle and off, as Shahbaz shaped to launch it down the ground but mistimed the shot for a simple catch for Shreyas

name: YBK Jaiswal
commentary: Clothed to mid-on and Jaiswal's distraught. Mohsin has shelled a few dollars but goes pace-on, into the pitch to hurry the in-form youngster. He went though with the shot but could only pop it straight to the man. The pitch has been on the slower side so far but this ball steered away from that trend.

name: JC Buttler
commentary: Nicked off on the flashing cover drive. Naveen is pumped! Excellent low catch from Rahul too. Was very full, there for the drive, but it's never as straightforward with Naveen's angles. Delivered from wide of the crease, it came in towards fifth stump, was coming back in. Buttler buckled his back knee and tried muscling it. The sound of the nick was like a stick breaking, it hovered low to Rahul's right. He was wrong-footed, as the first step was to the leg side, but he then flung himself low and timed the gather well.

name: R Parag
commentary: Tries doing something similar but holes out to deep-backward square. Was a shortish ball, 119ks, just outside off, Parag tried dragging it across with a pull, top edged it and Hooda took a dolly. Excellent 93-run stand though

name: SO Hetmyer
commentary: Appeal, given. Hetmyer reviews. Think it was caught behind even though Rahul dislodged the stumps while lifting his hand in celebration. Length ball landed in line and turned away, the lid-less Hetmyer's front foot was well in front of the crease and he tried to defend with a straight bat. Hawk-Eye shows there was a feather, much to Hetmyer's surprise. Rahul and Bishnoi are delighted, LSG are pulling it back

name: Q de Kock
commentary: taken at fine leg! This is pretty much a gift from de Kock for Boult, who offers a wry smile. Boult bowled this on the fuller side of a length, angling it in and pitching outside leg. De Kock went clipping it with the line, only to find the fielder positioned bang where he hit it. Burger took it with a reverse cup to get into the action right away

name: KL Rahul
commentary: taken at deep point! The pendulum swings late into the game. Sandeep bowls a wide line to Rahul, with the length being there to be hit. But Rahul went lofting far away from his body. Jurel had to take a few steps forward to catch that, and he did well in the end to send Rahul back

name: A Badoni
commentary: spooned to mid-off! Buttler sees the ball dying on to him, and takes a low catch. Burger leaving Royals' mouths spicy already. He bowled on a length, while shaping it across the right-hander and arriving outside off. Badoni went lofting seeing the length, but couldn't time his shot, and found the man inside the circle

name: DJ Hooda
commentary: nabbed at deep midwicket! Chahal strikes straight away. Jurel was actually placed a good few steps into the field of play, and had to take some steps back to gobble it in front of his stomach. Chahal tossed one up to invite Hooda, with the ball being fullish and outside off. Hooda skipped down and heaved, but it came off the inside half of the bat

name: MP Stoinis
commentary: finds Jurel at deep midwicket! Ashwin went with the carrom ball, on a length on middle and leg. Stoinis had his front leg out of the way, and went hammering for a big hit. But Jurel ended up grabbing another catch, as he came a few steps forward

name: Shubman Gill
commentary: this is why IPL teams still back Piyush Chawla! He has the knack of taking wickets and he has delivered with a massive scalp. Chawla lets a quicker one rip, and Gill comes down the track to hit him over long on. But Gill's shot doesn't have the distance and Rohit takes a simple catch on the line

name: B Sai Sudharsan
commentary: full ball on middle, and he flicks it well to the leg side. In fact, too well! Straight to the fielder from deep backward square leg running in. A second diving catch from Tilak tonight

name: Azmatullah Omarzai
commentary: holes out at deep midwicket! And Coetzee celebrates in his trademark fashion with a big roar. Cross-seamed good length ball on off and Omarzai attempts to slog a shot over cow corner. But the leg-side is the bigger side over here at the Narendra Modi Stadium and Tilak composes himself in the deep to close out the catch

name: DA Miller
commentary: easy for Hardik at mid off running back! Bumrah starts off with the slower ball, fullish on off. Miller wants to loft it over mid off but then cannot change his momentum. Meets the ball too early and the ball pops off the bat's face and lobs towards mid off

name: R Tewatia
commentary: a stunning catch at deep square leg diving forward! Naman Dhir the fielder there. Coetzee bowls a shin-high full toss around leg and Tewatia tried to dropkick a shot over the leg side. But Dhir covers a lot of ground running in and commits to the forward drive! "I can see the fingers underneath," says the TV umpire, so Tewatia must go

name: Ishan Kishan
commentary: Has he nicked it? He has! Pulls his length back and pitches it around middle and off. The ball is going away from Kishan who pokes at it and gets a feather through to Saha. Omarzai has a wicket in his first over in the IPL!

name: RG Sharma
commentary: Rohit misses the sweep and the umpire raises his finger! Full outside off and angling in. Rohit can't connect. Big appeal and given immediately. Rohit reviews it. Was impact outside off? Impact in front of off and that's shown to be crashing into middle stump. No fifty for Rohit Sharma, but a very good knock nonetheless. Sai Kishore also gets his reward for a good spell

name: Naman Dhir
commentary: Big appeal for lbw, but the umpire doesn't give it. Gill chooses to review! It's on a length outside off and seams in sharply. Stays low to beat the bat as Dhir looks to swing across the line. Hit on the thigh on his back foot. And that's crashing into middle and leg according to ball tracking! Entertaining cameo comes to an end. And Omarzai ends his second over well after conceding 21 runs off it!

name: D Brevis
commentary: This time Mohit holds on! The pressure tells. Brevis falls for 46. 108kph delivery dug in short outside off. Brevis flat-bats it straight to Mohit who holds on at waist height. Brevis throws his head up in disgust. Is there a twist still in the waiting?

name: NT Tilak Varma
commentary: What a comeback from Spencer Johnson! Tilak flicks it straight to deep backward square! Spencer Johnson is pumped. Sub fielder Abhinav Manohar is pumped. This was full and angling into Tilak who moved across as he looked to flick it, but he can't get the elevation or the placement. Hit straight to the fielder. First IPL wicket for Spencer Johnson!

name: TH David
commentary: Brilliant catch by David Miller! Mohit goes for another slower bouncer, this time cramping David for room. He swivels as he looks to pull and gets it off the top edge. Miller has to run in from deep midwicket and dives forward to grab that. What a big wicket that is!

name: HH Pandya
commentary: And there ends Hardik's cameo! Short ball, cramping Hardik who goes for a pull and he only manages to top edge it down Tewatia's throat at long-on. He takes a rather simple catch and Hardik has to go.

name: G Coetzee
commentary: Top edge and it's taken! Bouncer on middle at 137kph, Coetzee miscues the pull and it goes high in the air. Spencer claims it, gets under it and holds on to it comfortably. What a brilliant 19th over from him after an expensive start to his debut! Sensational stuff!

name: JM Bairstow
commentary: Skied and gone! Great comeback for Siraj. Bairstow swipes at a length ball. The bat turns in his hand as he hits it and it goes up. Kohli settles under it coming in from cover and takes it with no fuss

name: Prabhsimran Singh
commentary: Goes for another big shot and perishes! The ball bounces quite sharply from a length as Prabhsimran looks to slog. Gets a top edge high in the air and to Rawat's left and the keeper makes no mistake

name: LS Livingstone
commentary: Joseph gets the big wicket of Livingstone! Joseph bowls it short and wide and Livingstone looks to cut it away. Gets a thick edge through to the keeper. That's a big breakthrough delivered by one of their marquee signings!

name: SM Curran
commentary: What a brilliant catch! Take a bow, Anuj Rawat! Dayal directs a bouncer at Curran, who looks to pull but was cramped for room. Ends up gloving it. Rawat sticks his right arm up as he jumps in the air and plucks it out of the air. And that will be all for Sam Curran today. A few boundaries this over, but overall a fantastic spell from Yash Dayal. And he has another delivery to go!

name: JM Sharma
commentary: Short ball does the trick again! Second bouncer of the over, outside off. Jitesh looks to slap it on the off side but it goes high in the air. Anuj Rawat has been excellent with the gloves today and again, he makes no mistake. But wait! They are checking if it hit the Spider-Cam! But no reprieve for Jitesh after a check by the umpire. Seems to not have had any contact with the camera or the wires

name: V Kohli
commentary: Slashed, taken at wide third! That is field placement at its best! Harpreet was somewhere between deep third and deep-backward point and got a low catch in front. Kohli made room and tried to lift the 135ks full ball, couldn't get the placement right or the power to clear the man. He leans on his bat in disappointment. PBKS ahead now?

name: F du Plessis
commentary: Skied to mid-on, Rabada gets his former skipper. Full ball at 137ks at the stumps, Faf tried getting under it and launching it down the ground, got it off the bottom edge. The open stance doesn't help as he can't transfer the weight forward. He knew he was gone right away. Easy enough catch for Curran, who is pumped

name: RM Patidar
commentary: Darted into middle, beats Patidar's attempted hoick over midwicket! Length ball, coming in with the angle, was an arm ball. Pace in the late 90ks. Patidar couldn't shift gears after being promoted.

name: Anuj Rawat
commentary: Slower one hits pad, that looked dead. Given too, Rawat reviews. Offcutter at 108ks, was the turn taking it too far? Impact seems to have been on middle and leg. Rawat was on the front foot and got hit under the knee roll after being beaten on the flick. Dhawan has a word with Curran while Hawk-Eye loads. That might not have been a cutter, almost looks like a back of handed. Chetan, our scorer, says it might be a googly. What does it say now? Clipping leg stump, decision stands.

name: R Ravindra
commentary: Oh, is Rachin stumped there? His reaction and Saha's reaction seems to suggest so. Oh, yeah, he is gone. Was a quicker length ball on leg stump, he gets a thick inside edge through his leg. Don't think Rachin knew how far or where exactly the ball went and ventured out of his crease. Saha was quick to whip off the bails to finds Rachin short

name: AM Rahane
commentary: Sai Kishore gets his man. The Chennai boy strikes at home. Was pushed quicker on middle, and that turned just a little bit as well. Rahane jumps down looking to go across the line. Misses, and Saha does the rest.

name: S Dube
commentary: Mistimed and Rashid gets his man! Googly right across Dube and away from his arc outside off. Was at the tramline. Dube looks to go after him, gets it right of the bottom of blade as the ball balloons straight up for extra cover to take a simple catch

name: Sameer Rizvi
commentary: Straight down long-on's throat! On-pace full ball on off stump. He gets under it, and looks to go over long-on. Gets it off the bottom of the bat with Miller making no mistake.

name: RA Jadeja
commentary: Pace off and there is a run out. Slower back of a length ball, Jadeja pulls to deep midwicket. Mitchell hares back for a second, but Jadeja isn't going anywhere. Saha casually whips off the bails and Jadeja is deemed out after the TV umpire has a look

name: Shubman Gill
commentary: celebrappeal from Chahar, and given by the umpire lbw! Gill reviews. This is a good length ball on off and Gill looks to play a check-pull in front of square, but the ball stays marginally low and hits him on the back leg. Will height save Gill? Tense GT fans in the crowd.... and that's three reds! The Titans captain is gone for a five-ball eight

name: B Sai Sudharsan
commentary: full ball on off stump. Sudharsan shows aggression by trying to lift it over long on, but checks it in the last moment. Doesn't have the distance to clear Rizvi at long on and he takes a simple catch near the boundary line.

name: V Shankar
commentary: vintage Dhoni! Dives to his right and he gets both hands to it! Full ball on a fourth-stump line. Shankar tries to drive straight but the ball takes the edge, and Dhoni pounces onto it in legendary fashion

name: DA Miller
commentary: Rahane with an athletic effort to take a stunning catch! Deshpande searches for the yorker on leg stump. Miller clears his front leg and tries to dropkick a flick through midwicket, but Rahane attacks the ball from cow corner, running in, and then dives forward to take a low catch! Stunning catch

name: Azmatullah Omarzai
commentary: this time Rachin takes the catch! Redeems himself. Short and wide ball. Omarzai tries to slap it over backward point but ends up flat-batting a shot high up in the air. Rachin runs across from extra cover to cover and brings out the reverse-cup catching technique again...however, this time it sticks! Takes a tumble on the followthrough but sports a smile as the ball is firmly in his grasp

name: R Tewatia
commentary: full ball wider outside off stump. Tewatia shapes up to clobber it over long off but it doesn't have the power. Rachin stays on the long-off boundary line, and takes a simple catch

name: Rashid Khan
commentary: that's...... gone! Good length ball on leg, and this is the cutter in fact. Ball scrambles Rashid as he clears the front leg and tries to club it over the leg side. Totally mistimed, and Rachin is in the action again at deep square leg

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: MA Agarwal
commentary: David makes no mistake this time. Hardik goes short, just outside off. Agarwal goes for the pull but ends up top-edging it towards mid-on. A much simpler catch this time for David

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: TM Head
commentary: Plan B has worked. Coetzee bowls a length ball but more importantly keeps it away from Head who had to reach out for it. Ends up hitting it to deep-backward point where Dhir pouches it

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: Abhishek Sharma
commentary: Chawla finally provides a breakthrough. This was bowled at 112.8kph. Abhishek went for another six but was a bit hurried. As a result, he doesn't get the timing right and Dhir settles under it at deep midwicket

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: AK Markram

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: H Klaasen

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: RG Sharma
commentary: High in the air, no reprieve this time. Rohit has to walk back. Cummins gets the big wicket! Back of length delivery seams in slightly from outside off. Rohit is looking for the pull but gets a top edge that goes miles in the air. Abhishek Sharma has to take a few steps in from deep backward square and he makes no mistake as he catches it falling forward

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: Ishan Kishan
commentary: Kishan holes out at deep midwicket! Shahbaz tosses it up wider, Kishan goes for the slog sweep again and ends up not hitting it cleanly. The ball goes high but doesn't have the distance. Aiden Markram takes it backtracking near the boundary

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: Naman Dhir
commentary: And there is the breakthrough! Slapped straight to cover! On a length around middle and off, Naman makes room and looks to get it over cover. Can't get the elevation and captain Cummins grabs it above head height

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: NT Tilak Varma
commentary: And Cummins gets the well set Tilak! Slower bouncer angled across Tilak. He tried to fetch it from well outside off with a pull and miscues it. Mayank Agarwal comes in from deep midwicket and holds on!

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: HH Pandya
commentary: And Hardik Pandya has to go! Unadkat is all laughs. SRH know they now all but have it in the bag. Cutter dug into the pitch, seaming away. Hardik goes for a pull but can only top edge it. Easy peasy lemon squeezy for Heinrich Klaasen

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: TH David

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: R Shepherd
YBK Jaiswal
commentary: Is that one stump out the ground or two? Looks like off stump was flattened and middle rattled. The flying bails was shiver-inducing. Seam-bowling special. 141ks on a length, angling in from around the wicket, decked in to cramp Jaiswal for room on the flick, ball ricocheted off the bat onto the stumps.

name: JC Buttler
commentary: Beaten on the reverse, lbw appeal turned down. Kuldeep took the matter, and literally Rishabh's hands, into his own and signalled the T. Where did this pitch though? Buttler tried playing it on the up, got beaten and ball hit just under the knee-roll, outer-half of the pad. It has pitched in line and Kuldeep might as well have Hawk-Eye tech embedded in him cos the ball is projected to land in line and go on to smash leg peg. It landed around a back of length too, which makes his conviction look even better. Buttler tried to break out of his struggle but perishes in the attempt

name: SV Samson
commentary: Guided to the keeper! Khaleel's around-the-wicket-angle works. He's cramped Samson for room, who tried to back away and chop the 141ks into-the-pitch ball through point. Ends up nicking it to Pant. Khaleel's jubilant, DC's new-ball bowlers have made it their powerplay.

name: R Ashwin
commentary: Ashwin tries to clear deep midwicket but can't. 95ks length ball angling in, he saw it in his half and tried went for a stand-up slog sweep. Got a decent chunk but not enough to clear Stubbs, who took it and swivelled back, the step was within the cushion. Ashwin's given RR's innings some impetus though

name: DC Jurel
commentary: Paddled onto off stump, that's a rare visual. Jurel picked the fast, full ball from outside the offside tramline, seemed to have middled it but unintentionally sent the bails on a sexy-looking flight. Second of those on the night after Jaiswal's dismissal. Nortje is pumped

name: MR Marsh
commentary: Gone through the gate! Missile on a length, swinging back in a touch at 145.8kph. Marsh tries to run it down to third but is done in by the inward movement and is knocked over. The after-holi pink hair of Burger sway as he celebrates with full vigour

name: RR Pant
commentary: Chahal strikes! Lands it on a shortish length, angling it wider outside, 91.1kph. Pant has to wait a bit and looks to cut but gets a faint lower edge. Samson does the rest

name: Abishek Porel
commentary: Porel perishes now. Chahal tosses it up and throws it wide outside off. He knows Porel will charge down again, and he has to fetch the ball from well outside off. Miscues it straight down long-off's throat

name: F du Plessis
commentary: And Rana has his revenge next ball! He is off and running in celebrations with his arms stretched. He bowls an offcutter on a length well outside off. Du Plessis once again goes for the scoop over short fine leg but couldn't counter the change of pace. Hits it straight to Starc in the circle. Early strike for KKR

name: RM Patidar
commentary: Catching practice for Rinku. Back of a length around off, 112.7kph, Patidar has to manufacture the power in his heave across the line. The bat turns in his hands and he hits it straight to deep midwicket. 100th IPL wicket for Dre Russ

name: Anuj Rawat
commentary: Soft dismissal. Full offcutter outside off, Rawat looks to go over the off side. Gets a thick outside edge that carries low to Salt behind.

name: PD Salt
commentary: buckethands Green grabs that! Short ball angling into him and Salt goes for a pull. But that's been drilled straight to the man who is guarding the deep square leg boundary. Big roar from Vyshak and Salt is gone for 30

name: SP Narine
commentary: yorker, on off stump, and this crashes into the base of off stump! Wicket soon after the time-out and Narine will not get a landmark fifty in his 500th T20. He was too slow to bring the bat down, but to be fair, he's done his job tonight

name: VR Iyer
commentary: holes out straight after fifty and Dayal has something to celebrate. Pace-off length ball outside off. Venkatesh tries to tonk a pull over cow corner but this time he drags the shot. Kohli makes no mistake

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: Q de Kock
commentary: And he strikes straightaway! Bangs it in short outside off, de Kock goes for the pull but gets a top edge. Jitesh gets under it and makes no mistake

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: KL Rahul
commentary: Arshdeep has his revenge! Rahul looks to dab away a length ball going across him, opens the face but steers it to Bairstow's left at backward point. The Englishman reacts sharply and sends Rahul packing for a 9-ball 15

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: MP Stoinis
commentary: Knocks him over! Rahul Chahar bowls this on a length on middle, quicker and flatter. Stoinis went for the pull and missed and the ball went in with the angle to hit leg stump. Just as Stoinis got going, he has to go!

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: N Pooran
commentary: Pooran drags it onto his stumps! Pooran is disgusted with himself. This is full and way wide, Pooran looks for a big shot and ends up getting a big inside edge onto the stumps. That's a massive wicket for Kings. Pooran was looking very deadly!

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: A Badoni
commentary: Holes out at long-on. Slower ball, full outside off, and Badoni can only lob it up to Bairstow in the deep. He struggled today and falls for 8 off 10. This was bowled at 86kph!

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: KH Pandya

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: Ravi Bishnoi
commentary: Two in two for Curran! Slower ball again, 109kph, dug in short. Bishnoi went for the pull but could only find the deep square leg fielder

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: JM Bairstow
commentary: Mayank's pace breaks the partnership 143ks. Bairstow started making room and Mayank followed him, went into the pitch, found the top edge off the pull. Was a length ball on top of leg, Bairstow was deep in the crease and was hurried despite seeming to have planned the shot in advance. Ball sailed three-fourth of the way out, Stoinis ran in and took a sliding catch

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: Prabhsimran Singh
commentary: Ballooned pull to mid-on, 147ks. Short and over midde, rose around rib-height, Prabhsimran cleared the front leg and tried forcing it but clothed it straight to Naveen. Pace doing pace things. Good cameo from the batter though

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: JM Sharma
commentary: 141ks, Another pull us ballooned, Naveen takes another catch. Short into the pitch, rises around rib-height, Jitesh looked to go over midwicket but like a few other team-mates, got hurried. Naveen went from mid-on towards short midwicket to catch the top edged ball

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: LS Livingstone

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: SM Curran
commentary: Pull skied, catch taken at mid-on, Mohsin has two in two. Another slower bumper, just under shoulder-height just outside off, Curran tried leathering the pull but got a top edge. No sighters and no second chances - death overs doing its thing

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: Shashank Singh
TM Head
