commentary: Kuldeep Yadav gets two wickets in an over! How often does he do this? Wrong'un on a length on off, Markram looks to slap it away but ends up giving Axar Patel an easy catch at cover. Kuldeep pumps his fists

name: H Klaasen
commentary: Knocks Klaasen over! Axar bowls it on a length around off. It stays a little low as Klaasen looks to swing across the line and the ball crashes into the stumps. Two wickets in two balls for Capitals

name: Abdul Samad
commentary: Goes full and across Samad, who hits it straight to Nortje at cover who takes a good low catch. Muted celebrations as Samad walks

name: PJ Cummins
commentary: Low full toss on middle, chips it down the ground and they look to run two. Oops, Cummins has slipped at the striker's end as Shahbaz sprints down for the second. Cummins can't recover and he will have to walk back

name: Abishek Porel
commentary: outwits Porel and gets him stumped. Porel was coming down the track a lot, and this time Markande acts on that impulse. Bowls a full ball very wide outside off, and Klaasen was ready for that line. Swiftly collected well outside off, and he cleans up the stumps thereafter

name: T Stubbs
shortText: c Shahbaz Ahmed b Nitish Kumar Reddy
commentary: short ball, banged in. Stubbs tries to maul a pull over cow corner but the lack of pace means Stubbs can't generate a lot of power off the bat. Mistimed, and straight to short midwicket

name: RR Pant
shortText: c Natarajan b Nitish Kumar Reddy
commentary: Goes to hook and finds the long leg fielder and that's wrapped up for Sunrisers Hyderabad! Short ball angled into him, Pant tried to get in the line and help it along but there was no pace for him to play with

name: AR Patel
commentary: knee-high full toss just outside off. Axar tries to get under it and lift it over cover, but fails to clear Cummins at cover. A second wicket for Natarajan

name: A Nortje
commentary: cleans him up! Full ball on middle. Nortje tries to swing across the line but he's beaten by the quicker delivery at almost 139kph.

name: Kuldeep Yadav
commentary: yorker searing into his toes. Kuldeep misses the flick, and it hits his boot. Umpire swift to raise his finger for the LBW and Kuldeep reviews. Replay shows three reds - crashing into leg stump. And that is also Natarajan's best figures in T20 cricket!

name: PD Salt
commentary: And Siraj gets the breakthrough! Phil's stunning assault comes to an end. This is full around leg and he looks for the flick, but hits it straight to deep midwicket. Salt's trudging off but that was a brilliant innings

name: SP Narine
commentary: Slices it high in the air and it falls to Virat Kohli at long-on! Back-of-hand slower ball outside off, Narine ends up slicing it as he goes downtown. Kohli holds on above head height. RCB planned well for Narine today and it came off as well!

name: A Raghuvanshi
commentary: Oh, wat a stunner by Cameron Green! Raghuvanshi looks to flick away a full ball angling into him. The ball is hit well over the fielder, and not many other than Green would have got their hands to it. But Green has big buckets and sticks his right arm out with momentum already taking him backwards and somehow holds on as he falls over. What a way to close out the powerplay.

name: VR Iyer
commentary: Hooked to deep square leg! Green bangs it in short and into Venkatesh and he gets  a top edge. Lomror makes no mistake and Green has his man after being hit for a boundary

name: SS Iyer
commentary: Blinder by Faf du Plessis! Shreyas picked the bones out of this length ball as he smacks it down the ground. He hit it hard, and flat, and Faf du Plessis moving to his left from long-off gets low and gets his body behind it and hold on safe! Ends a good knock. Important wicket at the back end

name: V Kohli
commentary: Caught and bowled but is it a no-ball? Much like what happened with Russell on his first ball. But did this dip enough? Rana runs in and slips in a slow full toss. Looked to come at an awkward height as Kohli went for an evasive action before trying to defend. Ends up getting the leading top edge towards the bowler. Kohli immediately signalled for the review but it is already an umpire's review. Kohli is not happy as he walks back as the TV ump has given him out. He was outside the batting crease when he made contact. Also, the Hawk-Eye's trajectory did suggest it went below his waist. Not sure if Kohli showing displeasure is right. But KKR can well celebrate. Smart stuff from Rana

name: F du Plessis
commentary: Venky Iyer takes a stunner! He has his arms wide as he gathers himself after getting up on his feet. KKR celebrates with Eden Gardens roaring. Flatter length ball, spinning in to du Plessis, who hacks it down the ground. Mid-on quickly moves to his left, dives and plucks it

name: RM Patidar
commentary: Dre Russ is a magician! Nonchalantly walks in to the bowling crease and nips out two set batters one after the other. Bowls it full on middle. Patidar has been smoking them out of the park all evening, But Russell has rolled his fingers on this one. As a result, the batter is a tad early into his hack across the line. Gets a top edge that is swallowed by backward point. Has Russell effectively killed the chase?

name: MK Lomror
commentary: Easy peasy for Narine! How can you keep him out of action for long? Slips it a carrom ball around leg, Lomror looks to turn it across but chips it back to the bowler. RCB digging a hole for themselves here...

venue: Maharaja Yadavindra Singh International Cricket Stadium, Mullanpur
name: SM Curran
commentary: full ball around leg stump and doesn't turn either way Curran tries to knock it leg side but he's beaten, and the ball hits him on the knee roll. Appeal for LBW - but not given, however, Gill reviews again. What do we have here.... another umpire error, and another Titans wicket on review! Pitched on leg stump, hit him on leg stump, and crashing into leg stump. Gill jumps onto Rashid to celebrate

venue: Maharaja Yadavindra Singh International Cricket Stadium, Mullanpur
name: Prabhsimran Singh
commentary: another promising Prabhsimran innings cut short in the IPL! Fullish ball just outside off. Singh comes down the track to try and loft it over cover but gets an edge to the keeper instead

venue: Maharaja Yadavindra Singh International Cricket Stadium, Mullanpur
name: JM Sharma
commentary: oh that's simply beautiful from Sai! Drags his length back, slows it in the air, and deceives Jitesh. The batter came charging down to try and hit another big shot after two dots, but the ball lands well before him, turns across, goes under his bat, and crashes into the stumps

venue: Maharaja Yadavindra Singh International Cricket Stadium, Mullanpur
name: LS Livingstone
commentary: first slip plan pays off! Length ball around off. Googly takes it across Livingstone. Batter tries to flick against the turn to midwicket, and that induces the outside edge. Ball goes quickly to Tewatia, but he sticks his hands out to his right to grab onto it

venue: Maharaja Yadavindra Singh International Cricket Stadium, Mullanpur
name: Shashank Singh
commentary: his third wicket! Sai has been varying his pace, forcing mis-hits and mistakes, and this time Shashank hits it right back to the bowler. Sai bowls a length ball, flat, and Shashank comes down the track to meet it. But he cannot get the bat swing through the ball, instead bunting a catch straight back to Sai

venue: Maharaja Yadavindra Singh International Cricket Stadium, Mullanpur
name: Harpreet Singh
commentary: bunts a shot away to deep square leg, and they take off for a couple of runs, but the throw from the deep to Saha is decent, and the keeper throws the balls with both hands onto the stumps. And the smart work has resulted in Bhatia being run out at the keeper's end by an inch! Story of the PBKS innings... Punjab Kings bowled out for 142

venue: Maharaja Yadavindra Singh International Cricket Stadium, Mullanpur
name: Harpreet Brar
shortText: c Shahrukh Khan b Sai Kishore
commentary: six? Looks like, but no, Shahrukh Khan is a tall man and he earns his fellow Tamil Nadu team-mate a four-for. It was an overpitched ball on off and Brar did really well to get the bat under it to get the elevation, but it wasn't enough on this occasion as SRK jumps up and grabs the ball from only inches inside the boundary line

venue: Maharaja Yadavindra Singh International Cricket Stadium, Mullanpur
name: HV Patel
commentary: full ball outside off stump. Slot length and Harshal smacks it down the V, but Shahrukh is lurking on the long-on boundary again. Harshal got a solid connection there but the slower ball was the difference between him clearing the fence and falling short

venue: Maharaja Yadavindra Singh International Cricket Stadium, Mullanpur
name: K Rabada

venue: Maharaja Yadavindra Singh International Cricket Stadium, Mullanpur
name: Shubman Gill
commentary: goes down the ground... but hasn't caught it! Livingstone roars in delight, he's got the big wicket of Gill. He stretched out to meet it but didn't get enough on the lofted shot and it's a comfortable catch for Rabada some way in from the rope at long-off. Gill gulled and Kings have a window of opportunity

venue: Maharaja Yadavindra Singh International Cricket Stadium, Mullanpur
name: B Sai Sudharsan
commentary: chopped on! Sammy Cuz-Cuz strikes back! Length ball, scrambled seam and Sai plays a flat-footed jab that detonates his own stumps. Big wicket for Kings with Sai looking to anchor the chase. Curran making things happen in approved style

venue: Maharaja Yadavindra Singh International Cricket Stadium, Mullanpur
name: DA Miller
commentary: timbeeeeeeerrrrrrrrrr! No Miller Time for GT tonight. Livingstone has put him on ice. Another vein-popping celebration from Livingstone and Kings might just start to believe they can do this. The ball was tossed up and skidding under Miller's big swing across the line to rattle middle and leg stumps

venue: Maharaja Yadavindra Singh International Cricket Stadium, Mullanpur
name: Azmatullah Omarzai
commentary: nicked behind, Harshal strikes! Hard length coming back at Azmat and he flashes at a drive, producing a fat edge through to the keeper. Kings are not letting this one go

venue: Maharaja Yadavindra Singh International Cricket Stadium, Mullanpur
name: R Tewatia

venue: Maharaja Yadavindra Singh International Cricket Stadium, Mullanpur
name: M Shahrukh Khan
commentary: yorked him! Full with pace off and it dips under the bat, flicks the boot and then hits off stump. Beautifully bowled but probably too late for Kings

venue: Maharaja Yadavindra Singh International Cricket Stadium, Mullanpur
name: Rashid Khan
commentary: full toss, scooped to midwicket! Titans continue to make a meal of the chase. Rashid picks out Rossouw for a good, low catch, smiles and walks off. Anyone else want to hit the winning run?

venue: Maharaja Yadavindra Singh International Cricket Stadium, Mullanpur
name: R Sai Kishore
RG Sharma
commentary: gone in the first over! Rohit opens up and goes for a big, big swing. He has skied that so high, it's not an easy catch for Samson even though he doesn't have to move too much. Settles under it nicely and Boult has got him again, struck in the first over

name: Ishan Kishan
commentary: nearly edges it behind. Oh, Samson thinks there's an edge and he has reviewed it straightaway! Poking outside off and away from the body, UltraEdge shows a spike and the crowd loves it. Kishan is gone caught behind for a duck

name: SA Yadav
commentary: SKY is gone as well, another big wicket for Sandeep! Scrambled seam from Sandeep on middle from good length, SKY wants to whip it away into the leg side but in the air and he has hit it high, for Powell to settle under it easily at mid-on

name: NT Tilak Varma
commentary: finds long-on where Powell pouches it with a tumble. An excellent innings with a salvaging act and some attacking strokes comes to an end. He tries to whack that away but can't clear long-on

name: N Wadhera
commentary: strikes first ball and breaks the partnership! Slow bouncer wide of Wadhera, he has to go for the shot but can only hand a catch to short third. Didn't middle the upper cut at all

name: HH Pandya
commentary: trapped right in front and given straightaway! Hardik has reviewed this too. He missed a full toss with pace angling into the stumps, it struck him on the front pad right in front on the knee roll and ball-tracking says it's hitting. Hardik gone and Royals trying to limit Mumbai now after some carnage in the middle overs

name: TH David
commentary: five wickets for Sandeep Sharma! David swings big, he has to because it's a full toss, even if low. He dispatches it high into the sky and that comes down at the cow corner where Riyan Parag makes it look very easy

name: G Coetzee
commentary: two in two for Sandeep! 124.4kmh and shaping away from well outside off, Coetzee smashes it down the ground but he's not middled that and Hetmyer runs in to grab that without losing his balance

name: JC Buttler
commentary: Timbaaaa! Chawla makes his frustration evident. Strikes right after the boundary. Slips in the wrong'un on a shortish length around off, Buttler goes back to punch but it turns in just a touch to clatter off

name: AM Rahane
commentary: superb catch by KL Rahul. They have just moved the slip wide, and for a second I think that has cost them a wicket. This is the only full ball this over, draws the drive, holds its line, and takes a healthy edge. An easy grab for first slip if in regulation position, but now Rahul has to make up for it with a one-handed diving grab

name: RA Jadeja
commentary: slower bouncer, Jadeja tries to pull, and ends up gloving it to KL Rahul. Hardly any celebration from LSG. Do you really want the wicket oof Jadeja when he is going at under a run a ball?

name: S Dube
commentary: full and wide, out of his swinging arc, hit straight to extra cover. Gaikwad this time says he wants the strike back. Dube is caught short at the non-striker's end. Gine for 66 off 27. Huge roar in the ground because they are expecting Dhoni with two balls to go

name: Q de Kock
commentary: Cleans him up! A drag on and LSG have been rocked just three balls into the chase. Gentle length ball angling across de Kock, who feebly reaches for the ball away from his body, gets an inside-edge and the ball clatters into his stump. Chahar is pumped, the crowd is pumped. CSK are away.

name: KL Rahul
commentary: He gets his man and that's the big wicket of Rahul! Excellent captaincy, excellent bowling. He had closed the angle and that meant Rahul had to create extra room. Steps to the leg side but Fizz has kept the ball right on off stump on a length. Rahul slaps it only as far as Gaikwad who is right on the circle at extra cover

name: N Pooran
commentary: That goes straight up and Pooran has perished! Pathirana wins the battle yet again. Was in the slot outside off, as Pooran looked to straight down the ground. But he could only slice it and Shardul coming in from long-off takes a comfortable-enough catch

name: AR Patel
shortText: c Sai Kishore b Noor Ahmad
commentary: taken at long-on! Axar went for another heave, but this time hit only flat without any distance. Sai Kishore takes with his hands going low. Noor bowled on a length, and went for a googly again. This was bowled outside off, as Axar picked it up to swing across the line but mistimed

name: SD Hope
shortText: c Rashid Khan b Sandeep Warrier
commentary: looks to go inside-out over cover, but doesn't get to the pitch and slices this down Rashid Khan's throat at third man. Rashid did well to get around the ropes, but lost sight of the ball, or so it seemed, but somehow recovered in the nick of time to dive forward and complete the catch. It's a third for Warrier.

name: Shubman Gill
commentary: sliced to mid-off! Axar there back-pedals and takes the ball with a reverse cup over his head. Gill is beaten by the pace, which is 147kph. Nortje went on a fullish length on off, and Gill went lofting. But the ball seemed to skid through, and took the bottom of Gill's bat before chipping for a simple catch

name: B Sai Sudharsan
commentary: he has picked out long-off. Didn't get to the pitch of the ball but went through with the stroke, the timing was slightly off as he hit it off the bottom part and Axar didn't have to move much to complete the catch.

name: Azmatullah Omarzai
commentary: this is simply outstanding from Jake Fraser-McGurk! Incredible anticipation and speed off the turf to get to the ball from the deep midwicket boundary. Omarzai heaved that to the leg side, didn't time it well at all, which made the catch that much harder. Oh, JFM, you beauty.

name: DA Miller
shortText: c Rasikh Salam b Mukesh Kumar
commentary: taken at deep backward square leg! There was almost another drop there! Mukesh went on the fuller side of a length on leg, and Miller again got his front leg out of the way in trying to flick it hard and flat. But Salam at the boundary went low to grab that, and nearly fumbled while his hands touched the ground

name: M Shahrukh Khan
commentary: ahh, well taken by Pant! He hardly had time to react there. He is up to the stumps, and the ball stays slightly low off a length around sixth stump. Shahrukh attempts to swing across the line and gets a bottom edge, but Pant brings both his legs close together to nab it quickly. Salam strikes again, as the wide-ish line outside off finally works for DC after Mukesh was dispatched in the last over

name: R Tewatia
commentary: taken by Pant again! Kuldeep gets Tewatia, even as the ball wasn't all that well bowled. There were a lot of revs on it all right, but the length was short outside off, and the turn away meant it ended further wide outside off. Tewatia went chasing after it with a flat bat, and ended up playing away from his body. But a faint edge was pouched by Pant moving to his left, and ended Tewatia's stay

name: R Sai Kishore
commentary: bowled him! Salam hits back with an inswinging yorker, which Sai isn't able to keep out. But Sai has done a terrific job, and invariably ensured that Rashid has the strike for GT in the final over. The length of the ball was too full for Sai to try and clip across the line, and he missed. Salam is thrilled

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: V Kohli
commentary: all that pressure to break free has consumed Kohli! He was looking increasingly edgy in the last few overs to break free, and has perished trying to pick another slower short of length ball that Unadkat did well to bowl well outside off and have him fetch it from far. RCB's innings on decelerate mode.

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: F du Plessis
commentary: there's Faf out trying to sustain RCB's powerplay salvo! Looks to slap this over cover but this delivery gets big on him, which means he can't generate much power on the shot as Markram takes a simple catch at mid-off. Nattu did well to bowl this into the pitch again.

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: RM Patidar
commentary: lives by the sword, dies by it! Unadkat serves up a full toss into Patidar's hitting arc, he flicks this superbly but ends up picking the lone man in the leg side boundary, at deep square leg.

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: MK Lomror
commentary: Cummins holds on at deep midwicket! Three-for for Jaydev Unadkat! Full and wide, Lomror goes back and across and swings it across the line. Cummins was backtracking and sticks his hands up and grabs it while falling backward

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: Swapnil Singh
commentary: Miscues it to long-off and that leaves SRH with 207 to win

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: Abhishek Sharma
commentary: Goes straight up in the air and that's easy as it comes for Dinesh Karthik! Abhishek Sharma looked to pull away a length delivery and ends up slicing it high in the air. Karthik settled under it early and watched it all the way into his gloves. That's the two openers gone inside four overs! Do RCB have an opening?

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: TM Head
commentary: The plan works for RCB! The big wicket of Travis Head! Head goes for the big shot, but it spins away so he is completely out of shape. Takes a top edge and pops up fine on the off side and Karn Sharma makes no mistake!

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: AK Markram
commentary: That is some way to make your RCB debut! Markram steps out and misses a full toss and is hit on his back thigh. Finger goes up immediately and Markram is quick to review. No bat involved. Impact seems to have been just in front of off - it could be umpire's call. But we wait for ball tracking. Impact in line and that was dropping to hit the base of leg stump. Swapnil Singh, hit for a four first ball, gets the big wicket of Markram next. Kohli can't hold back his laughter as du Plessis takes Swapnil aside for some instructions. He won't mind how it's come, he'll be elated he got that wicket!

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: H Klaasen
commentary: And Klaasen goes for another big shot but only skies it to mid-on! Swapnil Singh is absolutely pumped! That's a huge wicket! Swapnil pulls his length back just enough and the ball spins away as Klaasen goes after it. The ball balloons high in the air and with Cameron Green under it, there was very little chance of it being dropped. And SRH are four down! Double-wicket over is not a bad way to start your IPL bowling career! Especially if you account for Klaasen!

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: Shahbaz Ahmed

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: Abdul Samad
commentary: chipped back to Karn! Samad steps out and has to readjust because Karn flattens his trajectory and gets this to bite off the deck, he attempts a nudge down the ground but ends up lobbing it back as Karn dives to his right to take an excellent catch.

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: PJ Cummins
commentary: excellent catch from Siraj at short third! Green bowls a good bumper and bumps out his Australian captain. Goes short, wide and Cummins has no pace on it as he tries to help it over, but ends up spooning it high in the air as Siraj running back takes it nice and easy.

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: B Kumar
commentary: Chipped to midwicket and Green has his second! Green looks for the yorker and it ends up as a low full toss around leg. Bhuvneshwar looks to work it leg side but ends up hitting it to Siraj, who grabs on at head height

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: JD Unadkat
PD Salt
commentary: 131.1kmh, pace does it for Curran! Full and straight on off, nearly in the block hole, quick as well, at least quicker, and Salt misses his hit

name: SP Narine
commentary: they've finally got a wicket and it's of Narine! After three chances that have gone down, Punjab Kings have held on to one. Short and flat outside off, Narine wants to pull it over long-on but he can't get the distance and he holes out

name: VR Iyer
commentary: cutter outside off just inside the line, Venkatesh misses the big swing and he's run out after venturing out because Ramandeep doesn't run. Oh hello! The umpire has given it wide but it wasn't and is reviewed immediately. Changed to a legal delivery and KKR end on 261

name: AD Russell
commentary: short ball does it for Russell! Maybe he wasn't expecting a bouncer with that field, he's a tad late on the pull for the bouncer and gets it only as far as square leg

name: SS Iyer
commentary: it didn't look threatening at all but the wide and full ball has done it. Shreyas had to reach out for it and scythed it straight to deep third

name: Prabhsimran Singh
commentary: Anukul escapes from the final ball, thick-edged to short fine leg... and Prabhsimran has been run-out from a direct hit! Narine fires down the stumps as they try to scamper one! The crowd erupts, KKR needed that after a blood-and-thunder start to this chase from Kings

name: Abishek Porel
commentary: beats him in flight and Ishan effects a superb stumping. Porel stepped out to heave this into the leg side but was nowhere near the pitch. Got a thin edge on that as he swung across the line.

name: SD Hope
commentary: whips him straight to Tilak coming in from the ropes at deep midwicket. It was timed well, but it was almost as if Hope didn't account for the fielder there. Also the fact that he had to slightly force the pace on this slower length ball made it that much harder. Good, little hand from Hope. Sustained momentum created by JFM. Incredible stuff.

name: RR Pant
commentary: the loopy bouncer has done the trick! Pant loses control of this attempted pull as the ball gets big on him, he goes through with the top and gets a top-edge that's taken by Rohit who circles around at fine leg.

name: Ishan Kishan
commentary: fired into the good length but took the pace off! Ishan goes for the slap through the line but can't time it on impact. The bat turns in his hands as Axar swoops round to gather at wide mid-off. Big breakthrough!

name: RG Sharma
commentary: Rohit falls! Hope in the covers cleans up an attempt to clear mid-off, scuffed off a leading edge. Not a particularly successful day out, as Hope leaps to his left to gather

name: SA Yadav
commentary: jabbed to cover and the SKY has fallen in! Pace off from round the wicket, sticks in the pitch, Suryakumar Yadav's hard hands were committed to the back-foot drive, and it was a simple popped chance to Williams to end the powerplay on a high for DC!

name: NT Tilak Varma
commentary: slower ball, hoisted high in the air! Lands short of square leg, but Varma's sprawl for the second run looks to be short! Indeed, he's been given on-field as well. A valiant knock, but that, surely, is the game!

name: HH Pandya
shortText: c Mukesh Kumar b Rasikh Salam
commentary: straight up in the air and now DC are closing in! Hardik had another hack through the full length outside off, but he wasn't close enough to the pitch and Mukesh at point had oodles of time to steady himself

name: N Wadhera
commentary: genuine nick outside off, tumbling take by Pant! It's like watching Test cricket, welcome back old friend! Outside off, angle nipping back into the left-hander, who shaped to run the ball down to deep third but was conventionally beaten off the pitch. The die is cast

name: TH David
commentary: Pant comes up to the stumps. Interesting... nailed on the pads! The review has been taken but this looks plumb! The keeper ruse prevented David from using his feet with the same freedom, but it took a dead-eyed delivery to capitalise. Full, straight, attacking the stumps, he had walked across to the off-side once more, but was struck in line on umpire's call, and the rest was plumb!

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: Q de Kock
commentary: hot knife through butter, and off stump has been flattened! Back of a length on off. Seems to pitch and nip back in just a shade. de Kock tries to defend but his bat comes down at an angle. He is late on the stroke too and the ball breezes past the willow before leaving off stump in a mess. Another first-over wicket for Boult!

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: KL Rahul
commentary: holes out to deep backward point! Short, wide and asking Rahul to hit to the bigger side. He throws the kitchen sink at it but never seemed to get the power to clear that massive boundary. Slices it towards Boult on the deep backward point fence, and the Kiwi makes no mistake. Rahul is gutted with the way he has gotten out. RR, meanwhile, will be chuffed with how they have been chipping away!

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: MP Stoinis
commentary: middle stump has been flattened now! RR all over LSG in the powerplay! Full ball that starts on off and swerves back in. Stoinis, without really getting to the pitch of the ball, tries to drive it. Leaves a gap between bat and pad, and the ball zips through. Stoinis might have gotten an inside edge but it does not matter in the grander scheme of things. LSG two down, and under the pump!

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: DJ Hooda
commentary: Ashwin gets Hooda! Carrom ball on a length on middle and leg. Hooda advances, trying to smash it over long on. Does not quite get to the pitch, though, and has to reach out in front. Chalks it off the toe-end and Powell circles under it at long on to complete the catch. Vital wicket for the Royals, but take nothing away from the excellent hand Hooda has played here!

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: N Pooran
commentary: Sandeep outfoxes Pooran! Dug into the track on middle and leg. Pooran tries to swipe this over deep backward square leg but the ball gets big on him. Cannot control his stroke and the ball skews off the top edge towards Boult, who keeps his eyes on the ball and takes the catch well inside the fence. Pooran is disappointed, but this is just what RR would have wanted after the time-out!

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: A Badoni

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: KH Pandya

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: YBK Jaiswal
commentary: Jaiswal bites the dust now! LSG go bang-bang! Back of a length delivery angled across the batter outside off. Jaiswal winds up and has a huge swish at it. Slices across it too much, though, and finds the sweeper on the deep cover fence. Stoinis is delighted; Jaiswal, not quite!

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: JC Buttler
commentary: Yash Thakur has the last laugh! Zips in a low full toss on leg stump. Buttler, having walked across his stumps, almost cuts himself off by how far he has moved. Cannot lay bat on ball, and hears the death rattle soon after. Sees the image of his leg stump flattened too, and he is absolutely livid with himself. Thakur will not mind one bit, though, and he has given LSG the breakthrough they were after!

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: SV Samson

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: R Parag
commentary: Mishra gets rid of Parag, and he points to the back of his shirt in celebration! What theatre! Dangles the carrot outside off and Parag bites. He thinks he can go inside out but this is a slider that seems to just hurry onto him a touch. Slices it and Badoni makes no mistake at deep cover. Mishra is elated, and LSG are now right back in this!

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: DC Jurel
Shubman Gill
commentary: Green takes a skimmer at long-on, Maxwell takes out Gill in his first over! His veins are popping as he thunders about in celebration. Length ball, drifting away on a wide line, did sit up and Gill tried to forehand-smash it straight. The connection was good, the trajectory was flat. Green charged to the left, tumbled across and held on with both hands. The pace was 94ks if I looked at it right

name: M Shahrukh Khan
commentary: Reverse-swings at 140ks to castle Shahrukh. Not a yorker but very full, very little Shahrukh could've done about it. He tried whipping it through midwicket but got nothing on it and fell over. Middle and off were rattled. As sexy as it gets with pace bowling.

name: F du Plessis
shortText: c sub (V Shankar) b Sai Kishore
commentary: taken at deep midwicket! This seemed like a variation from Sai, who also went quicker. The release seemed like that of a wrong 'un for a moment. Anyway, Sai saw du Plessis back away a little, and bowled on a short of a length while angling it into middle and leg. Du Plessis went pulling hard and through the line, but found the man in the deep, who had to move a few steps to his left to take that

name: AM Rahane
commentary: pumped to Shahbaz at wide long-on. Chepauk goes silent now. On a length and outside off, Rahane swings once again, but I suspect this was the slower ball from Bhuvneshwar. Rahane ends up dragging it to one of the two men at the boundary in the powerplay. So, Bhuvneshwar snags Rahane once again

name: TM Head
commentary: Sliced to deep point! The slower ball does the trick. Deshpande goes full and fires it across Head, who looks to loft it on the off side. Doesn't get a hold of it and Daryl Mitchell settles under it for the catch

name: Abhishek Sharma
commentary: Abhishek perishes like Head did! Short ball angled across him and he stands tall and cuts it in the air but straight to Daryl Mitchell at deep point! Tushar Deshpande has come in like a wrecking ball against this SRH lineup!

name: Anmolpreet Singh
commentary: Two in two! Full on middle, 134/5kph, Anmolpreet looks to turn it leg side and is early into the shot. Gets a leading edge that pops up on the off side. Moeen Ali backtracks from mid-off and grabs it. There was a hint of away swing there that did Anmolpreet in

name: AK Markram
commentary: Yorked him! Middle stump is out of the ground and so is Markram! Inswinging yorker at 147kph and Markram swings across the line in vain. Brilliant delivery from the Sri Lankan!

name: H Klaasen
commentary: Klaasen holes out. End of his struggles today. Pathirana went for the yorker, but it turned into a low full toss on middle and off. Klaasen goes downtown, but can't get under it enough. Simple catch at long-on and CSK will feel much more relaxed now that the danger man is back in the hut

name: Abdul Samad
commentary: Samad picks out long-off and Shardul gets a wicket. Good catch by Rizvi in the deep. Cross-seam length ball outside off. Samad free his arms and swings through the line but ends up slicing it a little and not getting the elevation he needed. Rizvi settles under it and takes it low

name: Shahbaz Ahmed
commentary: Catch no. 5 for Daryl Mitchell! Full and angling into Shahbaz, who looks to play a pick-up shot over square leg. But like so many before him today, he just doesn't hit it long enough

name: PJ Cummins
commentary: Another catch for Mitchell! Fourth in the match! Full outside off, not quite in the slot, but Cummins goes downtown. Doesn't get all of it and that's gone straight the Daz, who has been safe as a house all evening long

name: JD Unadkat
commentary: Spanked straight to Moeen Ali and that's a massive win wrapped up for CSK! Full outside off, Unadkat looks to slap it away on the off side but hits it right at the fielder. Good low catch by Moeen Ali

name: Abishek Porel
commentary: stumps all over the place! Goes fuller, and slants this into middle and leg. Porel, feeling the pinch of his strike rate, walks across his stumps and looks for the scoop. Makes a complete hash of it, though, and the ball ends up making a complete mess of his stumps. Leg stump and middle stump have been uprooted, and DC are four down. Rana gives Porel a bit of a send-off too!

name: SD Hope
commentary: cleans him up with an absolute peach! Back of a length delivery that pitches on off and seems destined to come in. Until it doesn't. Nips away against the angle after hitting the seam and Hope is opened up like a deck chair. Tries to fence it away but sees the ball thud into the top of off stump. DC under early strife!

name: RR Pant
commentary: induces the false stroke again, and taken this time! Floated up full just outside off. Pant's eyes light up looking at the extra flight and he tries to smash it into next week. Loses his shape completely and slices it high up. Shreyas settles under it at extra cover, and makes no mistake. KKR keep chipping away; DC keep sliding!

name: AR Patel
commentary: the stumps have been lit up, and Axar has to go! Shortish just outside off again. Axar stays back, trying to force it through the off side. The ball seems to nip back in just a shade and that induces an inside edge that clips the middle and off bail. Axar, for a moment, was preparing to run, only to find out that his stumps had been disturbed. DC sink further!

name: T Stubbs
commentary: edged and gone! Chakravarthy has another! Hung up on a length outside off. Seems to swerve away late and that draws the mistake. Stubbs wafts at it away from his body without any footwork and feathers it through to Salt, who snares a sharp chance!

name: K Kushagra
commentary: was there an inside edge?! The finger goes up, and Kushagra is happy to walk back! Plenty of revs on this one, and this is a googly that pitches just outside off. Spins back in and Kushagra does not seem to pick it. Jams it off the inside edge into the flap of his front pad. Lobs up and Salt tracks it all the way to complete the catch!

name: Rasikh Salam
shortText: c SS Iyer b Harshit Rana
commentary: soft dismissal! Excellently-disguised slower ball. Pitches on a full length on off and Rasikh has no clue this is the change-up. Too early into the stroke and reaches out way in front. Pops it straight to Shreyas at mid off, and Rana breaks a 29-run partnership!

name: PD Salt
commentary: knocks him over! Axar Patel strikes again! Darted in on a length just outside off. Keeps coming back in with the angle and skids on too. Salt, looking to cut it away, is beaten for pace and his off stump gets flattened soon after. A fantastic tone-setting innings comes to an end!

name: SP Narine
commentary: wicket first ball for Axar! Length ball angled across the batter just outside off. Narine tries to fetch it but does not get the power he is after. Hits it straight to the fielder at deep mid wicket, and Fraser-McGurk does the rest. The first ball of spin this innings yields the first wicket, but is it too late already?

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: Ishan Kishan
commentary: Bishnoi flummoxes Kishan! Slows up the googly on a length on middle and leg. Kishan, feeling the pinch of the scoring rate, has a huge mow across the line. Does so without getting to the pitch of the ball and it spins just enough to take the top edge. Swirls towards short third, where Mayank does the rest!

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: RG Sharma
commentary: four and out! Full just outside off. Does not shape back in and if anything, continues on with the arm. Rohit, trying to cover drive, slices it and hits it uppishly towards Stoinis at short extra cover. Stoinis, down on one knee, takes it in front of his face, and Mumbai have been jolted early!

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: SA Yadav
commentary: another appeal, and called a wide again! Reviewed again! Shortish, angling into the body. SKY tries to help it fine but gets cramped for room. There seems to be a noise when the ball went past him, and Rahul thinks SKY has gloved it. The ball is pretty close to the gloves, and Rahul is indeed right. There is a spike when the ball is next to the gloves, and SKY has to go!

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: NT Tilak Varma
commentary: appeal for an lbw initially! Zipped in full and the ball finishes on leg stump. Tilak plays all over his flick and gets rapped on the pads. The ball rolls away towards Bishnoi at backward point, who takes aim and hits the stumps at the batter's end. This could be tight. Tilak went walkabouts after the ball struck his pad. Looks like Bishnoi has gotten his man. Tilak is just short of his ground, and that is a superb bit of fielding. MI in big, big trouble!

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: HH Pandya
commentary: gone first ball! This is going from bad to worse for the Mumbai Indians! Length ball angled into the fifth stump channel. The ball pitches and straightens ever so slightly. Hardik goes feeling for it and only manages to nick it through to Rahul. Mumbai in all sorts of strife, and LSG are over the moon!

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: N Wadhera
commentary: cleans him up with a corker! Searing yorker, angling into the base of middle stump. Bowled at 140.6kph and Wadhera does not seem to pick it early. He is late to get his bat down and the ball thumps into middle and leg. A handy knock but the end-overs fireworks will have to come from elsewhere!

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: TH David

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: G Coetzee

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: KL Rahul
commentary: goes a mile up, and taken by Nabi! Hardik takes out his opposite number! Hard length just outside off. Rahul tries to heave it over the leg side but shanks it a little. Gets more elevation than distance. Nabi settles himself on the deep square leg fence, and takes a smart catch, while balancing and ensuring he did not step onto the cushion. Massive moment in the game!

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: AA Kulkarni
commentary: big appeal, not given! Speared in full and this swings away late. Kulkarni, having walked down the track, plays all over it. The MI players ask Thushara where this has pitched, and Hardik then signals for the review, rather theatrically. No bat involved. All eyes on Ball Tracking now. Pitching in line, impact in line and the ball would have been smashing into the middle of middle stump. First IPL wicket for Thushara; first-ball duck for Kulkarni!

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: MP Stoinis
shortText: c Tilak Varma b Mohammad Nabi
commentary: holes out to deep mid wicket! Another half-tracker but somehow, it fetches Nabi a wicket. Stoinis tries to mow it over cow corner but the bottom hand comes off as he slugs at it. Does not get the requisite power and Tilak takes the catch, right on the fence. Stoinis departs, but after playing what looks like a match-winning knock!

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: DJ Hooda
commentary: Hardik has another! Hardik keeps badgering that hard length outside off, and Hooda's patience has run out. He has a wild slog at it and does not have any shape to play that stroke. Goes straight up off the outer half of the bat and Bumrah makes no mistake at mid off!

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: N Pooran

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: A Badoni
commentary: oh, how has Kishan missed that?! Short and wide outside off. Badoni throws his hands at it and jabs it towards deep cover point. Scrambles back for two but he is always struggling. Kishan, after collecting, misses the stumps at the first go. Makes contact with the stumps on the second attempt and Badoni's bat, at that point, is up in the air as he dives, despite being past the crease. The question now is whether the bat was, at any point, grounded behind the crease. The third umpire does not think so, and Badoni has to walk back! LSG are not happy with the decision!

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: KH Pandya
AM Rahane
commentary: Brar gets the breakthrough! Tosses up a full ball on middle. Rahane gets low and tries to play a slog sweep, but he gets a big top-edge as he plays against the turn. Cow corner calls for it and then completes the rest. Breakthrough for PBKS!

name: S Dube
commentary: big lbw appeal, and given! Oh what a moment in this game. Dube reviews. This was a full ball on middle stump, slowed down by Brar, and Dube gets low for a big heave across the line. The ball goes under the bat and hits him on the back leg. Verdict: crashing into middle stump!

name: RA Jadeja
commentary: Chahar is interested, and the umpire gives it out! Jadeja turns to walk back but then Gaikwad runs across to have a chat. Sends it up for a review. This was a full ball on off stump that turned in sharply and hit Jadeja on the front leg. The bat is nowhere near the ball, so the TV umpire moves directly to ball-tracking. Has it turned too much? No, crashing into leg stump. CSK lose their second review and third wicket. Jadeja beaten by the degree of turn

name: Sameer Rizvi
commentary: Harshal slides and completes the catch! Rabada with a length ball around off stump and Rizvi tries to play an upper cut over third man but Rizvi doesn't add any pace to it with his bat, and he ends up offering catching practice to Harshal running in from deep third. A laboured innings comes to an end

name: MM Ali
commentary: leg stump pegged back! Chahar silences the crowd again. Full ball on middle and leg but without much air. Moeen clears the front leg and tries to swing a shot across the line, but he's nowhere near the pitch of the ball. Chahar gets it to turn in and the poles light up

name: MS Dhoni
commentary: Dhoni's streak of unbeaten innings end at 7 as he is run out trying to pinch a second run. Mistimes a shot just over short third man, and he gives it a chase to the boundary line before releasing a flat throw over the stumps at the keeper's end. Dhoni is short by a couple of inches and CSK finish on 162 for 7

name: Prabhsimran Singh
commentary: Gleeson has his first wicket of the season and this is well deserved! The length ball on middle does the job. A hint of nibble away from the batter and that was enough. Prabhsimran looks to turn it to the leg side but is caught in a tangle. He gets a leading edge and Gaikwad moving to his left from mid-off ain't dropping those.

name: JM Bairstow
commentary: Edged and Dube has a wicket second ball of the season. Was a nonchalant short of a length ball just on off. Was a cutter at 122.6kph. He opens the face of the bat, looking for a ramp. Instead gets a thick edge straight to Dhoni. Jonny B falls for a 30-ball 46

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: TM Head
commentary: no need for any review this time! Avesh sticks to nailing the wide yorkers and that prompts Head to do something different. Walks across and looks for the lap. Gets a good piece of it but deflects it straight into middle stump. Middle stump is flat on the ground, and Head has to walk back after a 44-ball 58!

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: Abhishek Sharma
commentary: Avesh gets Abhishek! Banged into the track on off. Abhishek tries to help it over the deep square leg fence but does not free his arms fully. It is not a full swing of the bat either, and all he does is pick out the sweeper to perfection. Avesh has a smile on his face, and this is just reward for a tidy start by RR!

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: Anmolpreet Singh
commentary: and Sandeep into the wickets! Length ball that pitches on middle and off, and nips back in a shade. Anmolpreet tries to flick it away but cannot keep it down. A tame chip towards Jaiswal at mid wicket, who dives forward and completes a smart catch!

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: H Klaasen

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: YBK Jaiswal
commentary: stumps everywhere! Natarajan with the breakthrough! Very similar to what happened with Travis Head. This is a yorker outside off. Jaiswal tries to lap it away and gets into position. Gets bat on it but only manages to smash it into his stumps. Middle and leg stump are left in a mess, and that might be the window of opportunity SRH were after!

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: JC Buttler
commentary: the slip is in play straightaway! Bhuvneshwar rips out Buttler again! Back of a length just outside off. A hint of movement away from the batter. Buttler wafts at it and gets a thick outside edge that flies towards Jansen. He gobbles up the chance, and that is just the start SRH would have wanted!

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: SV Samson
commentary: cleans him up with an absolute jaffa! Bhunveshwar Kumar at his absolute best! Fullish ball that starts just outside off and boomerangs back in. Samson does not get to the pitch and goes searching for it. Does so in front of his pad and leaves a massive gap. The ball zips through and flattens middle stump!

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: R Parag
commentary: straight to long on! Cummins takes out Parag, and is there a twist in this tale!? Full just outside off. Parag, sitting back in his crease, looks to go downtown. This one, though, is just full enough to prevent him from getting under it completely. Jansen makes no mistake on the fence, and both of RR's set batters have been dismissed in quick succession!

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: SO Hetmyer
commentary: Hetmyer falls! Low full toss slanted into leg stump. Hetmyer's eyes light up and he tries to smash the cover off the ball. Cloths it, though, and long on completes the catch well inside the fence. Are SRH still in this, somehow?

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: DC Jurel
commentary: the Sunrisers Hyderabad are alive! And the Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium in Hyderabad is alive too! Half-volley on the pads. Jurel steps across his stumps and tries to shovel it over deep square leg. The bottom hand comes off ever so slightly, though, and he arrows it to Abhishek, who tracks the ball and holds on to the chance!

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: R Ashwin
PD Salt
commentary: straight up and there's a collision and they still take the catch!. Once again the ball into the wicket holds up. Thushara is also able to get it to nip away off the pitch. Salt, seeing the length short, tries to access the midwicket boundary. But ends up with leading edge that soars up into the night sky. Tilak is running back from point. The sweeper, Dhir, I think, is running in from the boundary, neither of them are pulling out and in the end and Tilak somehow keeps his concentration and takes the catch

name: SP Narine
commentary: Narine swings again, this time he misses, and Hardik hits the leg stump. The length was there to be hit, but Narine swings so very hard that he loses his shape and doesn't touch it at all. KKR four down inside five overs

name: A Raghuvanshi
commentary: taken at cover. A shift in line works. And the pitch lends a hand as well. Thushara invites it to by bowling a slightly slower ball into the wicket. Raghuvanshi sees the length and immediately thinks its hittable but he doesn't account for the ball coming off the pitch so slowly and he's through with the shot too soon. Ends up popping a simple catch

name: SS Iyer
commentary: easy as that. Even full length deliveries are not behaving like the batter is expecting. Iyer sees the chance to flick one off his pads for four, but all he manages to do is end up spooning a catch to mid-on. Definite signs of a two-paced pitch

name: VR Iyer
commentary: Venkatesh is on the floor, and so is the middle stump. Another Bumrah special. Venkatesh, having shuffled across the stumps, was looking to flip it over the keeper but loses his balance. Bumrah has the stumps splayed once again. Un-hittable stuff from Bumrah

name: MK Pandey
commentary: Pandey holes out. 121.2ks cutter on a wide line with six men on the off side. Pandey loses his shape while reaching out for this wide delivery. He skies it and it's taken by the extra-cover fielder (Brevis, the sub) running to his right

name: AD Russell
commentary: A horrible mix-up between the wickets and Russell has to go. Russell almost ran two like Daz Mitchell had done at Chepauk. He's caught short at the non-striker's end. The single was on but Venkatesh was simply ball-watching and KKR have to pay the penalty with the wicket of Russell. Venkatesh jumps across the stumps and reverse-sweeps it just wide of short third. The throw from Thushara to Hardik is fairly accurate, and the MI captain does the rest

name: Ramandeep Singh
commentary: ramped straight into the lap of Coetzee at deep third. On-pace short ball outside off, Ramandeep leans back and hits it in the air. Coetzee, for a brief moment lost the ball in the lights, but eventually slides on his knees to take it with both hands

name: Ishan Kishan
commentary: gone, the leg stump is flattened! And Starc hardly even reacts, even as this was quicker at 142kph. Starc went fuller than the previous ball, angling it into leg again. Kishan swung his bat to go through the line, but this time was beaten. I think the ball skidded through rather quickly too, with Kishan getting completely beaten

name: RG Sharma
commentary: taken by midwicket moving back! He tried the sweep last ball but mistimed it, and this time fails to time and place the pull. Narine shortened his length a good deal to go short around off, and Rohit pulled with his front leg out of the way. But the ball ballooned up to midwicket's right, with the fielder moving back and across to gobble that

name: Naman Dhir
commentary: cleaned up first ball! Dhir was again looking to be adventurous, and this time was bowled in looking to swing across the line. He even seemed off balance, as he played from middle stump to a length ball which was bowled from back of the hand on middle and off. It turned in and hit middle, as Dhir played early and missed

name: SA Yadav
commentary: taken by Salt after ending up halfway back to fine leg! In fact, Salt and the fine leg fielder nearly collided, with the latter moving away last moment. The ball was dropping over Salt's right shoulder, and he did well to keep his eyes on it. Russell bowled a full toss at a comfortable, lowish height on the stumps, as Surya swung and sent it miles up. I think that allowed Salt time to move back and nab it

name: NT Tilak Varma
commentary: spooned to short third! This went off the top edge, as Varma went driving with a horizontal bat at this fullish ball which is wide of off. Varun got it to dip, and slowed it down, with Varma playing early and letting it pop up for a simple catch for Narine

name: N Wadhera
commentary: chopped on! Salt is delighted with that result, even as Narine is barely moved. Narine went on the shorter side of a length around fifth stump, as this one went with the angle from over the wicket. The ball didn't move off the pitch, though, as Wadhera went slapping but ended up smashing his off stump

name: HH Pandya
commentary: chipped for a catch for midwicket! Pandya falls early again, as MI go deeper into the hole. The slowness of the pitch came into the picture there, as he closed the face of the bat to try and clip, but seemed to have done so quite early. The bat was more horizontal, though, as the ball popped up off the top edge to midwicket's left, as Russell had bowled on a short of a length on leg

name: TH David
commentary: taken at long-off! KKR hit back each time MI threaten to make a match out of it. David and the MI camp are stung, as the former pumps a full toss right into Shreyas' hands. KKR know it is a bonus wicket, but will take it, as it gets rid of a big-hitter at this crucial stage

name: G Coetzee
commentary: the middle stump is flattened! Starc erupts in joy, as he cleans the tail up. This is not a full toss, though, and is all but the perfect delivery for a tailender. Coetzee's front leg is out of the way, as he attempts to whack this yorker on middle but is completely beaten. KKR beat MI at Wankhede after 12 years

name: Shubman Gill
commentary: Wicket no.2 for Siraj! What a start this has been from Siraj. Bowls a length ball around off stump, seams away, Gill looks to play a lofted shot but manages only the toe end of the bat. Vyshak takes the catch comfortably at deep point to leave GT 10 for 2

name: B Sai Sudharsan
commentary: GT in big trouble! Another batter, the in-form batter, walks away inside the powerplay. Short of a length delivery on off, Sudharsan looks to power it through leg side but there is no timing on it. Ends up top-edging to Kohli at mid-off. Big, big wicket for RCB

name: M Shahrukh Khan
commentary: RCB are celebrating the run-out! Pace off, full length on middle and off, he taps this towards point. Shahrukh wants the run but is sent back. Kohli nails a direct hit at the non-striker's end. And he is gone! Brilliant from Kohli

name: DA Miller
commentary: Karn strikes back! Miller's gone. The legspinner wins the battle. Bowls full on middle and leg, Miller slices it across the line, doesn't connect well this time. Maxwell at long-on collects the ball cleanly. Miller is clearly disappointed

name: R Tewatia
commentary: Brilliant catch from Vyshak! Short delivery outside off stump, he looks to pull this across the line but gets a top edge to deep third. Vyshak runs in to his right and dives at the perfect time to complete the catch, wicket no.2 for Dayal

name: Rashid Khan
commentary: Cleaned up! Excellent from Dayal. He mixed up the pace very well. Bowls a yorker on leg stump, Rashid shuffles across and tries to play this towards leg side, exposing his middle and leg stumps. He misses and Dayal knocks off the leg stump

name: V Shankar
commentary: 147 all out! Three deliveries and three wickets. Another short pitched ball outside off stump, he comes down the track to go big but gets a top edge to short third where Siraj takes the catch with a bit of fumble

name: MJ Suthar
commentary: Taken! Short ball outside off, pace off, clears the front leg but doesn't have enough power to clear the fielder at deep point

name: MM Sharma
commentary: Terrible mix-up! Unfortunate for Mohit. Full length ball outside off, he goes for a push and misses. Vijay Shankar had already left to take the strike. Karthik throws the ball immediately to Vyshak, who takes the bails off a the non-striker's end

name: V Kohli
commentary: gottim, Kohli nicks off! Gets the wrong'un in the right area this time, full and inviting the drive and RCB's bellwether batter only succeeds in feathering a catch behind. Silence around the Chinnaswamy as it dawns on the crowd their team could actually lose this...!

name: F du Plessis
commentary: dug in, spliced to short fine leg! Little hits back, but has the damage already been done? Du Plessis was in no mood to duck a challenge but seemed hurried by the bouncer and ended up presenting a simple catch to the man on the edge of the ring

name: RM Patidar
commentary: smoked... but straight to deep square leg, another one goes! Patidar takes on the shot and hits it pretty well. But the man in the deep barely has to move as the ball homes in on him a few yards in from the rope

venue: Himachal Pradesh Cricket Association Stadium, Dharamsala
name: AM Rahane
commentary: did that carry?! Very full and swinging into the pads. Rahane falls across and tries to dig it out. Flicks it towards mid wicket, who dives forward and snaffles it up. Rahane is not going anywhere, though. He thinks it is a bump ball, and there is some uncertainty over whether the catch was clean too. The ball clearly carried and Rabada did brilliantly to get his hands under it. Rahane has to go, and CSK have been rocked early!

venue: Himachal Pradesh Cricket Association Stadium, Dharamsala
name: S Dube
commentary: two in two for Chahar! Back to back golden ducks for Shivam Dube, and CSK are now in disarray! Googly on a back of a length just outside off. Dube, while being caught on the crease, wafts at it and gets an outside edge that Jitesh pouches sharply. Chahar is off on his celebratory run, and these are two body blows in succession for CSK!

venue: Himachal Pradesh Cricket Association Stadium, Dharamsala
name: MM Ali
commentary: goes straight up, and Moeen perishes! Slower bumper outside off. Moeen tries to swipe it into next week but does not come to grips with the variation. The ball gets big on him and it skews off the top edge. Spoons up to Bairstow at short fine leg, who makes no mistake. CSK slide further!

venue: Himachal Pradesh Cricket Association Stadium, Dharamsala
name: RA Jadeja
commentary: miscued, and taken by Curran running in! Banged into the track outside off. Jadeja has to fetch it and does not manage to do so. Slices it straight up, and Curran tracks it all the way from long on. Completes the catch just a little outside the ring, and CSK lose their ninth!

venue: Himachal Pradesh Cricket Association Stadium, Dharamsala
name: MJ Santner
commentary: holes out to long on! Dangles the carrot outside off and Santner bites. Tries to smash this over long on but has to reach out, meaning that he clinks it off the toe-end. Does not get the requisite power to clear Bairstow, and he takes a simple catch on the fence!

venue: Himachal Pradesh Cricket Association Stadium, Dharamsala
name: SN Thakur
commentary: knocks him over! Another slower yorker, and this is on the stumps. Finishes on middle and off, and Shardul, trying to muscle it down the ground, is early into the stroke. Breezes past the bat and smashes into the stumps. And the crowd now finds its voice because MS Dhoni is walking out to the middle!

venue: Himachal Pradesh Cricket Association Stadium, Dharamsala
name: MS Dhoni
commentary: and Harshal cleans up Dhoni first ball! A superb slower yorker that drops on Dhoni late and does him all ends up. He does not pick the variation and like Shardul, is early into the stroke. The ball sneaks under the toe-end, and then thunders into the stumps. The CSK fans at the venue cannot quite believe what has happened, and their day goes from bad to worse!

venue: Himachal Pradesh Cricket Association Stadium, Dharamsala
name: TU Deshpande

venue: Himachal Pradesh Cricket Association Stadium, Dharamsala
name: RJ Gleeson

venue: Himachal Pradesh Cricket Association Stadium, Dharamsala
name: Prabhsimran Singh
commentary: Prabhsimran perishes too! Jadeja slows this up, and bowls it full enough on the stumps to entice the lofted stroke. Prabhsimran needs no second invitation but the lack of pace means he cannot get the distance he is after. Arrows it straight to Rizvi at long off, and PBKS are four down!

venue: Himachal Pradesh Cricket Association Stadium, Dharamsala
name: JM Bairstow
commentary: cleans him up, and Deshpande is absolutely pumped! Back of a length delivery that pitches just outside off and decks back sharply. Bairstow does not account for that much movement and pokes at it tentatively. Goes past the bat, richochets off the pads and crashes into off stump. Just the start CSK would have wanted!

venue: Himachal Pradesh Cricket Association Stadium, Dharamsala
name: Shashank Singh
commentary: Santner gets Shashank! Throws up the bait outside off and Shashank takes it. Tries to make it back-to-back boundaries but this is slower than the previous ball. Shashank has to reach out and cloths it in the process. Chips it to Simarjeet at long on, who does the rest!

venue: Himachal Pradesh Cricket Association Stadium, Dharamsala
name: SM Curran
commentary: taken this time! PBKS continue losing their way! Short and wide outside off. Curran, feeling the pinch of the run-rate and wanting to take on the left-arm spinner, swings at it. Laces it but does not get the requisite elevation. Straightforward catch to Santner at long off, and he was never going to drop that!

venue: Himachal Pradesh Cricket Association Stadium, Dharamsala
name: JM Sharma
commentary: First ball duck for Jitesh! Simarjeet into the act straightaway!Short and wide outside off. There to be hit and Jitesh goes after it. Throws his hands at it but cannot quite get on top of the bounce. Flies off the outside edge and Dhoni gobbles it up! Simarjeet is delighted, and PBKS in a bit of strife now!

venue: Himachal Pradesh Cricket Association Stadium, Dharamsala
name: Harpreet Brar

venue: Himachal Pradesh Cricket Association Stadium, Dharamsala
name: HV Patel
shortText: c sub (Sameer Rizvi) b Simarjeet Singh
commentary: Harshal falls to Simarjeet! Simarjeet sticks to the hard length, and Harshal continues to swing. The ball, though, gets big on Harshal and he cannot control the pull. Goes straight up and Rizvi does well to track it from mid wicket. Completes the catch in the square leg region, and that is the eighth nail hammered into the PBKS coffin!

venue: Himachal Pradesh Cricket Association Stadium, Dharamsala
name: RD Chahar
commentary: timbbeerrrrr! Speared in full on off stump. Chahar walks a mile across his stumps and gets too ambitious. Looks for the paddle but he is so far across that he cannot access the ball altogether. Fresh-air shot, and he hears the death rattle soon after!

venue: Himachal Pradesh Cricket Association Stadium, Dharamsala
name: K Rabada

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: PD Salt
commentary: strong response from Naveen! The bowler goes slow, entices Salt with a full and wide ball, and the change of pace does the trick. Salt throws his bat at it, looking to pump another boundary through the off side, but the has flummoxed him. Takes an edge, flies to Rahul's right, and the keeper dives to seal the deal.

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: SP Narine
shortText: c sub (D Padikkal) b Ravi Bishnoi
commentary: holes out for 81 and finally Bishnoi can smile. He went full outside off, and Narine ended up slicing this shot down the V. Padikkal, from long off, takes a few steps in, and this time takes a clean catch. Bishnoi pats Narine on the back as the allrounder trods out of the ground. Another stunning innings from the maestro

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: A Raghuvanshi
commentary: dream start for Yudhvir! A wicket first ball and he folds his hands in celebration. Back of a length ball outside off. Raghuvanshi tries to play it towards deep third by attempting to poke it, but all it does is take an edge to the wicketkeeper

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: AD Russell
commentary: stunning catch by K Gowtham! Naveen does not change his plans despite the adversity. Goes full and wide again, and Russell toe-ends the attempted launch over cover. Ball goes high up and three fielders look to converge but it is K Gowtham, who turns and runs back from cover, who dives away from the action, facing the crowd, and takes a brilliant two-hander. Russell gone for a strike-rate of 150

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: SS Iyer
commentary: KL with a stunning catch! Slower ball bouncer on middle and leg. Iyer attempts the pull behind square but ends up gloving it. Rahul dives to his left, at full stretch with his left-hand sticking out, and the ball stays glued! Thakur folds his hands in admiration of Rahul. Rubbish line but great result.

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: Ramandeep Singh

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: VR Iyer

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: KL Rahul
shortText: c Ramandeep Singh b Harshit Rana
commentary: Skipper gone! It's Ramandeep again. Full length delivery outside off stump, Rahul hits this hard but finds Ramandeep at deep point. Easy catch for him this time. Big wicket for Rana and KKR

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: AA Kulkarni
commentary: Whaaaat a catch! Superb effort from Ramandeep. Full length delivery on middle and off, he looks to flick it towards leg side, gets a leading edge to covers, Ramandeep sprints backward and dives in front of Russell to take the stunning catch. He had to cover a lot of distance for this

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: MP Stoinis
commentary: Russell takes out Stoinis! Full length ball pitched slightly outside off, nips back in a bit, he gets a leading edge to Rana at point. This is the fifth time Russell has dismissed Stoinis in T20s, says Shastri

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: DJ Hooda
commentary: Varun strikes in his first over! Hooda reviews. Length ball turns in to the right-hander from outside off stump and hits his front pad. UltraEdge confirms there is no bat involved. Will be umpire's call with impact in front of middle. On-field decision is out, so Hooda has to depart.

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: N Pooran
commentary: Russell strikes again! First Stoinis and then Pooran. Huge, huge wickets. Short of a length delivery outside off stump, Pooran looks to hit this across the line, gets an edge to keeper. Salt jumps high and collects the ball head-high

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: A Badoni
commentary: Holes out to Starc! fuller outside off stump, he comes down the track to slice it over long-off, Starc runs in a bit and slides to complete the catch. Well-judged! KKR inching towards win

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: KH Pandya
commentary: Gone this time! Another top edge and another wicket. Excellent from Salt. Short delivery outside off, Pandya tries to lean back in and play an upper-cut but gets a thick edge to keeper

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: Yudhvir Singh
commentary: Varun gets his third! Full delivery outside off, he looks to slash this, gets a top edge to Russell at cover-point. KKR one wicket away from a massive win

venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
name: Ravi Bishnoi
commentary: Finger goes up! It's all over for LSG. Full length delivery on off stump, pace off, Bishnoi looks to push this forward and misses. Gets hit on the back pad. LSG review it. Flat line on UltraEdge. pitching outside off, impact in line and umpire's call. LSG bowl out for 137

name: TM Head
commentary: straight to Tilak at deep-backward square leg. Full on the stumps and Head goes for the slog sweep, only to hit it straight into the lap of the fielder

name: Abhishek Sharma
commentary: Bumrah strikes! A length ball around off, just holding its line. Abhishek pushes at it with little footwork, got an outside edge and Kishan dived to his left to take it with both hands - there was no slip in place

name: MA Agarwal
commentary: Finally! Finally, Anhsul Kamboj has an IPL wicket. Was a length ball that came in a touch. Agarwal had cleared his front leg and look to smash it on the leg side. He missed and the ball trimmed the bails

name: H Klaasen
commentary: bowled 'im! A quicker one - 91.9kph - and full on the stumps. Klaasen goes for the big shot but gets an inside edge onto the stumps. This is a big, big moment in the game

name: M Jansen
commentary: Hardik has his third. A length ball that came back in sharply to beat Jansen's wild swing and rattle the stumps. SRH in deep, deep trouble here

name: Shahbaz Ahmed
commentary: And he has his second wicket. Full on the pads and Shahbaz goes aerial, towards long-on. But there's no timing or power behind it. SKY charges in and completes the catch with a slide

name: Abdul Samad
commentary: Given lbw. A length ball on the stumps. Samad was looking to work it on the leg side but ends up playing all over it and gets hit on the pad. The ball-tracking seems to be taking some time. And when it eventually loads, it shows the ball would have hit the leg stump

name: Ishan Kishan
commentary: Jansen turns into both a can opener and an artist to have Kishan caught at slip! 137ks on a fullish length, shaped to come in before deceiving oh so sweetly. Seam-movement deluxe. Kishan tried to clip it square but got it off the leading edge. Mayank takes it in front of his chest

name: RG Sharma
commentary: Leading edge, right up, Klaasen clears coast and takes it well! Cummins has Rohit again, fourth time informs Ian Bishop on air. Bashed on a length into the pitch, scramble-seam ball landed around middle and off and straightened just a bit. Rohit's idea of staying in the crease and helping it square with a closed bat face seemed reasonable, but the movement off the pitch did him in. Klaasen didn't have to travel much but had to wait a while before it came down. Was right below and took it with gloves pointing up

name: Naman Dhir
commentary: Naman tries to hit out of trouble and ends up flashing it to first slip. Shortish, around fifth stump, he charged down and tried to slash it over point. He couldn't account for the bounce and got it off the edge. Jansen took it around head-height, would've been four if a normie was standing there

name: Abishek Porel
commentary: ballooned to point! Ashwin has three, as a dangerous Porel departs. Ashwin went slower, and teased the batter by slightly tossing it up and dipping on a length on middle and off. Porel backed away and went lofting, but the ball popped up off the outside half of the bat

name: SD Hope
commentary: ahh, Hope is run-out at the non-striker's end! And he walks back immediately, even before the third umpire confirms. This dismissal always hurts, as he was backing out too far outside the crease, and the ball trickled off Sandeep's hands and went on to hit the stumps. Sandeep bowled on the shorter side of a length, while angling it into Porel, who swat-pulled back, only for the bowler to get a hand to his left

name: AR Patel
commentary: taken at long-off! Ashwin went wide of the crease, and bowled on the fuller side of a length with a horizontal seam around off. Axar went lofting through the line, and ended up giving a catch taken by Parag in front of his stomach

name: RR Pant
commentary: whip-swept straight to fine leg! Pant falls and loses his balance, as he goes swiping late at this full and flat ball on middle. I think the fact that he played it quite late meant he couldn't clear the boundary, even as it wasn't a very big one. DC slightly losing their way ahead of the death overs

name: T Stubbs
commentary: ahh, appeal for lbw, and given! This was bowled at a near yorker-length on leg, and seemed gone the moment Stubbs missed. But he has still reviewed, because why not? The ball dips on to a be a near yorker on leg, as Stubbs looked to whip this with his front leg out of the way. There was no bat involved, as the ball struck his back pad. And replays show the ball would have, surprisingly, hit middle stump, and not leg!

name: Rasikh Salam
commentary: short ball, and wide of off. Salam pulls after riding the bounce, and plays on the bounce to deep midwicket. They attempt two with nothing to lose, and Salam is run-out at the striker's end. He was well short of the crease when the throw landed into Samson's gloves

name: YBK Jaiswal
commentary: Khaleel goes wide and removes Jaiswal. Back of length, 133ks, around sixth stump, Jaiswal took a step back and stayed in line with the stumps. He tried to flat-bat it downtown rather than slap it square, didn't have the reach to middle it and got it off the bottom. Ball went right up and Axar moved back, to his diagonal left, from mid-off to take it

name: JC Buttler
commentary: Underedged onto the stumps! Brave as Deep Dasgupta calls it on air. Not that dissimilar a ball from the last one that was pumped for six. Shorter but still on the fuller side of length if not full. 93ks, landed around fourth and spun a bit. Buttler tried slugging it across the line and toed it back onto leg stump, walks back disappointed as Axar looks up and pumps the air.

name: SV Samson
commentary: Hope's claiming a hoppity ballet catch at the long-on boundary! He moved to the left, took the catch around chest height. The first two-three steps were with both feet stuttering and sliding forward. Hope then got the smallest of strides while staying away from the cushion. The boots were almost parallel to the cushion. Did it move though, especially at the second step as he tip-toed towards the cushion? Commentators unsure, Samson - who was halfway back - was returning but it's given out. He's now chatting with the umpire. He doesn't want to go but is trudging back. Was a slower one on a length outside off, 118ks, Samson pulled it flat. Oh boy, looked like Samson's being suggested to review the umpire's decision but it's too late. Guess that'd have been too much drama to handle

name: R Parag
commentary: Slower one trims the top of off! Back of hand length ball lands just outside off and spins back in, Parag's eyes lit up and he wanted to maul it. But he was through the pull early and the ball tickled the stumps. Hard enough to send the bails flying. Rasikh twirls his finger above the head in celebration

name: SB Dubey
commentary: Back to pace-on and it's pulled straight to long-on! 138ks shortish ball around off, Dubey didn't have much room but committed fully towards pulling/flat-batting that over long-on. Stubbs takes it and leaps high in delight. Got it off the top edge. This chase keeps catting and mousing along

name: D Ferreira
commentary: Pinned on the pad. Ferrerira couldn't have been deeper in the crease and survives the lbw appeal on field. But DC send it upstairs. Pant was unsure but looked like he had no other option but to go up when he did. Kuldeep almost did that thing where he physically got Pant into the T... and he's vindicated! Was a length ball that wasn't far from full, it landed in line with leg (that was the big factor) and straightened to beat the flick. Hit flush on the pad and Hawk-Eye predicts it to crash leg peg. Kuldeep's like a lion personified with raucous roars and fist-pumps

name: R Ashwin
commentary: Ashwin, as Ravi Shastri predicted on air, tries to take Kuldeep on. But he holes out to long-on. The roar from Kuldeep is almost as big as it was after the last wicket. Ashwin charged, didn't get to the pitch of the real-slow ball, heaved across the line and got it off the edge. Easy catch

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: KL Rahul
commentary: On-pace ball does the trick for Cummins and Rahul goes! Good length ball on leg stump, Rahul helps it fine bit does not enough bat on it. Natarajan sprints to his right from deep backward square and takes a comfortable catch.

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: Q de Kock
shortText: c Nitish Kumar Reddy b Kumar
commentary: That is a super catch from Nitish Kumar Reddy! It looked for a second that the ball had evaded him, but he's held his balance brilliantly. Just the kind of awareness he's showed is superb. Bhuvneshwar goes back of a length on middle and leg as de Kock swivels and pulls to deep backward square leg. He gets a good connection, but Nitish leaps, catches the ball and upon realising he's going over the ropes, lets go of the ball comes back into the field of play and completes the catch

venue: Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Uppal, Hyderabad
name: MP Stoinis
